faculty.name: Daniel Weissman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in theoretical physics and evolution, building models to predict biological population evolution, including inferring dynamics from genomic data and understanding complex adaptations requiring multiple mutations, with applications like predicting antibiotic resistance.

faculty.name: Wladimir Benalcazar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works in condensed matter theory on how topology, symmetries, geometry, and interactions give rise to topological phases in insulators, superconductors, and metamaterials including photonic and acoustic crystals.

funding.note: Assistantships/fellowships available; see department graduate fellowships page

faculty.name: Paola Barbara
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies superconductivity, superconducting devices, and the transport properties of nanowires and nanoscale electronic devices. Serves as Department Chair.

faculty.name: Daniel Blair
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates soft glasses, colloidal systems, and polymer dynamics using advanced microscopy and rheology techniques.

faculty.name: Rhonda Dzakpasu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Examines pattern formation and dynamics in neural networks using multi-electrode recordings and computational methods.

faculty.name: Emanuela Del Gado
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses statistical mechanics and computational physics to study the structure and dynamics of amorphous solids, gels, glasses, and cement.

faculty.name: David Egolf
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies nonequilibrium dynamical systems and spatiotemporal chaos spanning fluids, granular media, and neural tissue.

faculty.name: Jim Freericks
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates strongly correlated electrons, nonequilibrium many-body physics, and ultracold atoms in optical lattices.

faculty.name: Maryam Kohram
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Holds a joint appointment in Physics and Biology; studies self-replication, senescence, and phenotypic variability in single bacterial cells using microfluidic devices.

faculty.name: Amy Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses first-principles computational methods to study the structural, electronic, and vibrational properties of novel materials including superconductors.

faculty.name: Kai Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on nanostructured magnetic materials, magnetic skyrmions, and magneto-ionic control of interfaces. Holds the McDevitt Chair.

faculty.name: Peter Olmsted
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on soft matter physics and biophysics, including non-equilibrium phase transitions and the dynamics and mechanics of polymers, proteins, and lipid bilayer membranes.

faculty.name: Makarand Paranjape
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops micro- and nanotechnologies, including MEMS devices and sensors, for biomedical and electronic applications.

faculty.name: Jeff Urbach
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches cellular biophysics, physics of soft matter and biomaterials, biomedical optics, and granular dynamics.

faculty.name: Edward Van Keuren
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on optics, nanoparticle synthesis and characterization, and applications of nanoparticles in photovoltaics and biomedical imaging.

faculty.name: Gen Yin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on computational quantum geometry and topology, charge and spin transport theory, and the modeling of topological materials.

requirements.admission_model: School graduate committee recommends admission to Office of Graduate Education; recurring Dec 15 deadline (year not posted)

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Feryal Ozel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chair and Professor working in theoretical and observational high-energy astrophysics, including neutron star structure, black holes, and black hole imaging.

faculty.name: Dimitrios Psaltis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies black hole imaging and tests of general relativity in the strong-field regime around compact objects.

faculty.name: David Ballantyne
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on high-energy astrophysics, accretion disks, and the co-evolution of galaxies and black holes.

faculty.name: Tamara Bogdanovic
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches black hole astrophysics, accretion physics, the intracluster medium, and computational astrophysics.

faculty.name: Laura Cadonati
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on gravitational-wave astrophysics and data analysis within the LIGO Scientific Collaboration.

faculty.name: Surabhi Sachdev
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant professor in the astrophysics, astroparticles and gravitation group working on gravitational-wave detection and analysis.

faculty.name: John Wise
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Computational astrophysicist studying early-universe structure, galaxy formation, and the formation of the first stars and black holes.

faculty.name: Ignacio Taboada
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies neutrino astrophysics with the IceCube Neutrino Observatory.

faculty.name: A. Nepomuk Otte
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on astroparticle physics, detector instrumentation, and photon detectors.

faculty.name: Aishik Ghosh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational and AI/ML methods for challenges in fundamental physics and astrophysics, including automating theoretical calculations.

faculty.name: Michael Chapman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental AMO physicist manipulating the quantum behavior of single atoms and photons for quantum science.

faculty.name: Brian Kennedy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist working on quantum optics and atomic physics.

faculty.name: Chandra Raman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental AMO physicist studying Bose-Einstein condensation and quantum atomic sensors.

faculty.name: Colin V. Parker
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses ultracold atoms and atomic defects in solids for quantum simulation of strongly correlated systems.

faculty.name: Rick Trebino
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops methods and devices for measuring and manipulating complex ultrafast laser pulses.

faculty.name: Walter de Heer
faculty.title: Regents' Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist known for nano-patterned epitaxial graphene and nanocluster beam studies.

faculty.name: Zhigang Jiang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies quantum transport and infrared optical properties of graphene, topological insulators, and semimetals.

faculty.name: Phillip First
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pursues atomic-scale understanding of solid-state systems, including epitaxial graphene, via scanning probe methods.

faculty.name: Martin Mourigal
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates collective electronic and magnetic phenomena in quantum materials, including quantum magnetism.

faculty.name: Chunhui Rita Du
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on quantum materials characterization and hybrid device engineering, including spin dynamics and quantum sensing.

faculty.name: Itamar Kimchi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theory of quantum matter and entanglement in quantum many-body systems.

faculty.name: Carlos Sa de Melo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist studying superconductors, quantum magnets, superfluids, and Bose-Einstein condensates in condensed matter and ultracold atomic systems.

faculty.name: Gongjie Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the dynamics of exoplanets and interactions between supermassive black holes.

faculty.name: Jennifer Curtis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Living Systems / Soft Matter
faculty.summary: Studies cell biophysics, including the mechanics of cell adhesion and migration, and immunophysics/immunoengineering.

faculty.name: Daniel Goldman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Living Systems / Soft Matter
faculty.summary: Studies the biomechanics of locomotion of organisms and robots on and within complex materials, and the physics of granular media.

faculty.name: Simon Sponberg
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates neuromechanics, locomotor control, the multiscale physics of muscle, and maneuverability.

faculty.name: JC Gumbart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Computational biophysicist using molecular simulation to study biomolecular systems such as membranes and proteins.

faculty.name: Elisabetta Matsumoto
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on soft condensed matter physics, the geometry of materials, and nonlinear elasticity.

faculty.name: D. Zeb Rocklin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geometric-structural mechanics, including topological and origami mechanics and their applications.

faculty.name: Roman Grigoriev
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on dynamical systems, fluid dynamics, excitable systems, machine learning, and control.

faculty.name: Michael Schatz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental nonlinear physicist studying fluid physics, including low-gravity fluid phenomena and pattern formation.

faculty.name: Shiladitya Banerjee
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical biophysicist developing quantitative and data-driven models of living systems, focusing on cell and tissue mechanics, collective cell dynamics, cytoskeletal organization, and bacterial physiology and adaptation.

faculty.name: Dragomir Davidovic
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist studying properties that emerge at nanometer scales in metallic and insulating particles, molecules, and atomic-scale wires through electronic transport measurements.

faculty.name: Flavio Fenton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Nonlinear Physics / Physics of Living Systems
faculty.summary: Studies excitable media and complex systems using a combined approach of theory, experiments, and computer simulations, with a focus on cardiac dynamics, spiral waves, and pattern formation in biological and chemical systems.

faculty.name: Harold Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental biophysicist using single-molecule and single-cell techniques to study the physical organization and architecture of the genome.

faculty.name: Itamar Kolvin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental soft condensed matter physicist studying out-of-equilibrium systems including active matter, hydrodynamics, elasticity and fracture, using biological molecular systems as platforms.

faculty.name: Uzi Landman
faculty.title: Regents' Professor; Institute Professor; F.E. Callaway Chair in Computational Materials Science
faculty.summary: Computational physicist using classical and quantum molecular dynamics simulations to study the structure, dynamics, and properties of materials governed by microscopic interactions, including surfaces, growth processes, and nanoclusters.

faculty.name: Zhu-Xi Luo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on condensed matter theory and its intersections with quantum information and high energy theory.

faculty.name: Michael Pustilnik
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying transport and correlations in low-dimensional quantum systems, including quantum criticality, viscous dissipation in quantum liquids, and excitations in one-dimensional systems.

faculty.name: Audrey Sederberg
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Living Systems / Computational Neuroscience
faculty.summary: Computational and theoretical neuroscientist studying neural activity dynamics across brain areas and species, using computational methods to understand collective patterns in neuronal populations.

faculty.name: Sven Simon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical space plasma physicist studying the magnetospheres of the outer planets, large-scale magnetospheric dynamics, and the interaction of planetary moons and small bodies with their plasma environments.

faculty.name: Hailong Wang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist working on the synthesis of functional quantum materials and on spin transport and dynamics in quantum materials and devices, with applications to spin-qubit magnetometry and hybrid quantum devices.

faculty.name: Xueda Wen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist studying non-equilibrium physics in quantum many-body systems, topological phases of matter, and connections between quantum many-body physics and quantum information.

faculty.name: Peter Yunker
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Soft Matter / Physics of Living Systems
faculty.summary: Experimental soft matter and biophysics researcher studying nonequilibrium systems including densely packed active matter with life-and-death events, microbial physics, structural and fracture mechanics, and evolution.

faculty.name: Andrew Zangwill
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist (author of Modern Electrodynamics and Physics at Surfaces) whose current work focuses on the history of 20th century physics.

funding.note: Fully funded (SEAS PhD guarantee: tuition + stipend)

faculty.name: Federico Capasso
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer of flat optics and metasurfaces and co-inventor of the quantum cascade laser.

faculty.name: David A. Weitz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental soft-matter physicist working on colloids, emulsions, and microfluidic technologies.

faculty.name: Eric Mazur
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies ultrafast optics, laser-matter interactions, and nanophotonic materials.

faculty.name: Conor J. Walsh
faculty.title: Paul A. Maeder Professor of Engineering and Applied Sciences
faculty.summary: Global leader in soft wearable robotics for human augmentation and rehabilitation. His soft exosuits use textiles and soft actuation to assist mobility-impaired individuals. Work has direct clinical impact for stroke and ALS patients.

faculty.name: Lene Vestergaard Hau
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Famous for experimentally stopping light in a Bose-Einstein condensate — a landmark demonstration of quantum optical control. Research has profound implications for quantum information storage and quantum networks. MacArthur Fellow.

faculty.name: Philip Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies electronic transport in graphene, two-dimensional materials, and van der Waals heterostructures.

faculty.name: Evelyn Hu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on nanoscale photonic and quantum devices and the fabrication of engineered semiconductor materials.

faculty.name: Vinothan N. Manoharan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies self-assembly of colloidal particles and soft-matter physics using optical methods.

faculty.name: Zhigang Suo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the mechanics of materials, including soft materials, tough hydrogels, and electromechanical systems.

faculty.name: Donhee Ham
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on electronic devices and circuits, including brain-inspired chips and nanoelectronic bio-interfaces.

faculty.name: Frans A. Spaepen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure and properties of materials, including metallic glasses and colloidal crystals; serves as a director of graduate studies for Applied Physics.

faculty.name: Joanna Aizenberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on biologically inspired materials, self-assembly, wettability, and adaptive surfaces.

faculty.name: Jennifer Lewis
faculty.title: Hansjorg Wyss Professor of Biologically Inspired Engineering
faculty.summary: World leader in 3D printing of functional, structural, and biological materials. Pioneered multimaterial 3D printing for soft robotics, printed electronics, vascularized tissues, and structural materials. Her innovations are commercialized through multiple startups. Member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Kit Parker
faculty.title: Tarr Family Professor of Bioengineering and Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Army veteran and Harvard professor who builds human heart tissue on chips to study cardiac disease and drug responses. His group bridges fundamental biophysics with tissue engineering, creating models of neuromuscular and cardiac diseases. Multiple startup companies spun out.

faculty.name: Efthimios Kaxiras
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for electronic structure and quantum materials, including twisted graphene.

faculty.name: Julia A. Mundy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Synthesizes atomically precise complex-oxide thin films to engineer new quantum materials.

faculty.name: David J. Mooney
faculty.title: Robert P. Pinkas Family Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Leader in biomaterials for immunoengineering and tissue regeneration. His group developed implantable biomaterial scaffolds that program immune cells in situ for cancer immunotherapy — a transformational approach now in clinical trials. Member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Katia Bertoldi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies mechanical metamaterials and how instabilities and geometry produce novel material behaviors.

faculty.name: Boris Kozinsky
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops machine-learning and first-principles methods for materials discovery, including energy and battery materials.

faculty.name: Michael J. Aziz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on materials for energy storage and conversion, including organic flow batteries.

faculty.name: Daniel Needleman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the biophysics of cell division, the cytoskeleton, and self-organization in living matter.

faculty.name: Joost J. Vlassak
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies mechanical behavior of thin films and materials, and develops microfabricated characterization tools.

faculty.name: Philippe Cluzel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Quantitative biologist studying gene regulation and behavior in single cells and microbial systems.

faculty.name: Zachary Schiffer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on electrochemical reaction engineering for decarbonization and sustainable chemical manufacturing.

faculty.name: Maxim Prigozhin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Chemistry and Chemical Biology developing optical and single-molecule imaging methods to study biomolecular dynamics.

faculty.name: David Mooney
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops biomaterials and hydrogels for tissue engineering, regenerative medicine, and cancer immunotherapy.

faculty.name: Samir Mitragotri
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on drug delivery technologies, including transdermal and targeted delivery systems.

faculty.name: Kevin Kit Parker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies cardiac tissue biophysics and builds engineered tissues and organs-on-chips.

faculty.name: Jia Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops soft, tissue-integrated bioelectronics and cyborg-engineering tools for neuroscience, cardiac disease, and developmental biology.

faculty.name: Sharad Ramanathan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies decision-making in cells and developmental systems using theory and quantitative experiments.

faculty.name: Doeke Romke Hekstra
faculty.title: Unknown/Verify
faculty.summary: Studies protein conformational dynamics and molecular biophysics using structural and quantitative methods; exact professorial rank should be verified.

faculty.name: Ahmad S. Khalil
faculty.title: Unknown/Verify
faculty.summary: Synthetic biologist (listed among Applied Physics affiliated faculty) working on engineered gene circuits and cellular systems; primary appointment and rank should be verified.

faculty.name: Markus Basan
faculty.title: Unknown/Verify
faculty.summary: Studies quantitative microbial physiology and metabolism; exact professorial rank should be verified.

faculty.name: Martin Bechthold
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on materials, digital fabrication, and design robotics; appointment is at the Graduate School of Design with Applied Physics affiliation.

faculty.name: David Clarke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies structural and functional materials, including ceramics, coatings, and electromechanical materials.

faculty.name: Roy G. Gordon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on thin-film materials chemistry, atomic layer deposition, and materials for energy applications.

faculty.name: Jenny Hoffman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses scanning probe microscopy to study superconductors, topological materials, and engineered thin films.

faculty.name: Jennifer A. Lewis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneers 3D printing of functional and biological materials through engineered inks and additive manufacturing.

faculty.name: Conor Walsh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops soft wearable robotic devices and exosuits for augmentation and rehabilitation.

faculty.name: Gage Hills
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops emerging nanoelectronic technologies, including carbon-nanotube-based computing systems.

faculty.name: Marko Loncar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on integrated and quantum nanophotonics, including thin-film lithium niobate and diamond photonic devices.

faculty.name: Kiyoul Yang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops integrated photonic devices and systems, including chip-scale frequency combs and quantum photonics.

faculty.name: Anurag Anshu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Computer Science working on quantum information theory, Hamiltonian complexity, and quantum algorithms.

faculty.name: Sitan Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Computer Science working on the theory of machine learning and quantum learning algorithms.

faculty.name: Giulia Semeghini
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental AMO physicist using neutral-atom arrays for quantum simulation and quantum information.

faculty.name: Amir Yacoby
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies quantum electronic systems, spin qubits, and nanoscale magnetic sensing.

faculty.name: Michael P. Brenner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applied mathematician working on fluid dynamics, machine learning for science, and problems in climate technology.

faculty.name: David Brooks
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on energy-efficient computer architecture and hardware systems for machine learning.

faculty.name: Frank N. Keutsch
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric chemistry, aerosols, and climate, including field and laboratory spectroscopy.

faculty.name: Petros Koumoutsakos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on computational science and engineering, combining simulation, fluid dynamics, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Na Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on control, optimization, and learning for networked and energy systems.

faculty.name: John H. Shaw
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies structural geology and geophysics, including earthquake hazards and subsurface energy resources.

faculty.name: Le Xie
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on electric power systems, grid data analytics, and the integration of renewable energy.

faculty.name: David R. Nelson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist working on soft matter, biophysics, and statistical mechanics of ordered and living systems.

faculty.name: L. Mahadevan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on the physics and mathematics of living and non-living matter, including morphogenesis and biomechanics.

faculty.name: Robert J. Wood
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops insect-scale and soft robots and the microfabrication and actuation technologies behind them.

faculty.name: Lene V. Hau
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on quantum optics, ultracold atoms, and light-matter interactions, including slowing and stopping light.

faculty.name: Robert M. Westervelt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist working on nanoscale electronic systems, imaging of electron flow, and quantum devices.

funding.note: Fully funded (GSAS PhD guarantee)

faculty.name: Adam E. Cohen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops optical and molecular tools, including fluorescent reporters of membrane voltage, to probe electrical signaling in cells and map neural and cardiac activity across scales from single molecules to whole organisms.

faculty.name: Xiaowei Zhuang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: HHMI Investigator who develops advanced imaging methods with single-molecule sensitivity and molecular-scale resolution, including super-resolution microscopy and MERFISH spatial genomics, to visualize cellular structures from chromatin organization to neuronal connectivity.

faculty.name: Andrew W. Murray
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses budding yeast, experimental evolution, and synthetic biology to uncover the 'rules of the game' that explain how cells function, evolve, and generate biological novelty in response to environmental pressures.

faculty.name: Rachelle Gaudet
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the mechanisms of signaling and transport across cellular membranes, using structural biology, biochemistry, and computation to study Nramp transporters, non-classical cadherins, ABC transporters, and TRP channels.

faculty.name: Nicholas Bellono
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how organisms adapt to their ecological and behavioral niches by dissecting signal transduction and ion-channel biophysics in specialized biological systems, connecting molecular mechanisms to organismal physiology and behavior.

faculty.name: Vinothan N. Manoharan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Wagner Family Professor of Chemical Engineering and Professor of Physics whose research spans soft matter and biophysics, studying self-assembly and developing optical methods such as engineered structural color and tools to manipulate microscale particles.

faculty.name: Sean R. Eddy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: HHMI Investigator who uses computational and probabilistic modeling of genome sequences to reconstruct evolutionary history, emphasizing RNA structure and function and improved methods for remote protein homology detection.

faculty.name: Michael P. Brenner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Catalyst Professor of Applied Mathematics and Applied Physics who uses mathematics and computation to study fluid mechanics, soft matter, and biological self-organization, including AI-assisted approaches to biomolecule design.

faculty.name: L. Mahadevan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lola England de Valpine Professor whose work applies mathematics and physics to soft matter, active systems, and biomechanics, modeling collective behavior and the mechanics of living systems.

faculty.name: Sharad Ramanathan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how multipotent cells receive and process signals to build correctly patterned tissues during early human development, and how neural circuits (e.g., in C. elegans) process signals to drive decision-making and behavior.

faculty.name: Ethan Garner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the self-organizing mechanisms that create and read out long-range shape in bacteria and archaea, using genetics, biochemistry, and biophysical imaging to examine cytoskeletal dynamics and cell-wall synthesis.

faculty.name: Philippe Cluzel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Molecular and Cellular Biology and Gordon McKay Professor of Applied Physics whose lab performs real-time systems analysis of signal transduction and genetic networks at the single-cell level, combining experiment and computation.

faculty.name: Maofu Liao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Cell Biology who uses single-particle cryo-electron microscopy and biochemistry to determine structures and mechanisms of membrane proteins and protein-DNA/RNA complexes, including transporters, CRISPR, and lipid-interacting proteins.

faculty.name: Eugene I. Shakhnovich
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Roy G. Gordon Professor of Chemistry whose group integrates theory, computation, and experiment to understand the physical principles of protein folding, functional dynamics, molecular evolution, drug resistance, and the origins of life.

faculty.name: Andrew C. Kruse
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Biological Chemistry and Molecular Pharmacology who uses protein engineering, structural biology, and pharmacology to elucidate the molecular basis of membrane-protein signaling, focusing on G protein-coupled receptors and other targets in neurotransmission and metabolism.

faculty.name: Stephen C. Harrison
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: HHMI Investigator and Professor of Biological Chemistry and Molecular Pharmacology who leads the Laboratory of Structural Cell Biology, using crystallography and cryo-EM to study viral architecture and cell entry, protein-DNA recognition, and signaling mechanisms.

faculty.name: David R. Nelson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Arthur K. Solomon Professor of Biophysics and Professor of Physics whose research spans collective effects in condensed matter and physical biology, including single-molecule biophysics, spatial population genetics, and the buckling of viral shells.

faculty.name: Vamsi K. Mootha
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: HHMI Investigator and Professor of Systems Biology and of Medicine (based at Massachusetts General Hospital) who combines genomics, systems biology, and classical biochemistry to study mitochondrial bioenergetics, evolution, and disease, including membrane biophysics of ion and metabolite transport.

faculty.name: Johan Paulsson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Systems Biology who studies how molecular randomness affects cellular processes in single cells, using theory and experiment to understand noise in gene networks and how cells exploit or suppress fluctuations.

faculty.name: Debora S. Marks
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Systems Biology who directs a computational biology lab building methods, drawing on statistical physics and machine learning, to model protein conformational plasticity, predict the effects of mutations on disease and drug response, and design synthetic proteins.

faculty.name: Timothy A. Springer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Latham Family Professor of Biological Chemistry and Molecular Pharmacology (Boston Children's Hospital) who studies how protein conformational change and tensile force activate integrins, von Willebrand factor, TGF-beta family members, and malaria-sporozoite adhesins.

faculty.name: Wesley P. Wong
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Biological Chemistry and Molecular Pharmacology and of Pediatrics (Boston Children's Hospital / Wyss Institute) who develops and applies single-molecule manipulation and detection methods, including DNA nanoswitch technology, to understand the physical basis of biological systems at the nanoscale.

funding.note: Fully funded (GSAS PhD guarantee)

faculty.name: Adam E. Cohen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops new physical tools to study molecules and cells, including the first fluorescent voltage-indicating proteins that enable all-optical mapping of neural activity. Holds joint appointments in Chemistry & Chemical Biology and Physics.

faculty.name: Eric J. Heller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneers time-dependent wavepacket methods for molecular spectroscopy and reaction dynamics. Known for discovering quantum scarring of wavefunctions by unstable periodic orbits, and extending wave mechanics to electron flow in 2D materials and graphene quantum dots. Earned his PhD in Chemical Physics from Harvard.

faculty.name: Hongkun Park
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the interface of quantum materials, nanophotonics, and bio-nano interfaces. Pioneered single-molecule transistors and developed diamond NV-center quantum sensors for room-temperature NMR detection of single proteins. Currently on leave as Corporate President of Samsung Electronics.

faculty.name: Kang-Kuen Ni
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Creates ultracold molecules at temperatures near absolute zero to study quantum-controlled chemical reactions. Recently demonstrated a two-qubit entangling quantum gate using individually controlled molecules, advancing molecular quantum information science.

faculty.name: Joonho Lee
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theoretical and computational methods for electron-electron and electron-vibration correlation problems. Applies quantum chemistry and quantum computing methods to energy-relevant materials including perovskites, moire materials, and electrocatalysis. Also a visiting researcher at Google Quantum AI.

faculty.name: Eugene I. Shakhnovich
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses statistical mechanics, Monte Carlo simulations, and machine learning to understand protein folding principles, the relationship between amino acid sequence and structure, and how protein stability mutations affect organismal fitness.

faculty.name: Boris Kozinsky
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Materials Intelligence Research group developing ML-accelerated atomistic and electronic structure methods for functional materials. Creator of the NequIP and Allegro neural network interatomic potentials, applied to catalysts, batteries, and 2D materials. Joint appointment with SEAS.

faculty.name: Jarad A. Mason
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Designs new classes of porous materials including liquids with intrinsic microporosity, microporous nanocrystals for gas transport in aqueous environments, and barocaloric materials for sustainable solid-state heating and cooling. Applies synthetic chemistry and nanoscience to challenges in energy, sustainability, and medicine.

faculty.name: Frank N. Keutsch
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies photochemical oxidation of volatile organic compounds that produce tropospheric ozone and secondary organic aerosol, combining field observations aboard NASA aircraft, laboratory kinetics studies, and laser-induced fluorescence instrument development. Also leads the SCoPEx solar geoengineering research project.

faculty.name: Daniel G. Nocera
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneered the artificial leaf — a silicon solar cell coated with self-healing catalysts that splits water into hydrogen and oxygen at high efficiency. Extended this to the bionic leaf, integrating engineered bacteria to produce liquid fuels and renewable ammonia fertilizer from sunlight, air, and water.

faculty.name: James G. Anderson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneered measurements of chlorine radical chain reactions that directly linked CFCs to stratospheric ozone depletion, informing the Montreal Protocol. Develops advanced laser, lidar, and high-altitude aircraft instrumentation to study climate-radiation-dynamics coupling in the atmosphere.

faculty.name: Joanna Aizenberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneers biomimetic materials chemistry, studying biological architectures such as biominerals and using those principles to design bio-inspired synthetic materials. Inventor of SLIPS (Slippery Liquid-Infused Porous Surfaces), omniphobic self-healing coatings inspired by pitcher plants. Holds joint appointments with SEAS and the Wyss Institute.

faculty.name: George M. Whitesides
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Developed foundational technologies including self-assembled monolayers on gold, soft lithography for nanofabrication, and paper-based microfluidic diagnostics. Currently investigates the origin of life through prebiotic chemistry, with over 1,200 publications and the highest Hirsch index of any living chemist.

faculty.name: Xiaowei Zhuang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Invented STORM super-resolution microscopy that overcomes the diffraction limit, and MERFISH for genome-scale single-cell imaging of thousands of RNA species simultaneously. Created a comprehensive cell atlas of the entire adult mouse brain. Joint appointment in Chemistry & Chemical Biology and Physics.

faculty.name: Efthimios Kaxiras
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Holds the John Hasbrouck Van Vleck Professorship in Physics with an affiliate appointment in Chemistry and Chemical Biology. Works on electronic properties of solids, graphene and 2D materials, and machine learning for materials modeling. Founding Director of the Institute for Applied Computational Science.

faculty.name: Roy G. Gordon
faculty.title: Professor Emeritus
faculty.summary: Studied chemical physics at Harvard under Nobel laureate J.H. Van Vleck. Research spans quantum mechanics, spectroscopy, chemical vapor deposition of thin films for solar cells and energy-efficient windows, and flow batteries for grid-scale energy storage. Elected to National Academy of Sciences in 1975.

faculty.name: Dudley R. Herschbach
faculty.title: Professor Emeritus
faculty.summary: Nobel Laureate (1986) who pioneered crossed molecular beam experiments that revealed detailed molecular-level mechanisms of elementary chemical reactions in single collisions. His work uncovered both rebound and stripping reaction dynamics, fundamentally transforming the field of chemical reaction dynamics.

faculty.name: Cynthia M. Friend
faculty.title: Professor Emerita
faculty.summary: First female full professor of chemistry at Harvard (1989). Research focused on heterogeneous catalysis on coinage metals and their alloys, developing selective catalytic syntheses. Directed the DOE Energy Frontier Research Center IMASC on mesoscale architectures for sustainable catalysis. Now President of the Kavli Foundation.

funding.note: Fully funded (GSAS PhD guarantee)

faculty.name: Cumrun Vafa
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: String theorist working on string compactifications, F-theory, and the swampland program constraining quantum gravity.

faculty.name: Andrew Strominger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: String theorist known for work on black hole entropy, quantum gravity, and asymptotic symmetries.

faculty.name: Mikhail Lukin
faculty.title: Joshua and Beth Friedman University Professor
faculty.sub_field: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics / Quantum Information
faculty.summary: Leading experimentalist in quantum optics and quantum information science. Co-director of the Harvard Quantum Initiative. Pioneered quantum simulation with Rydberg atom arrays and quantum networking with diamond color centers. Member of the National Academy of Sciences. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Subir Sachdev
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Theory / High Energy Theory
faculty.summary: Theorist studying strongly correlated quantum matter, quantum phase transitions, strange metals, and connections between condensed matter and holography.

faculty.name: Lisa Randall
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist working on particle physics, extra dimensions, dark matter, and cosmology.

faculty.name: Philip Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist studying electronic transport in graphene, two-dimensional materials, and van der Waals heterostructures.

faculty.name: Ashvin Vishwanath
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist studying topological phases of matter, quantum criticality, and emergent phenomena in correlated and moire systems.

faculty.name: Howard Georgi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical particle physicist known for grand unified theories, effective field theory, and model building beyond the Standard Model.

faculty.name: John Doyle
faculty.title: Henry B. Silsbee Professor of Physics
faculty.sub_field: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics / Precision Measurement
faculty.summary: Leading experimentalist in cold molecule physics and precision tests of fundamental symmetries. Co-director of the ACME collaboration measuring the electron electric dipole moment. 2024 Norman F. Ramsey Prize recipient. Member of the National Academy of Sciences.

faculty.name: Markus Greiner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist using ultracold atoms in optical lattices and quantum gas microscopy to simulate quantum many-body systems.

faculty.name: David A. Weitz
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: World leader in soft condensed matter physics. Pioneered droplet-based microfluidics and its applications in biology and materials science. His group develops widely used tools for high-throughput single-cell analysis. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Daniel Jafferis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: String theorist working on quantum gravity, holography, and the AdS/CFT correspondence.

faculty.name: Matthew Schwartz
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Leading expert in precision QCD calculations for the LHC. Author of the widely adopted graduate textbook 'Quantum Field Theory and the Standard Model.' Research on jet physics, precision collider phenomenology, and effective field theories.

faculty.name: Amir Yacoby
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist studying quantum electronic systems, spin qubits, and nanoscale magnetic sensing for quantum information.

faculty.name: Norman Yao
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Brilliant theorist working at the intersection of AMO physics, condensed matter, and quantum information. Pioneered Floquet time crystals and made key contributions to many-body localization and quantum sensing protocols. PECASE award (2025). Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Melissa Franklin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist working on collider experiments (ATLAS at the LHC) and detector development.

faculty.name: Matthew Reece
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist studying physics beyond the Standard Model, dark matter, and swampland/quantum-gravity constraints on effective theories.

faculty.name: Kang-Kuen Ni
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies ultracold molecular collisions and single-molecule quantum control for quantum information and controlled chemistry.

faculty.name: Jenny Hoffman
faculty.title: Clowes Professor of Science
faculty.summary: Expert in scanning tunneling microscopy of quantum materials, including high-temperature superconductors and topological materials. Co-author of the highly influential textbook 'The Physics and Chemistry of Materials.' Also known for completing a cross-country run across the US.

faculty.name: John Huth
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist working on collider experiments and searches at the energy frontier.

faculty.name: Lene Vestergaard Hau
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Famous for experimentally slowing light to 17 meters per second and stopping it entirely in a Bose-Einstein condensate. These dramatic demonstrations opened new possibilities for quantum information storage and manipulation.

faculty.name: Xiaowei Zhuang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer of STORM super-resolution microscopy and spatial genomics imaging (MERFISH) for studying cells and tissues.

faculty.name: Efthimios Kaxiras
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for electronic structure and materials, including studies of twisted graphene and quantum materials.

faculty.name: Carlos Argüelles-Delgado
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Rising star in neutrino physics and multi-messenger astronomy. Group works on IceCube neutrino observatory physics, quantum decoherence searches in neutrino oscillations, and detecting supernova neutrinos with the LHC detectors.

faculty.name: Jordan Cotler
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working at the interface of quantum field theory, gravity, and quantum information, including quantum chaos and holography.

faculty.name: Cora Dvorkin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical cosmologist studying the cosmic microwave background, large-scale structure, and the nature of dark matter, including machine-learning approaches to cosmological data.

faculty.name: Douglas Finkbeiner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Astrophysicist working on galactic structure, the interstellar medium, and observational signatures of dark matter.

faculty.name: John Kovac
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental cosmologist leading CMB polarization experiments (BICEP/Keck) at the South Pole to probe inflation and the early universe.

faculty.name: Christopher Stubbs
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist studying dark energy, supernova cosmology, and tests of fundamental physics.

faculty.name: John M. Doyle
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist developing cooling and trapping of atoms and molecules for quantum science and precision tests of fundamental physics.

faculty.name: Xing Fan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Searches for physics beyond the Standard Model through precision measurements using ion traps, including electron/positron Penning-trap experiments for matter-antimatter symmetry tests.

faculty.name: Lene V. Hau
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Known for slowing and stopping light in Bose-Einstein condensates; works on quantum optics, ultracold atoms, and nanoscale science.

faculty.name: Mikhail D. Lukin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads research in quantum optics and quantum information science, including neutral-atom quantum computers, solid-state qubits, and quantum networks.

faculty.name: Hongkun Park
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the interface of condensed matter, nanoscience, and biology, developing nanoscale and quantum tools including nano-bioelectronic interfaces.

faculty.name: Norman Y. Yao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies non-equilibrium quantum many-body physics, quantum simulation, and phenomena such as time crystals, bridging AMO and condensed matter.

faculty.name: Eric J. Heller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical/computational physicist working on semiclassical dynamics, quantum chaos, and wave phenomena in chemical and condensed systems.

faculty.name: Susanne F. Yelin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical AMO physicist working on quantum optics, collective effects, and quantum information science.

faculty.name: Michael P. Brenner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applied mathematician and physicist working on fluid dynamics, biophysics, self-assembly, and machine learning for the natural sciences.

faculty.name: SueYeon Chung
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies theoretical and computational neuroscience, connecting the geometry of neural representations in brains and artificial networks.

faculty.name: Adam E. Cohen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops optical tools such as genetically encoded voltage indicators to probe electrical dynamics in cells and neurons.

faculty.name: Michael M. Desai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the dynamics of evolution using theory and high-throughput experiments in microbial populations.

faculty.name: L. Mahadevan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on the physics and mathematics of living and non-living matter, including morphogenesis, biomechanics, and pattern formation.

faculty.name: Mara Prentiss
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental biophysicist studying DNA homology recognition, molecular motors, and optical techniques.

faculty.name: Sunghan Ro
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the statistical physics of soft and living matter out of equilibrium, focusing on collective phenomena in active matter.

faculty.name: Aravinthan D. T. Samuel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental biophysicist studying the neural basis of behavior in model organisms using imaging and quantitative methods.

faculty.name: Jennifer E. Hoffman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist using scanning probe microscopy to study superconductors, topological materials, and engineered quantum thin films.

faculty.name: Matteo Mitrano
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses ultrafast optical and X-ray techniques to control and probe emergent phenomena in correlated quantum materials.

faculty.name: Julia A. Mundy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Synthesizes atomically precise complex-oxide thin films and heterostructures to engineer new quantum materials.

faculty.name: Su-Yang Xu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Chemistry and Chemical Biology (affiliated with Physics) studying emergent electronic and optical properties of topological and broken-symmetry quantum materials.

faculty.name: Eslam Khalaf
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed matter theorist working on topological insulators/semimetals, superconductivity in graphene heterostructures, and moire systems.

faculty.name: Carlos Arguelles-Delgado
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies neutrino physics and searches for new physics using large neutrino telescopes such as IceCube.

faculty.name: Stefan Knirck
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist searching for axion dark matter using novel detectors at the intersection of particle physics, quantum sensing, and photonics.

faculty.name: Masahiro Morii
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist working on collider and flavor physics experiments.

faculty.name: Arthur M. Jaffe
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Mathematical physicist working on constructive quantum field theory and mathematical aspects of quantum theory.

faculty.name: Sonia Paban
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on cosmology, inflation, and connections to string theory and quantum field theory.

faculty.name: Matthew D. Schwartz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on quantum field theory, collider phenomenology, and machine-learning methods for particle physics.

faculty.name: Xi Yin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: String theorist working on conformal field theory, holography, and non-perturbative aspects of string theory.

faculty.name: Peter Galison
faculty.title: University Professor
faculty.summary: Historian and philosopher of physics studying the culture and practice of modern physics, imaging, and concepts of space and time.

programs.program_name: Biology (Cellular, Molecular, Developmental Biology and Biophysics), PhD

funding.note: Fully funded

faculty.name: Xin Chen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the epigenetic regulation of stem cell lineages, studying how chromatin and inherited histone marks control self-renewal versus differentiation decisions. HHMI Investigator in the Department of Biology.

faculty.name: Rejji Kuruvilla
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the sympathetic nervous system, focusing on its connectivity, development, and physiological functions and the neurotrophic signaling that shapes neuronal circuits.

faculty.name: Mark Van Doren
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates germ cell development and gonad formation, using Drosophila to study how sexual identity and the reproductive system are established.

faculty.name: Carl Wu
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Studies eukaryotic chromatin structure and function, including the biochemistry of nucleosome remodeling and gene transcription and live-cell single-molecule imaging of histone and nuclear protein dynamics.

faculty.name: Vincent Hilser
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Department of Biology
faculty.summary: Studies protein conformational fluctuations and their role in function, adaptation, and disease, developing statistical-thermodynamic frameworks for allostery and protein ensembles. Chair of the Department of Biology.

faculty.name: Steven A. Farber
faculty.title: Professor; Director, CMDB Graduate Program
faculty.summary: Uses real-time imaging in live zebrafish to study the cell and molecular biology of lipids, identifying genes that regulate digestive-organ lipid metabolism through biochemical and genetic strategies. Director of the CMDB Graduate Program.

faculty.name: Robert Johnston
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies development and gene regulation controlling cell-fate specification in human retinal organoids and fly eyes, including stochastic and deterministic mechanisms of photoreceptor patterning.

faculty.name: Yumi Kim
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates chromosome dynamics during meiosis, studying how chromosomes pair, recombine, and segregate to ensure genome integrity across generations.

faculty.name: John Kim
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works to decipher the biological function of microRNAs and other small RNAs encoded in the genome and how they regulate gene expression.

faculty.name: Tatjana Trcek
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the spatial organization of mRNAs and the regulation of post-transcriptional gene expression, using imaging to investigate RNA granules and germ-granule assembly.

faculty.name: Haiqing Zhao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the function and development of olfactory sensory neurons and the molecular mechanisms of odor detection and signal transduction.

faculty.name: Andrew Gordus
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how innate behaviors are encoded at the cellular and genetic level in invertebrate systems, combining behavior, imaging, and genetics.

faculty.name: Nichole Broderick
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the molecular and cellular mechanisms by which the microbiome affects animal development, physiology, and immunity, using Drosophila as a model.

faculty.name: Erik Andersen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the genetics and genomics of complex traits, host-pathogen genomics and drug resistance, and evolutionary genetics, largely using natural variation in Caenorhabditis nematodes.

faculty.name: Damian Ekiert
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Combines microbiology, parasitology, structural biology, cell biology, and molecular biology to study host-pathogen interactions and the machinery bacteria use to build and maintain their cell envelopes.

faculty.name: Gira Bhabha
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies structural mechanisms and the cell biology of microbes using cryo-electron microscopy and cryo-electron tomography to understand molecular machines and host-pathogen systems.

faculty.name: Jocelyne DiRuggiero
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the molecular microbial ecology of extremophiles and the molecular mechanisms of stress response and ribosome rescue, with a focus on archaea living in extreme environments.

faculty.name: Douglas Barrick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works toward an atomic-level description of structure and function in proteins, using repeat proteins as model systems to dissect folding, stability, and cooperativity. Department of Biophysics.

faculty.name: Karen Fleming
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure, energetics, and folding of membrane proteins, elucidating the biophysical principles that govern how membrane proteins fold and assemble. Department of Biophysics.

faculty.name: Gregory Bowman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies chromatin structure and nucleosome remodeling, investigating the mechanisms by which remodeling enzymes reposition nucleosomes to regulate access to DNA. Department of Biophysics.

faculty.name: Yuan He
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure and mechanism of how cells read, write, and repair the genome, using structural biology and biophysics to visualize large protein-DNA assemblies. Department of Biophysics.

faculty.name: Margaret Johnson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the control and dynamics of self-assembly in living systems using theory, modeling, and computation to understand how molecular components organize in space and time. Department of Biophysics.

funding.note: Fully funded

faculty.name: Doug Barrick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Statistical Thermodynamics, Protein Folding and Design
faculty.summary: Studies protein folding thermodynamics and design using repeat proteins as model systems, and investigates conformational transitions in the Notch signaling pathway that control cell fate determination. Authored the textbook Biothermodynamics: from Theory to Application.

faculty.name: Gregory Bowman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the structure and mechanisms of ATP-dependent chromatin remodelers such as Chd1 that reposition nucleosomes along DNA, using cryo-EM and X-ray crystallography to understand the twist-defect mechanism of nucleosome sliding.

faculty.name: Brian Camley
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses theory, computation, and simulation to study the physics of cell biology — how cells crawl, sense chemical gradients and electric fields, and migrate collectively through complex environments. Joint appointment with Physics and Astronomy.

faculty.name: Karen Fleming
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the physical principles of membrane protein folding and biogenesis in lipid bilayers, including chaperone functions and the BAM complex in Gram-negative bacteria. Elected AAAS Fellow in 2024 and received the Biophysical Society Avanti Award in Lipids.

faculty.name: Bertrand Garcia-Moreno
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies electrostatic effects in proteins using experimental and computational methods, focusing on buried ionizable groups in hydrophobic environments and engineering pH-sensitive switches for therapeutic applications. Serves as Executive Vice Dean of the Krieger School.

faculty.name: Thomas Graham
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops proximity-assisted photoactivation (PAPA) and single-molecule tracking methods to visualize individual endogenous molecular complexes regulating transcription in live cells. Joined the department in July 2025.

faculty.name: Yuan He
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Uses cryo-electron microscopy and biochemical approaches to study how cells read, write, and repair their genomes, with focus on transcription initiation complexes and DNA double-strand break repair. Joined Johns Hopkins in January 2025 as a Bloomberg Distinguished Professor with appointments in Biophysics and Biology.

faculty.name: William Huang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates biochemical reactions at the cell membrane using reconstitution and single-molecule approaches, focusing on kinetic proofreading mechanisms in Ras GTPase activation and membrane localization effects on signaling.

faculty.name: Margaret Johnson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational and theoretical models of protein self-assembly on membranes, focusing on clathrin-mediated endocytosis and HIV-1 Gag lattice assembly. Creator of the NERDSS reaction-diffusion simulation package.

faculty.name: Albert Lau
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses molecular dynamics simulations and free energy calculations on supercomputers to study atomic mechanisms of ionotropic glutamate receptors that mediate excitatory synaptic transmission, informing drug discovery for neurological disorders.

faculty.name: Juliette Lecomte
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses NMR spectroscopy and other biophysical methods to investigate the structure, dynamics, and function of heme proteins, particularly truncated hemoglobins from bacteria, cyanobacteria, algae, and deep-sea extremophiles. Elected AAAS Fellow in 2023.

faculty.name: Evgenia Nikolova
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure and dynamics of nucleic acids and their protein complexes, focusing on how pioneer transcription factors such as Sox2 and Oct4 engage nucleosomes to remodel chromatin and activate gene expression. Joined the department in 2017.

faculty.name: Sarah Woodson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how RNA molecules fold into three-dimensional structures and how RNA-protein complexes such as the ribosome assemble, using single-molecule fluorescence, X-ray footprinting, and SAXS. Also investigates small non-coding RNAs and the Hfq chaperone in bacteria.

faculty.name: Yaojun Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theoretical and computational models of biomolecular phase separation using statistical mechanics and polymer theory, studying how condensates form, the interface resistance that controls material exchange, and how phase separation organizes intracellular space. Named a 2025 Sloan Research Fellow.

funding.note: Fully funded

faculty.name: Xun Jia
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chief of Medical Physics Division. Develops GPU-accelerated Monte Carlo radiation transport simulations, AI-driven CBCT reconstruction, deep learning for treatment planning, and preclinical FLASH radiotherapy technologies. AIMBE Fellow, FAAPM, over 300 publications and 7,500+ citations.

faculty.name: Kai Ding
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Founding Co-Director of the Medical Physics PhD Program. Directs the Smart Biomaterial & Image-Guided Radiotherapy Lab, developing X-ray-based intra-operative image-guided platforms (XIOSIS) for smart material delivery and AI-based tumor contouring.

faculty.name: Todd McNutt
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Clinical Informatics and Director of the Medical Physics Residency. Creator of Oncospace (now Plan AI), an FDA-approved AI-powered informatics system that uses data from over 5,000 patients to optimize radiation treatment plans. Acquired by Sun Nuclear/Mirion Medical in 2025.

faculty.name: Heng Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Proton Physics and Vice Chief of Physics/Clinical-NCR at the Johns Hopkins National Proton Center. Leads commissioning of ultra-high dose rate (UHDR) proton beamlines for FLASH research, real-time gated proton therapy, and spatially fractionated proton therapy clinical trials. FAAPM, 142+ publications.

faculty.name: Robert Hobbs
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Radiation Safety. Develops microscale dosimetry models for alpha-particle emitting radionuclides, including nephron-based kidney and bone marrow toxicity models. Chair of AAPM committee on Radionuclide Therapy and elected MIRD Committee member.

faculty.name: Junghoon Lee
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Medical Imaging and Computing Analysis (MICA) lab. Develops real-time intraoperative dosimetry systems (iRUF) for prostate brachytherapy, automatic seed reconstruction algorithms, and multimodality image registration for brachytherapy treatment planning. 145+ publications.

faculty.name: Weihua Mao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in MRI-guided adaptive radiotherapy on MR-linac systems, markerless lung tumor tracking using EPID imaging, CBCT reconstruction for soft-tissue visualization, and real-time motion management for pancreas and lung cancers. 119 publications, 2,000+ citations.

faculty.name: Wil Ngwa
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Biomaterial Drones & Global Health Radiation Oncology
faculty.summary: PI of the Biomaterial Drones and Radio-immunotherapy Lab. Develops implantable biomaterial drones for smart delivery of immunoadjuvants combined with radiotherapy to trigger in-situ vaccination against metastatic disease. Director of Global Health Catalyst; Chair, Lancet Oncology Commission for Sub-Saharan Africa.

faculty.name: Sarah Han-Oh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Tumor Motion Management & Pancreatic Cancer Radiotherapy
faculty.summary: Vice Chief of Medical Physics Division (Baltimore). Develops novel tumor tracking technologies including microwave radar fiducial localization and ultrasound-based intra-fraction motion tracking for pancreatic cancer SBRT. Active APEx surveyor for ASTRO accreditation.

faculty.name: Michael Roumeliotis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Brachytherapy Physics. Advances AI-based auto-segmentation (DCT-UNet) for MRI-guided cervical cancer brachytherapy, robotic bronchoscopy-guided lung brachytherapy, and ultrasound-compatible gynecologic brachytherapy applicators.

faculty.name: Mohammad Rezaee
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Invented an orthovoltage x-ray FLASH irradiator for preclinical research and established the FLASH radiation research program at Johns Hopkins. Investigates DNA damage mechanisms from high-LET particles, UHDR proton FLASH dosimetry, and platinum-based radiosensitizers.

faculty.name: William T. Hrinivich
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Proton Therapy & Deep Reinforcement Learning Planning
faculty.summary: Medical physicist at the Johns Hopkins Proton Therapy Center. Develops GPU-based ultra-fast proton pencil beam dose calculation algorithms, deep reinforcement learning for automated VMAT/IMPT planning, and custom hardware solutions (table-mounted range shifter boards) for pediatric craniospinal irradiation.

faculty.name: Rachel B. Ger
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on quantitative imaging biomarkers (radiomics) for cancer prognosis, proton dose evaluation and range uncertainty analysis, deformable image registration accuracy, and stem cell-sparing radiotherapy to reduce xerostomia in head and neck cancer patients.

faculty.name: Khadija Sheikh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Program Director of the Medical Physics Residency. Specializes in breath-hold techniques for breast proton therapy, daily CBCT-based adaptive planning workflows for proton treatment, and pediatric proton therapy planning optimization.

faculty.name: An Qin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Proton Arc Therapy & Deformable Image Registration
faculty.summary: Develops spot-scanning proton arc therapy (SPArc) techniques for head and neck, lung, and prostate cancers. Works on deformable image registration for tumor/organ shrinkage monitoring, CBCT-based treatment dose monitoring, and U-net-generated synthetic CT for MRI-only treatment planning.

programs.program_name: Molecular Biophysics, PhD (Program in Molecular Biophysics)

funding.note: Fully funded

faculty.name: Cynthia Wolberger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structural and mechanistic basis for transcriptional regulation and ubiquitin signaling, using X-ray crystallography and cryo-EM to understand how histone modifications control gene expression and DNA repair.

faculty.name: James M. Berger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how multi-subunit assemblies use ATP to control DNA replication and chromosome superstructure, with a focus on replication initiation, replisome assembly, and topoisomerases as anti-cancer drug targets.

faculty.name: Jie Xiao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops advanced single-molecule and superresolution imaging approaches to study molecular mechanisms of bacterial cell division, transcription regulation, and chromosome organization in living cells.

faculty.name: Doug Barrick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies tandem repeat proteins as model systems to quantify cooperativity in protein folding, using nearest-neighbor Ising models and consensus repeat design to understand folding energy landscapes and pathway selection.

faculty.name: Karen Fleming
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the physical principles governing membrane protein folding, stability, and assembly in bacterial outer membranes, studying how lipid bilayers and periplasmic chaperones catalyze beta-barrel protein biogenesis.

faculty.name: Juliette Lecomte
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses NMR spectroscopy and other biophysical methods to study heme protein structure, stability, and dynamics, including truncated hemoglobins from microorganisms and their roles in reactive nitrogen species management.

faculty.name: Sarah Woodson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies RNA folding, RNA-protein interactions, and ribosome assembly using single-molecule fluorescence methods, with a focus on how the Hfq chaperone facilitates small RNA-mRNA pairing and how ribosomal proteins assemble co-transcriptionally.

faculty.name: Gregory Bowman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the molecular mechanisms of ATP-dependent chromatin remodelers such as Chd1 that reposition nucleosomes along DNA, using cryo-EM and single-molecule approaches to understand how these motors control DNA accessibility.

faculty.name: Yuan He
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Uses cryo-EM and AI-based tools to elucidate the molecular mechanisms of large multi-subunit complexes involved in eukaryotic gene transcription and DNA damage repair, including the transcription pre-initiation complex and NHEJ machinery.

faculty.name: Edward Twomey
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops time-resolved cryo-EM methods to capture dynamic states of glutamate receptors, revealing how neurotransmitter binding gates ion channel opening at physiological temperatures, with implications for neurological disorders.

faculty.name: Bin Wu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops single-molecule microscopy methods to visualize RNA birth, trafficking, translation, and decay in live cells and neurons, including the SINAPS technique for real-time imaging of translation on single mRNAs.

faculty.name: Albert Lau
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Combines computational and experimental approaches to study the atomic details of ionotropic glutamate receptors, investigating conformational dynamics that underlie synaptic transmission and identifying novel drug-binding sites.

faculty.name: Jungsan Sohn
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how innate immune stress sensors assemble, detect danger signals, and initiate responses, focusing on inflammasome formation, self vs. non-self DNA discrimination, and phase separation in DNA sensing pathways.

faculty.name: Dominique Frueh
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel NMR methods to study protein dynamics in large enzymatic systems, focusing on the molecular mechanisms of nonribosomal peptide synthetases that produce antibiotics and other bioactive compounds.

faculty.name: Chang Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses cryo-EM and cryo-ET to study coronavirus-host interactions, including the structural basis of SARS-CoV-2 proofreading, mechanisms of antiviral drug resistance, and modulation of host transcription by viral proteins.

funding.note: All admitted PhD students fully supported (TA/RA/fellowship, tuition + health benefits)

faculty.name: Bitan Roy

faculty.summary: NSF CAREER awardee (2023). Studies topological phases of matter, superconductivity, magnetism, and disordered systems using field-theoretic and numerical approaches.

faculty.summary: Department Chair and Optica Fellow. Studies organic semiconductors, exciton physics, entangled spin states, and integrated photonics.

faculty.name: Volkmar Dierolf

faculty.name: Elliott Rosenberg
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies high-temperature superconductors and excitonic insulators using single crystal growth and strain-based measurement techniques.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Rickman
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Materials Science and Engineering

faculty.name: Michael Stavola
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; Sherman Fairchild Chair in Physics
faculty.summary: Sherman Fairchild Chair. Studies defects and impurities in semiconductors using vibrational spectroscopy.

faculty.name: Ariel Sommer
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Uses ultracold lithium-6 Fermi gases to simulate strongly correlated materials, electron transport, and normal-superconductor junctions.

faculty.name: Coraline Fujiwara
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies optical lattices, topological phenomena, Floquet engineering, and quantum thermodynamics with ultracold atoms.

faculty.name: Sera Cremonini
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Works on string theory, black holes, and connections between quantum field theory and gravity, with techniques applicable to condensed matter physics.

faculty.name: Timm Wrase
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies string theory, early universe evolution, inflation, and dark energy.

faculty.name: Rosi Reed
faculty.title: Professor of Physics

faculty.name: Anders Knospe
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies quark-gluon plasma and heavy ion collisions, with work on detector development at Brookhaven.

faculty.name: M. Virginia McSwain
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Observational astrophysicist studying massive binary stars and stellar evolution.

faculty.name: Daniel Ou-Yang
faculty.title: Professor of Physics

faculty.name: Aurelia Honerkamp-Smith
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics

faculty.name: Jerome Licini
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Focuses on physics education research and experimental physics instrumentation.

faculty.name: Joshua W. Shaevitz
faculty.title: Professor

Serves as Director of Graduate Studies for the Biophysics program and co-Director of the Center for the Physics of Biological Function.

faculty.name: William Bialek
faculty.title: Professor
