faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Observational cosmologist developing balloon-borne and ground instruments to study the CMB.

faculty.name: Aviram Uri
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist using local scanning probes to image quantum electronic phases in two-dimensional materials.

faculty.name: Ravindra Bhatt
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Electrical & Computer Engineering)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary ECE) working on condensed matter theory of disordered and correlated electronic systems.

faculty.name: Roberto Car
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Chemistry)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary Chemistry) known for the Car-Parrinello ab initio molecular dynamics method.

faculty.name: Mihalis Dafermos
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Mathematics)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary Mathematics) working on the mathematics of general relativity and black hole dynamics.

faculty.name: Nathalie P. de Leon
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Electrical & Computer Engineering)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary ECE) developing solid-state quantum systems for sensing and quantum information.

faculty.name: Sarang Gopalakrishnan
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Electrical & Computer Engineering)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary ECE) working on quantum dynamics and transport in many-body systems.

faculty.name: Andrew Houck
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Electrical & Computer Engineering)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary ECE) building superconducting circuits for quantum computation and simulation.

faculty.name: Leslie Schoop
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Chemistry)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary Chemistry) synthesizing and characterizing new topological and quantum materials.

faculty.name: Mansour Shayegan
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Electrical & Computer Engineering)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary ECE) studying correlated phenomena in high-mobility two-dimensional electron systems.

faculty.name: David Tank
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Neuroscience)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary Neuroscience) studying neural circuit dynamics with quantitative and imaging methods.

faculty.name: Jeffrey D. Thompson
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Electrical & Computer Engineering)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary ECE) building neutral-atom platforms for quantum computing and networking.

faculty.name: Salvatore Torquato
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Chemistry)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary Chemistry) working on statistical mechanics of disordered and hyperuniform materials.

faculty.name: Ned Wingreen
faculty.title: Associated Professor (primary: Molecular Biology)
faculty.summary: Associated physics faculty (primary Molecular Biology) applying physics to cellular organization and microbial systems.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Junichiro Kono
faculty.title: Karl F. Hasselmann Chair in Engineering; Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Leader in optical studies of condensed matter and nanosystems, spanning terahertz spectroscopy, ultrafast optics and quantum optical phenomena. Director of the Smalley-Curl Institute.

faculty.name: Alessandro Alabastri
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Expert in nanophotonics and computational modeling of photo-thermal interactions in nanostructures, with applications to light-to-heat conversion and solar distillation. Program Chair of Applied Physics.

faculty.name: Naomi J. Halas
faculty.title: University Professor; Stanley C. Moore Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Pioneer of plasmonics who showed that nanoparticle morphology controls optical properties, with applications in biomedicine, sensing, solar energy and photocatalysis.

faculty.name: Gururaj Naik
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops nanophotonic devices and metamaterials for renewable energy, imaging and chemical detection, focusing on light and heat management.

faculty.name: Yuji Zhao
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical & Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Leads the WIDE Lab. Studies quantum photonics, quantum computing and sensing, advanced materials and integrated circuits for quantum applications.

faculty.name: Douglas Natelson
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Astronomy; Associate Dean for Research
faculty.summary: Studies experimental condensed matter physics and nanoscale systems including nanoelectronics, strongly correlated materials and plasmonics.

faculty.name: Pengcheng Dai
faculty.title: Sam and Helen Worden Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist using neutron scattering to study correlated electron materials, superconductors and magnetism.

faculty.name: Emilia Morosan
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist synthesizing and studying quantum materials and their magnetic and electronic phenomena.

faculty.name: Andriy Nevidomskyy
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying strong electron correlations and exotic quantum phases in heavy-fermion materials, iron-based superconductors and frustrated magnets.

faculty.name: Qimiao Si
faculty.title: Professor of Physics & Astronomy
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist known for work on quantum criticality in strongly correlated electron systems and unconventional superconductivity.

faculty.name: Ming Yi
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist using angle-resolved photoemission and x-ray scattering to study high-temperature superconductors, topological and correlated materials.

faculty.name: Hanyu Zhu
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Materials Science and NanoEngineering
faculty.summary: Engineers materials at the atomic level with light, studying interactions among lattice, electrons and electromagnetic waves to create quantum behaviors for information technology and detectors.

faculty.name: Yonglong Xie
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Investigates emergent phenomena in quantum materials via scanning probe microscopy and quantum transport, focusing on topology, correlations and 2D heterostructures.

faculty.name: Thomas C. Killian
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Astronomy; Dean of the Wiess School of Natural Sciences
faculty.summary: Leads experiments in ultracold atomic and plasma physics, including ultracold neutral plasmas and Bose-Einstein condensates, with applications to quantum computing and precision measurement.

faculty.name: Randall G. Hulet
faculty.title: Fayez Sarofim Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Studies ultra-low-temperature atomic gases, including Bose-Einstein condensates and fermionic gases, using optical lattices to simulate condensed matter models.

faculty.name: Guido Pagano
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Uses laser-cooled trapped ions and neutral atoms to study quantum many-body phenomena and quantum information processing.

faculty.name: Songtao Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops quantum information processing using optically interfaced solid-state spins in silicon toward scalable quantum networks and integrated quantum chips.

faculty.name: Kevin Kelly
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Electrical & Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops novel imaging and sensing technologies including single-pixel cameras and computational spectroscopy.

faculty.name: Stephan Link
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry and Electrical & Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies plasmonics and nanophotonics at the single-particle and single-molecule level, including energy transfer processes.

faculty.name: Peter Nordlander
faculty.title: Professor of Physics & Astronomy
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist studying plasmonics and nanophotonics, including quantum plasmonics and nanoparticle optics.

faculty.name: Aditya D. Mohite
faculty.title: William M. Rice Trustee Professor of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates photophysical processes at interfaces of layered 2D and perovskite materials for photovoltaic and photocatalytic energy conversion.

faculty.name: Jacob Robinson
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering and Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Develops nanofabricated devices to measure and manipulate the structural and functional dynamics of living neural circuits.

faculty.name: Pulickel M. Ajayan
faculty.title: Benjamin M. and Mary Greenwood Anderson Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: A pioneer of carbon nanotube research who develops and characterizes nanostructured materials for energy, electronics and mechanical applications.

faculty.name: Yimo Han
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Materials Science and NanoEngineering
faculty.summary: Uses electron microscopy to characterize atomic-level structure of low-dimensional nanomaterials, biomaterials and interfaces for electronics, biosensing and clean energy.

faculty.name: Chong Xie
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering and Neuroengineering
faculty.summary: Develops ultraflexible nanoelectronic neural interfaces, including neural threads for brain recording and stimulation that integrate seamlessly with living tissue.

faculty.name: Richard G. Baraniuk
faculty.title: C. Sidney Burrus Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on multiscale computational signal and image processing, compressive sensing, data science and machine learning.

faculty.name: Anthony A. Chan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies theoretical space plasma physics, including acceleration and transport of relativistic electrons trapped in Earth's magnetospheric radiation belts.

faculty.name: Pramod Dash
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Nina and Michael Zilkha Distinguished Chair (UTHealth McGovern Medical School)
faculty.summary: Chair of Neurobiology and Anatomy at UTHealth McGovern Medical School; studies molecular mechanisms of memory and therapeutic targets for memory disorders after traumatic brain injury. Affiliated Applied Physics advisor via the Texas Medical Center.

faculty.name: K. Jane Grande-Allen
faculty.title: Isabel C. Cameron Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Investigates the structure-function relationship of heart valves and develops biomaterials and tissue-engineered constructs using cell mechanobiology and micro/nanofabrication.

faculty.name: Anna-Karin Gustavsson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Develops advanced 3D super-resolution and single-molecule imaging to study cellular nanoscale structures and dynamics related to disease. Directs the Rice Center for Nanoscale Imaging Sciences.

faculty.name: Caleb Kemere
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering and Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Works on neuroengineering and neural signal processing, including deep brain stimulation and the neural mechanisms of memory.

faculty.name: Anatoly B. Kolomeisky
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Uses statistical mechanics and analytical, computational and machine-learning methods to study molecular mechanisms including biological transport, catalysis and protein-DNA interactions. Chair of Chemistry.

faculty.name: Lan Luan
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops multimodal neural interfaces combining electrical and optical technologies to monitor and manipulate brain activity for studying and treating neurological disorders.

faculty.name: Jianpeng Ma
faculty.title: Professor (Lodwick T. Bolin Professor; now primarily at Fudan University)
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for simulating, modeling and refining flexible biomolecular systems and supramolecular complexes. Longtime Baylor/Rice professor now based primarily at Fudan University; listed among Applied Physics advisors.

faculty.name: Amanda Marciel
faculty.title: William Marsh Rice Trustee Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how monomer chemistry and sequence govern charged polymers in solution and at interfaces to design stimuli-responsive materials for separations and tissue engineering.

faculty.name: Peter J. A. Nordlander
faculty.title: Wiess Chair and Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Theoretical and computational modeling of plasmonics and nanophotonics, examining electronic and optical properties of nanoparticles and light-matter interaction at the nanoscale. (Listed in the catalog as 'Peter Norlander'.)

faculty.name: Xaq Pitkow
faculty.title: Associate Professor (now Carnegie Mellon University; formerly Rice ECE / Baylor)
faculty.summary: Theoretical and computational neuroscientist studying how the brain performs structured computations. Formerly held a Rice ECE appointment; now at Carnegie Mellon University's Neuroscience Institute and listed among Applied Physics advisors.

faculty.name: Robert M. Raphael
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Studies biological membranes and the biophysics of inner-ear hair cells to develop new hearing implants and therapies.

faculty.name: Harel Shouval
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering (Rice) and Neuroscience (UTHealth)
faculty.summary: Uses theoretical and computational approaches to study the rules of synaptic plasticity and their implications for learning, memory and neural development.

faculty.name: Jerzy Szablowski
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Develops noninvasive technologies combining synthetic biology and hardware to monitor and control brain activity, including drug delivery and blood-based biomarkers of neural activity.

faculty.name: Ming Tang
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Materials Science and NanoEngineering
faculty.summary: Uses mesoscale modeling such as the phase-field method to simulate materials evolution, with applications to lithium-ion batteries, grain boundaries and soft matter.

faculty.name: James M. Tour
faculty.title: T. T. and W. F. Chao Professor of Chemistry; Professor of Materials Science and NanoEngineering
faculty.summary: Develops methods to synthesize graphene and MXenes, extract critical metals from waste, destroy toxic chemicals such as PFAS, and build light-activated molecular machines.

faculty.name: Ashok Veeraraghavan
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on computational imaging, compressive sensing and computer vision, including lensless FlatCam and FlatScope systems for neural interfaces.

faculty.name: Rafael Verduzco
faculty.title: A. J. Hartsook Professor of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies polymer chemistry and characterization, including polymeric membranes for water treatment, bottlebrush polymers, covalent organic frameworks and bioelectronic devices.

faculty.name: Julea Vlassakis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Designs micro- and nanoscale single-cell and single-molecule technologies to study proteins and their interactions, with applications to pediatric cancers such as Ewing sarcoma.

faculty.name: Geoff Wehmeyer
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates nanoscale heat transfer, including thermal property measurements of nanomaterials and thermal management for microelectronics and energy conversion.

faculty.name: Peter G. Wolynes
faculty.title: Bullard-Welch Foundation Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Studies many-body phenomena in biology, chemistry and physics, including protein folding, biomolecular structure prediction and the energy landscapes of glasses.

faculty.name: Michael S. Wong
faculty.title: Tina and Sunit Patel Professor in Molecular Nanotechnology
faculty.summary: Applies materials chemistry to chemical engineering problems, focusing on catalysis for water treatment and metal nanoparticle synthesis and structure-property relationships.

faculty.name: Raudel Avila
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Models and simulates bioelectronics for healthcare, including dissolvable pacemakers, implantable sensors and drug-delivery technologies. Member of the Applied Physics admissions committee.

faculty.name: Kushal Bagchi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Studies molecular packing in organic solids and self-assembly for sustainability and healthcare, directing organization through nanostructured surfaces. Member of the Applied Physics admissions committee.

faculty.name: Vir Bulchandani
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist studying quantum many-body physics, far-from-equilibrium dynamics and transport, and connections between quantum computing and condensed matter. Member of the Applied Physics admissions committee.

faculty.name: Nai-Hui Chia
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Studies quantum algorithms, quantum complexity theory and quantum cryptography, identifying tasks with quantum advantage. Member of the Applied Physics admissions committee.

faculty.name: Shengxi Huang
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies light-matter interactions of quantum materials and nanostructures, developing quantum optical platforms and biochemical sensing for medical diagnosis. Member of the Applied Physics admissions committee.

faculty.name: Hae Yeon Lee
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Materials Science and NanoEngineering
faculty.summary: Investigates optoelectronic and photonic properties of low-dimensional quantum materials at the nanoscale using scanning microscopy, designing next-generation devices. Member of the Applied Physics admissions committee.

faculty.name: Xuedan Ma
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Materials Science and NanoEngineering
faculty.summary: Studies quantum optics, nanophotonics, cavity quantum electrodynamics and polariton chemistry in low-dimensional materials using high-resolution imaging and spectroscopy. Member of the Applied Physics admissions committee.

funding.note: Research Assistantships fund salary, tuition allowance, health subsidy

faculty.name: Charlotte Bøttcher
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies condensed matter systems at the intersection of quantum information science, focusing on quantum phase transitions in Josephson junction arrays and hybrid material platforms for quantum computing. Joining Stanford Applied Physics in 2025.

faculty.name: Philip H. Bucksbaum
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses ultrafast lasers to probe atomic and molecular structure and dynamics on femtosecond and attosecond timescales, directing the Stanford PULSE Institute and advancing strong-field laser-atom physics and time-resolved x-ray science.

faculty.name: Michel Digonnet
faculty.title: Professor (Research)
faculty.summary: Develops advanced fiber-optic sensors, gyroscopes, and radiation-balanced fiber lasers, with expertise spanning photonic-bandgap fibers, slow-light fiber Bragg gratings, and MEMS-based optical acoustic sensors.

faculty.name: Martin M. Fejer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates nonlinear optical materials and devices, guided wave optics, and microstructured ferroelectrics for applications in lasers, precision optical characterization, and quantum science.

faculty.name: Ian R. Fisher
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies quantum materials with unconventional electronic and magnetic ground states, with an emphasis on design and discovery of new materials and the use of strain as a probe of and tuning parameter for exotic electronic phases. Serves as Department Chair.

faculty.name: Surya Ganguli
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Applies statistical physics and deep learning theory to understand how neural circuits compute, spanning synaptic plasticity, population dynamics, and the theory of deep neural networks.

faculty.name: Benjamin Good
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies tools from statistical physics and population genetics to understand evolutionary dynamics in rapidly evolving microbial populations such as the gut microbiome, bridging non-equilibrium physics and quantitative biology.

faculty.name: Tony Heinz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates electronic properties and dynamics of nanoscale materials including graphene and transition metal dichalcogenide monolayers using advanced optical spectroscopies. Directs the Edward L. Ginzton Laboratory.

faculty.name: Harold Y. Hwang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies complex oxide materials and heterostructures, with notable contributions to superconductivity in infinite-layer nickelates, freestanding oxide membranes, and emergent phenomena at oxide interfaces. Directs SIMES.

faculty.name: Aharon Kapitulnik
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates correlated and disordered electron systems in reduced dimensions, developing precision instrumentation to detect subtle physical signatures including time-reversal symmetry breaking in unconventional superconductors.

faculty.name: Mark A. Kasevich
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops atom interferometry and quantum metrology techniques for precision inertial sensing, entanglement-enhanced measurements, gravitational wave detection, and tests of fundamental physics including the equivalence principle.

faculty.name: Young Lee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies novel electronic and magnetic materials in single crystalline form, focusing on quantum spin liquids, exotic superconductivity, and topological phases in quantum magnets using neutron and x-ray scattering.

faculty.name: Benjamin L. Lev
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Explores nonequilibrium quantum many-body physics using confocal cavity QED with ultracold quantum gases of dysprosium and twisted 2D materials, investigating quantum neural networks, spin glasses, and novel phases of quantum matter.

faculty.name: Hideo Mabuchi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the interface of quantum optics, cavity QED, and quantum information science, developing coherent Ising machines for combinatorial optimization and advancing nonlinear photonic systems.

faculty.name: Kathryn A. Moler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops and applies scanning SQUID microscopy to image magnetic fields and supercurrents in quantum materials with micron-scale resolution, studying vortex dynamics, topological insulators, and oxide heterointerfaces.

faculty.name: David A. Reis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses femtosecond x-ray scattering and laser-based techniques at LCLS to study ultrafast dynamics in solids, including nonequilibrium lattice dynamics, high-harmonic generation in solids, and photo-induced phase transitions.

faculty.name: Amir Safavi-Naeini
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops chip-scale quantum photonic and acoustic devices on thin-film lithium niobate, including quantum acoustic processors, squeezed light sources, and microwave-to-optical transducers for quantum networks.

faculty.name: David Schuster
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Advances superconducting circuit quantum electrodynamics for quantum computing, focusing on quantum error correction, high-coherence qubits, and microwave-to-optical quantum transduction using Rydberg atoms.

faculty.name: Zhi-Xun Shen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops photon-based instrumentation including ARPES to study quantum materials, best known for discovering the momentum-resolved d-wave pairing gap and pseudogap in high-temperature superconductors.

faculty.name: Jonathan Simon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Creates synthetic quantum matter using strongly interacting photons confined in metamaterial structures, exploring phenomena from Weyl semimetals to fractional quantum Hall states of light and developing mm-wave-to-optical transducers.

faculty.name: Yuri Suzuki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Explores emergent interfacial phenomena and functional properties in complex oxide thin films and heterostructures, focusing on low-dimensional electron gases, spin current generation, and multifunctional materials.

faculty.name: Alfred Zong
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies light-induced nonequilibrium phenomena in quantum materials using ultrafast electron diffraction, attosecond transient absorption spectroscopy, and coherent diffraction imaging with in-situ strain and gating capabilities.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: David Goldhaber-Gordon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how electrons organize and interact when confined to nanoscale structures, using cryogenic precision measurements and scanning probe techniques to explore exotic quantum states, moire superlattices, and topological insulators.

faculty.name: Kathryn Moler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses scanning SQUID microscopy to image and characterize superconducting and magnetic phenomena in quantum materials, including vortex dynamics, topological insulators, and oxide heterostructures.

faculty.name: Aharon Kapitulnik
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates emergent phenomena in strongly correlated electron systems, including superconductivity and quantum phase transitions, using home-built scanning probe microscopes and precision magneto-optical tools.

faculty.name: Steven Kivelson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theories for strongly correlated electronic systems, explaining how electron interactions produce emergent macroscopic phenomena including high-temperature superconductivity, quantum liquid crystals, and spin liquids.

faculty.name: Srinivas Raghu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies emergent behavior in quantum condensed matter systems, including non-Fermi liquids, quantum criticality, topological photonics, and Chern-Simons matter dualities in strongly interacting disordered systems.

faculty.name: Vedika Khemani
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies non-equilibrium quantum phases of matter, including time crystals and many-body quantum dynamics, exploring fundamental questions about thermalization and emergent phenomena in driven quantum systems.

faculty.name: Benjamin Feldman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Explores quantum systems in two-dimensional materials and moire heterostructures, investigating how electron interactions produce symmetry breaking, topological excitations, and new correlated phases.

faculty.name: Mark Kasevich
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneers atom interferometry and quantum sensing technologies for precision measurement, including tests of general relativity, gravitational wave detection with atomic sensors, and matter-wave interferometry at macroscopic scales.

faculty.name: Benjamin Lev
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Explores quantum many-body physics at the intersection of ultracold atomic physics, quantum optics, and condensed matter, using confocal cavity QED to engineer out-of-equilibrium quantum gases and control twisted 2D materials.

faculty.name: Monika Schleier-Smith
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Advances optical control of interactions among laser-cooled atoms for quantum simulation, metrology, and computation, developing entanglement-enhanced sensing protocols and engineering novel quantum many-body states.

faculty.name: Jason Hogan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops atom interferometry techniques for precision tests of fundamental physics, including gravitational wave detection with the MAGIS-100 atomic sensor, tests of general relativity, and searches for dark matter.

faculty.name: Savas Dimopoulos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates fundamental physics beyond the Standard Model, co-proposing the Supersymmetric Standard Model and large extra dimensions; currently uses atom interferometry to test general relativity at unprecedented precision.

faculty.name: Peter Graham
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theories beyond the Standard Model spanning cosmology, dark matter, and gravitational wave detection, inventing novel experiments such as CASPEr and DM Radio to search for axion dark matter.

faculty.name: Eva Silverstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops testable mechanisms for cosmic inflation and addresses fundamental questions in quantum gravity and string theory, including singularity resolution and holographic models of de Sitter expansion.

faculty.name: Stephen Shenker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Contributes foundational results in string theory and M-theory, including the first nonperturbative formulations of string theory and the matrix theory proposal, with recent work on quantum chaos and black holes.

faculty.name: Douglas Stanford
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on quantum gravity, black hole physics in holographic models, and connections between quantum information theory and spacetime geometry, including the dynamics of wormholes and chaotic quantum systems.

faculty.name: Xiaoliang Qi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the interplay of quantum entanglement, quantum gravity, and topological states of matter, using tensor networks and solvable models to understand emergent spacetime geometry from quantum dynamics.

faculty.name: Giorgio Gratta
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads experiments on fundamental particles and their interactions, including landmark neutrino oscillation measurements with KamLAND, searches for neutrinoless double beta decay with liquid xenon detectors, and tests of gravity at sub-millimeter distances.

faculty.name: Patricia Burchat
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies dark matter and dark energy using gravitational lensing with the Vera C. Rubin Observatory (LSST), with a deep background in accelerator-based particle physics including CP violation and charm mixing measurements.

faculty.name: Risa Wechsler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates galaxy formation, large-scale structure, and the nature of dark matter and dark energy using large numerical simulations combined with observational data from DES, DESI, and Rubin Observatory's LSST.

faculty.name: Chao-Lin Kuo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Searches for primordial gravitational waves in the cosmic microwave background through experiments at the South Pole (BICEP), Atacama (Simons Observatory), and in space (LiteBIRD), while developing superconducting detector systems for cosmology.

faculty.name: Peter Michelson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) collaboration as Principal Investigator, studying gamma-ray bursts, pulsars, active galactic nuclei, and searching for dark matter signatures in diffuse gamma-ray emission.

faculty.name: Tom Abel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses ab initio supercomputer simulations to study cosmic history, from the formation of the first stars and galaxies to cosmological reionization, pioneering adaptive-mesh-refinement algorithms and machine learning applications in astrophysics.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Greg Beroza
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Analyzes seismograms using machine learning and data mining to understand earthquake source processes, image shallow crustal structure with ambient seismic fields, and quantify seismic hazards.

faculty.name: Biondo Biondi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops algorithms for 3-D seismic imaging of Earth's subsurface, including wave-equation migration, migration velocity analysis, and distributed fiber-optic sensing for reflection seismology.

faculty.name: Ettore Biondi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Advances distributed fiber-optic sensing technologies to monitor dynamic Earth system processes, using existing telecom fiber cables as dense seismic arrays for earthquake, volcanic, and environmental applications.

faculty.name: Jef Caers
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops decision-making methods under uncertainty for Earth resource exploration, applying AI and geostatistics to critical mineral supply, geothermal energy, and subsurface risk quantification.

faculty.name: Eric Dunham
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses physics-based computational simulations to characterize earthquake rupture dynamics, tsunami generation and propagation, volcanic processes, and ice shelf mechanics.

faculty.name: Anton Ermakov
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the internal structure and evolution of Solar System bodies using spacecraft gravity, topography, and radiometry data combined with geophysical and orbital dynamics modeling.

faculty.name: Simon Klemperer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the structure, tectonics, and composition of the continental lithosphere using active and passive seismology, with major field programs across the Himalaya-Tibet orogen, Basin and Range, and Ethiopian Rift.

faculty.name: Rosemary Knight
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneered the field of hydrogeophysics, using near-surface geophysical methods including airborne electromagnetics and nuclear magnetic resonance to image groundwater systems and inform water resource management.

faculty.name: Alexandra Konings
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies interactions between the global carbon and water cycles using satellite remote sensing, particularly microwave sensing of vegetation water content and plant hydraulic processes.

faculty.name: Ching-Yao Lai
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates mathematical and machine-learned models with observational data to study ice dynamics in Antarctica and Greenland, advancing fundamental understanding of ice sheet flow under climate change.

faculty.name: Mathieu Lapôtre
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the physics of sedimentary and geomorphic processes that shape planetary surfaces, with applications to paleoenvironmental reconstructions on Earth and Mars using rover and orbital data.

faculty.name: Dustin Schroeder
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Advances ice-penetrating radar methods to observe and understand the interaction of ice and water across the solar system, from rapidly changing ice sheets to subsurface exploration of Europa and other icy moons.

faculty.name: Paul Segall
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies earthquake and volcano deformation using GPS, InSAR, and strainmeter data combined with physics-based models to understand fault mechanics, slow slip events, and induced seismicity.

faculty.name: Jenny Suckale
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops customized mathematical models of natural hazards — including volcanic eruptions, ice-sheet instability, and induced seismicity — tested against observational data for disaster risk reduction.

faculty.name: Sonia Tikoo-Schantz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses paleomagnetism and rock magnetism to investigate dynamo magnetic fields and thermal evolution of planetary bodies, and to understand impact cratering processes on the Earth, Moon, and asteroids.

faculty.name: Laura Schaefer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Models atmosphere-interior exchange on rocky planets, studying the evolution of planetary atmospheres from magma ocean outgassing through long-term volatile cycles, and assessing conditions for habitability on exoplanets.

faculty.name: Da Yang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric convection, cloud organization, and climate dynamics using theory, observations, numerical models, and machine learning to understand extreme weather and climate processes.

faculty.name: Howard Zebker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneered spaceborne radar interferometry (InSAR) for measuring millimeter-scale surface deformation over wide areas, with applications to earthquake strain, volcanic inflation, ice sheet mass balance, and groundwater subsidence.

requirements.admission_model: Biosciences umbrella / Home Program with lab rotations

funding.note: Fully funded (stipend, tuition, health insurance)

faculty.name: Joseph (Jody) Puglisi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the role of RNA in cellular processes and disease, focusing on the mechanism of translation and RNA structure/dynamics using biophysical tools such as NMR and cryo-EM.

faculty.name: K. Christopher Garcia
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structural and functional basis of receptor/ligand interactions relevant to human health, focusing on immune system receptors, host-pathogen interactions, and protein engineering for therapeutic development.

faculty.name: Soichi Wakatsuki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the structural biology of ubiquitin signaling, protein self-assembly, and vesicle transport using X-ray crystallography, cryo-EM, and integrative multi-scale bioimaging approaches.

faculty.name: William Greenleaf
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops high-throughput sequencing and microscopy tools to understand how the physical state of the genome governs gene regulation and cellular identity, working at the intersection of genomics, epigenomics, and biophysics.

faculty.name: Rhiju Das
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods to predict and design RNA structure and function, combining high-throughput experiments with the crowdsourced Eterna platform to solve RNA folding and design problems.

faculty.name: Daniel Fisher
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies theoretical physics approaches to understand evolutionary and ecological dynamics, including microbial diversity, cancer evolution, and the physical principles governing population-level biological phenomena.

faculty.name: Kerwyn Casey (KC) Huang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses analytical, computational, and experimental approaches to understand how physical principles govern bacterial cell shape, division, and organization, with a focus on membrane tension and cell-wall synthesis.

faculty.name: Judith Frydman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how proteins fold in living cells, focusing on molecular chaperones and protein quality control networks, and how their dysfunction contributes to cancer and neurodegenerative diseases.

faculty.name: Daniel Herschlag
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the fundamental behavior of RNA and proteins, focusing on enzyme catalysis, RNA folding, and RNA-protein interactions to understand the physical and chemical principles underlying biological function.

faculty.name: Axel Brunger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the molecular mechanisms of synaptic neurotransmission using structural biology and biophysics, focusing on the synaptic proteins that mediate membrane fusion and nerve cell communication.

faculty.name: Lynette Cegelski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops interdisciplinary chemical biology and solid-state NMR approaches to study bacterial biofilms and cell-wall architecture, with the goal of discovering new antibiotics and understanding microbial community structure.

faculty.name: Polly Fordyce
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops microfluidic platforms for high-throughput quantitative biophysics and single-cell genomics, enabling large-scale measurement of kinetic and thermodynamic constants governing molecular interactions and cellular function.

faculty.name: Zev Bryant
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the mechanochemistry of molecular motors using single-molecule biophysics techniques, investigating how nanoscale biological machines convert chemical energy into mechanical work with a focus on torque generation and unconventional myosins.

faculty.name: Alice Ting
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops and disseminates molecular technologies such as proximity labeling (APEX, TurboID) for mapping protein interactions and signaling networks in living cells and neurons.

faculty.name: Benjamin Good
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies statistical physics and population genetics to understand microbial evolutionary dynamics, developing theoretical principles to forecast and control evolution in microbial communities including the gut microbiome.

faculty.summary: Develops nanomaterial-based bioelectronic interfaces and stimuli-responsive systems for sensing, tissue interfaces and drug delivery.

faculty.name: Roger Tobin
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist studying surface physics and the vibrational and electronic properties of adsorbates and materials.

faculty.name: Arthur Utz
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Studies gas-surface reaction dynamics and the role of molecular energy in surface chemical reactions using molecular beams.

faculty.name: Robert White
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops MEMS sensors and transducers, including acoustic and tactile microsystems.

faculty.name: Qiaobing Xu
faculty.title: Professor of Biomedical Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops lipid and nanomaterial-based systems for drug, protein and gene delivery.

faculty.name: Hyunmin Yi
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemical and Biological Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops biofabrication approaches using biopolymers such as chitosan for biosensing and materials assembly.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Carlos Bustamante
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Carlos Bustamante is a biophysicist who uses single-molecule approaches to study the mechanics of DNA, RNA, and proteins. He is best know for pioneering optical tweezers experiments that measure the forces driving molecular motors as well as the forces required the study of unfolding and refolding of individual proteins and RNAs.

faculty.name: Ahmet Yildiz
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Research Interests

faculty.name: Jennifer Doudna
faculty.title: Nobel Laureate in Chemistry; Professor; Li Ka Shing Chancellor’s Chair in Biomedical and Health Sciences, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Jennifer Doudna is a Nobel Laureate in Chemistry, and a Professor of Biochemistry, Biophysics and Structural Biology. Her research focuses on RNA as it forms a variety of complex globular structures, some of which function like enzymes or form functional complexes with proteins.

faculty.name: Eva Nogales
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Our lab is dedicated to gaining mechanistic insight into crucial molecular processes in the life of the eukaryotic cell.

faculty.name: Susan Marqusee
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: The long-term goal of our research is to understand the structural and dynamic information encoded in the linear sequence of amino acids. Proteins undergo an incredible transformation from one-dimensional sequence information into complex three-dimensional shapes that carry out intricate cellular functions.

faculty.name: James Hurley
faculty.title: Professor; Kirsch Springer Chair in Biological Sciences, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: The interplay between proteins and membrane lipids is central to almost every aspect of cell biology.

faculty.name: Jay Groves
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Living cells are not at all well-mixed reaction chambers. Rather, the molecular processes of life occur in dynamic spatial patterns and often operate far from equilibrium.

faculty.name: Daniel Fletcher
faculty.title: Professor; Purnendu Chatterjee Chair in Engineering Biological Systems, Dept of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Daniel Fletcher joined the bioengineering faculty in 2002 and advanced from Associate Professor to Professor in mid 2010. He is also currently serving as Vice Chair for Bioengineering. His research interests include optical and force microscopy, microfabrication, mechanical properties of cells.

faculty.name: Ehud Isacoff
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Ehud (Udi) Isacoff is a neuroscientist and biophysicist whose research probes the molecular and cellular basis of brain function, with a particular focus on synaptic signaling, sensory perception, and circuit dynamics.

faculty.name: Teresa Head-Gordon
faculty.title: University Endowed Chair in Computational Science, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Teresa Head-Gordon is a theoretical and computational chemist and biophysicist whose research develops and applies molecular modeling techniques to understand water structure, protein dynamics, and biomolecular interactions.

faculty.name: Ke Xu
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Professor Xu and his research group develop advanced microscopy and spectroscopy tools to interrogate biological, chemical, and materials systems at the nanoscale with extraordinary resolution, sensitivity, and functionality.

faculty.name: Jamie Cate
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: The Cate Lab explores how genes are put into action by translation. Translation is the universal process of protein synthesis, in which the ribosome translates the four-letter genetic code in messenger RNA into the twenty-letter code of proteins.

faculty.name: Sanjay Kumar
faculty.title: E. H. and Mary E. Pardee Professor; Director, California Institute for Quantitative Biosciences at UC Berkeley, Dept of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Sanjay Kumar, M.D., Ph.D., is a leading figure in bioengineering whose research focuses on interactions between cells and biomaterials.

faculty.name: Stephen Brohawn
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Sensation of environmental stimuli, thought, learning, memory, and other aspects of neuronal communication, hormone secretion and movement all rely on cellular electrical signals. The Brohawn Lab studies life’s electrical system from a molecular and biophysical perspective.

faculty.name: Hernan Garcia
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Hernan Garcia is an associate professor in the the Department of Molecular & Cell Biology, and the Department of Physics.

faculty.name: Aaron Streets
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Lester John and Lynne Dewar Lloyd Distinguished Professor in the College of Engineering, Dept of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: The Streets lab develops hardware, software, and molecular tools to study how cells interpret their genome.

faculty.name: Markita Landry
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Dept of Chemical & Biomolecular Engineering
faculty.summary: Markita del Carpio Landry is a physicist, neuroscientist, and plant biotechnologist whose research combines nanotechnology, imaging, and neurobiology. She is best known for developing nanosensors that monitor neurotransmitter dynamics in the brain, providing new tools to study dopamine and serotonin in real time.

faculty.name: Nicholas Ingolia
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Nicholas Ingolia is a Professor in the Department of Molecular & Cell Biology.

faculty.name: Xavier Darzacq
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Genetics, Genomics and Development, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Life is motion and biological processes are inherently dynamic. When viewed under a microscope, living cells move, change shape, grow and divide.

faculty.name: Eunyong Park
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Eunyong Park is an associate professor in the Department of Molecular & Cell Biology. His research group studies the biogenesis and homeostasis of intracellular organelles, such as endoplasmic reticulum and mitochondria.

faculty.name: Evan Miller
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Research in the Miller lab operates at the interface of molecular and cell biology, synthetic chemistry, and neuroscience. They seek to exploit expertise in synthetic chemistry, probe design, imaging, molecular biology, and electrophysiology to create and deploy molecular tools for mapping brain activity.

faculty.name: Karthik Shekhar
faculty.title: John F. Heil Junior Assistant Professor, Dept of Chemical & Biomolecular Engineering
faculty.summary: Karthik Shekhar is a chemical engineer and computational biologist whose research is at the interface of neuroscience and biophysics. He is best known for developing single-cell transcriptomic methods to map cellular heterogeneity in complex tissues, including the brain and retina. Current projects are focused on two broad themes.

faculty.name: Bronwyn Lucas
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Bronwyn Lucas is an assistant professor of biochemistry, biophysics and structural biology in the Department of Molecular and Cell Biology.

faculty.name: Margaux Pinney
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Margaux Pinney is a biochemist whose research harnesses and refines high-throughput enzymology technologies to probe fundamental questions about protein function.

faculty.name: Robert Saxton
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Robert Saxton is an Assistant Professor of Molecular and Cell Biology and Chemistry studying molecular mechanisms of immune cell signaling in the context of tissue inflammation and repair.

faculty.name: Ahmad Omar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Materials Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Ahmad Omar is an assistant professor in the Department of Material Science and Engineering. The Omar Group's research aims to advance our theoretical understanding of both natural and synthetic soft condensed matter systems.

faculty.name: Ting Xu
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Researches in Xu's group take advantage of the recent developments in de novo protein design and peptidomimetics, polymer science and nanoparticles synthesis and manipulation, and use designed peptides and proteins in concert with the self-assembly of block copolymers, conjugated molecule and nanoparticles as platforms to generate...

faculty.name: Yvette Fisher
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Neuroscience
faculty.summary: Yvette Fisher is an assistant professor of Neurobiology. The goal of her research is to use navigational circuitry to understand how neural circuits perform different computations under different conditions. Her research group explores this question in the fruit fly, Drosophila melanogaster.

faculty.name: Jun-Chau Chien
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Division of Electrical Engineering (EECS)
faculty.summary: Jun-Chau Chien is an Assistant Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science. His research focuses on analog/mixed-signal integrated circuits, bioelectronics, and RF/mmWave high-speed ICs.

faculty.name: Charles Whittaker
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, School of Public Health
faculty.summary: Charles Whittaker is an Assistant Professor of Infectious Diseases and Vaccinology at UC Berkeley who uses advanced analytical approaches to study the transmission dynamics and control of pathogens with pandemic potential.

faculty.name: Max Staller
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: The Staller Lab combines experiments, computation and theory to study gene regulation. The group is initially focused on how transcription factors activate gene expression in yeast and human cells. They use high throughput methods and machine learning to study the relationship between protein sequence and function.

faculty.name: Ashley Wolf
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, School of Public Health
faculty.summary: Ashley Wolf is an Assistant Professor of Infectious Diseases and Vaccinology at the School of Public Health with a joint appointment in the Center for Computational Biology.

faculty.name: Greg Tikhomirov
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Division of Computer Science (EECS)
faculty.summary: Greg Tikhomirov is an assistant professor in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences, with a background in chemistry, bioengineering, medicine, and nanotechnology.

faculty.name: Holger Müller
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Physics
faculty.summary: Precision measurements using lasers and cold atoms, as well as using ultra-intense continuous-wave lasers to develop new molecular traps and contrast enhancement in transmission electron microscopy.

faculty.name: David Limmer
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: David Limmer is a Professor in the Department of Chemistry at University of California Berkeley, a Research Scientist in the Materials and Chemical Sciences Divisions of Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, and a Fellow of the Kavli Energy NanoSciences Institute. From 2016-2018 he held the Chevron Chair in Chemistry at UC Berkeley.

faculty.name: Gary Karpen
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Gary Karpen is a professor of Cell and Developmental Physiology in the Department of Molecular and Cell Biology, and a Senior Scientist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory.

faculty.name: Rachel Brem
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Dept of Plant and Microbial Biology
faculty.summary: Over the four billion years that life has evolved on this planet, organisms have acquired amazing phenotypes. Some, like the spots on a butterfly wing, might capture our attention by their sheer beauty. Others get us excited in a very different way—by their translational relevance.

faculty.name: Derfogail Delcassian
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Derfogail Delcassian is an assistant professor in the Department of Bioengineering. She brings a new research area to the Bioengineering Department– precisely designed chemical tools in combination with 3D printing and materials– to controllably deliver signals which direct immune cell behavior.

faculty.name: Michael Yartsev
faculty.title: Associate Professor , Dept of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Michael Yartsev is a Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) investigator and associate professor in the Departments of Bioengineering and Neuroscience. His lab seeks to understand the neural basis of natural spatial, social and acoustic behaviors in mammals.

faculty.name: Frederic Theunissen
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Integrative Biology
faculty.summary: The long-term goal of our research is to understand the neural mechanisms underlying the recognition of complex sounds such as speech or animal communications.

faculty.name: Ian Swinburne
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: The Swinburne Lab studies how tissues, cells, and subcellular dynamics coordinate to generate organs and initiate physiologies. The lab’s current goal is to understand the mechanisms of hydraulic control within vertebrate organs.

faculty.name: Peter Sudmant
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Integrative Biology
faculty.summary: The Sudmant Lab uses computational, statistical, and experimental methods to interrogate genetic and molecular phenotypic diversity at both the organismal and cellular level. Our lab studies the evolution of aging, and the evolution of genome structure.

faculty.name: Kranthi Mandadapu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Chemical & Biomolecular Engineering
faculty.summary: Kranthi K. Mandadapu is an associate professor and a theorist in the Department of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering (College of Chemistry) at UC Berkeley. His lab is broadly interested in understanding the physical behaviors of systems at multiple length and time scales.

faculty.name: Richard Kramer
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Neuroscience
faculty.summary: Optical control of signaling molecules to understand neuronal function and treat neurodegenerative disease. The nervous system uses action potentials to send signals over long distances within a neuron and synaptic transmission to send signal across the tiny gap between neurons.

faculty.name: Na Ji
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Neuroscience
faculty.summary: Na Ji develops next-generation optical microscopy methods for understanding the brain at higher resolution, greater depth, and faster time scales.

faculty.name: Jack Gallant
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Neuroscience
faculty.summary: Jack Gallant is a cognitive neuroscientist whose research investigates how the brain represents and processes naturalistic information. He is best known for developing computational models that decode brain activity to reveal how sensory and cognitive information is encoded in the cerbral cortex.

faculty.name: Marla Feller
faculty.title: Paul Licht Distinguished Professor in Biological Sciences, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Marla Feller is the Paul Licht Distinguished Professor in Biological Sciences.

faculty.name: Daniel Feldman
faculty.title: Professor; Coates Family Endowed Chair in Neuroscience, Dept of Neuroscience
faculty.summary: Dan Feldman is a neuroscientist whose research focuses on sensory processing, synaptic plasticity, and neural circuit function in cerebral cortex.

faculty.name: Michael DeWeese
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Neuroscience
faculty.summary: Michael DeWeese is a professor in the Department of Physics and the Department of Neuroscience. His group’s research spans three broad areas: nonequilibrium statistical mechanics, machine learning theory, and systems neuroscience.

faculty.name: Eric Betzig
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Eric Betzig is a Professor of Molecular and Cell biology, a Senior Fellow at the Janelia Research Campus, and an Investigator of the Howard Hughes Medical Institute at the University of California, Berkeley. His Ph.D.

faculty.name: Hillel Adesnik
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Dept of Neuroscience
faculty.summary: Hillel Adesnik is a neuroscientist whose research focuses on sensory processing, cortical circuits, and neural computation. He investigates how ensembles of neurons in the brain’s cortex encode perception and integrate sensory information.

faculty.name: Roberto Zoncu
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Roberto Zoncu is a Professor in the Molecular Therapeutics Division of the Department of Molecular & Cell Biology. His research focuses on the following questions.

faculty.name: Denis Titov
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: The Denis Titov lab uses computational and experimental approaches to work on quantitative understanding of human metabolic pathway regulation and deciphering the molecular basis of again.

faculty.name: David Savage
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Dave is a Professor in the Department of Molecular & Cell Biology at the University of California, Berkeley and an Investigator in the Howard Hughes Medical Institute. Dave was born and raised in rural Iowa. He continues to help manage his family’s farm, which was recognized in 2010 as an Iowa Heritage Farm.

faculty.name: Dan Rokhsar
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: My research is focused on understanding the origin, evolution, and diversity of animals (and, more generally, other groups of eukaryotes) by combining computational genome analysis with comparative developmental biology

faculty.name: Richard Mathies
faculty.title: Professor of the Graduate School, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Richard Mathies has developed a variety of modern laser-based spectroscopic techniques to study excited-state reaction dynamics in photoactive proteins and to develop novel microfabricated chemical and biochemical analysis devices for terrestrial and extra-terrestrial applications.

faculty.name: Priya Moorjani
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Priya Moorjani is an assistant professor in the Department of Molecular & Cell Biology. Her lab focuses on using statistical and computational approaches to study questions in human genetics and evolutionary biology.

faculty.name: Barbara Meyer
faculty.title: Professor; HHMI Investigator, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Barbara Meyer is a professor in the Department of Molecular and Cell Biology.

faculty.name: Evan Williams
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Professor Williams' research group is developing and applying novel instrumental and computational techniques in mass spectrometry, tandem mass spectrometry, separations, and laser spectroscopy to solve problems of fundamental interest in chemistry and biochemistry.

faculty.name: Andreas Martin
faculty.title: Professor; HHMI Investigator, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Andy Martin is a Howard Hughes Medical Institute Investigator and Professor of Biochemistry, Biophysics and Structural Biology. His research interests:

faculty.name: Steven Brenner
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: The Brenner research lab has four key research interests involving computational and experimental genomics. Gene regulation by alternative splicing and nonsense-mediated mRNA decay.

faculty.name: Oskar Hallatschek
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Dept of Physics
faculty.summary: The complexity of the biological world demonstrates that chance can produce powerful results since evolution is ultimately driven by random mutational events.

faculty.name: Michael Eisen
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: My lab is dedicated to understanding at a detailed molecular level how transcriptional enhancers generate the spatial and temporal patterns of gene expression that choreograph animal development.

faculty.name: Mohammad Mofrad
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Prof. Mohammad Reza Kaazempur Mofrad is a Professor of Bioengineering and Mechanical Engineering at UC Berkeley, where he leads a multidisciplinary research program at the interface of biomechanics, mechanobiology, artificial intelligence, and computational biology.

faculty.name: David Drubin
faculty.title: Professor; Ernette Comby Chair in Microbiology, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: Our research aims to elucidate the molecular mechanisms that underlie cellular morphogenesis and plasma membrane dynamics in mammalian cells and in budding yeast.

faculty.name: Abby Dernburg
faculty.title: Professor, Dept of Molecular & Cell Biology
faculty.summary: The Dernburg Lab investigates the physical organization of genetic information within chromosomes and genomes.

faculty.name: Adam Arkin
faculty.title: Professor; Dean A. Richard Newton Memorial Professorship, Dept of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: The Arkin Laboratory integrates systems and synthetic biology with biological data science to discover, predict, and engineer the behavior of microbes, viruses, and complex biological communities.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Mina Aganagic
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics and Physics, Chern-Simons Distinguished Chair in Mathematical Physics
faculty.summary: Mina Aganagic received her BS (1995) and PhD (1999) degrees from California Institute of Technology. From 1999-2003 she had a postdoctoral appointment at Harvard University. She was an Assistant Professor of Physics and an Adjunct Professor of Mathematics at the University of Washington, Seattle from 2003-4.

faculty.name: Ehud Altman
faculty.title: Professor
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Professor Ehud Altman received his Ph.D. from the Technion, Haifa in 2002. He was then a postdoctoral fellow at Harvard university for three years before joining the faculty of the Weizmann Institute of Science as a Yigal Alon fellow in 2005. He was promoted to the rank of Associate professor there in 2011.

faculty.name: James Analytis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: James Analytis joined the faculty in January 2013 as the Charles Kittel Chair in condensed matter physics, and served as Department Chair from 2020-2023. He received his B.Sc. in physics from Canterbury University in 2001 and his D. Phil. from the University of Oxford as a Rhodes' Scholar in 2006.

faculty.name: Stuart Bale
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Stuart D. Bale received B.A. and Ph.D. degrees from the University of Minnesota in 1989 and 1994, respectively. After three years of postdoctoral work at Queen Mary College, University of London, he came to a research position at the Space Sciences Laboratory (SSL) at Berkeley.

faculty.name: Raphael Bousso
faculty.title: Professor and The Chancellor's Chair in Physics
faculty.summary: Raphael Bousso received his Ph.D. from Cambridge University in 1998 and went on to become a Postdoc at Stanford University. He also worked at the Kavli Institute for Theoretical Physics in Santa Barbara. In 2002/03 he was a fellow at the Harvard University physics department and the Radcliffe Institute for Advanced Study.

faculty.name: Raúl Briceño
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Raúl Briceño was born and raised in Caracas, Venezuela. In 2013, he received his PhD in Physics from the University of Washington. He then joined the Center for Theoretical and Computational Physics at Jefferson Lab as a postdoc. After three years as a postdoc, in 2016 he became the Nathan Isgur Research Fellow at Jefferson Lab.

faculty.name: Carlos Bustamante
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Carlos Bustamante received his B.S. in 1973 from Universidad Peruana Cayetano Heredia; M.S. in 1975 from Universidad Nacional Mayor de San Marcos; Ph.D. in 1981 from the University of California at Berkeley; Fellow of the American Physical Society since 1996; Physics Department faculty member since 1998.

faculty.name: Yuan Cao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Yuan Cao is an Assistant Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, and Physics. He obtained bachelor's degree at University of Science and Technology of China in 2014, and master's degree and Ph.D. at Massachusetts Institute of Technology in 2016 and 2020 respectively.

faculty.name: Michael Crommie
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Mike Crommie received a B.S. degree in physics from UCLA in 1984 and his Ph.D. from UC Berkeley in 1991. He was a postdoc at IBM Almaden for two years before becoming an Assistant Professor in the Physics Dept. at Boston University in 1994. He moved his laboratory to the UC Berkeley Physics Dept.

faculty.name: Michael DeWeese
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Michael DeWeese received his BA (1988) in physics from UC Santa Cruz and his PhD (1995) in physics from Princeton. From 1995-1999 he took a computational postdoctoral appointment at the Salk Institute in La Jolla, California, with a fellowship from the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation.

faculty.name: Naomi Ginsberg
faculty.title: Professor
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Naomi S. Ginsberg is an Associate Professor of Chemistry and Physics at the University of California, Berkeley, and a Faculty Scientist in the Materials Sciences and Molecular Biophysics and Integrated Imaging Divisions at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, where she has been since 2010.

faculty.name: Heather Gray
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Heather attended the University of Cape Town in South Africa from 1999-2005 where she obtained a BSc, BSc (Hons) and then a MSc in Physics. She then attended the California Institute of Technology and obtained her Ph.D. in Physics in 2011.

faculty.name: Hartmut Haeffner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Hartmut Häffner received his PhD in physics from the University of Mainz / Germany in 2000. After short periods as a Postdoctoral fellow in Mainz and Bangalore/India, he received a Feodor-Lynen fellowship from the Alexander-von-Humboldt foundation Germany and went to NIST / Gaithersburg as a guest researcher (2000-2001).

faculty.name: William Holzapfel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: I was born March 15, 1965 in Pittsburgh PA. After surviving an early program of experimentation with motorcycles and explosives, I went on to receive a B.A. in physics from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute in 1987. My graduate studies were completed in 1996 with a Ph.D. in physics from Berkeley.

faculty.name: Petr Horava
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Petr Horava received his Ph.D. in 1991 at the Institute of Physics of the Czech Academy of Sciences in Prague.

faculty.name: Luca Victor Iliesiu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Iliesiu is a high-energy theorist with a broad set of interests in quantum field theory, quantum gravity, and their rela
tags: Iliesiu is a high-energy theorist with a broad set of intere
faculty.summary: Luca Iliesiu received his BA in Physics from Princeton University in 2015. He remained there for his PhD which he received in 2020.

faculty.name: Barbara Jacak
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: After an international search, Barbara Jacak of the State University New York, Stony Brook, has been named as Berkeley Lab’s new director of the Nuclear Science Division. Jacak has also accepted a joint appointment as Faculty Senior Scientist at Berkeley Lab and Professor of Physics at UC Berkeley.

faculty.name: Na Ji
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Na Ji received her B.S. in Chemical Physics from the University of Science

faculty.name: Shimon Kolkowitz
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Herst Chair in Physics
faculty.summary: Shimon Kolkowitz is an atomic physicist and quantum scientist, with his experimental research focusing on quantum sensing, precision measurement, and metrology.

faculty.name: Yury Kolomensky
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Yury G. Kolomensky joined the Physics Department faculty in July 2000. He received a B.S. in Physics at St. Petersburg Technical University and M.S. in Physics at the University of Massachusetts at Amherst. He completed his Ph.D.

faculty.name: Alessandra Lanzara
faculty.title: Professor, Charles Kittel Chair in Physics
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Alessandra Lanzara received her PhD in physics from Universita’ di Roma La Sapienza, Italy in 1999. She was a postdoc at Stanford University for three years since 1999. In 2002 she joined the physics Department faculty at UC Berkeley as Assistant Professor and since 2011 she is a Full Professor.

faculty.name: Harry Levine
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Harry Levine is an experimental physicist working in quantum science with neutral atom and superconducting qubit systems. His research interests include quantum computing, quantum error correction, atomic physics, many-body physics, and quantum sensing.

faculty.name: Chung-Pei Ma
faculty.title: Judy Chandler Webb Professor of Astronomy, Physics
faculty.summary: C-P Ma received both her undergraduate and Ph.D. degrees in physics from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology.

faculty.name: Raffaella Margutti
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Marc and Cristina Bensadoun Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Raffaella Margutti received her undergraduate degree in Astrophysics in 2006 (magna cum laude), and her PhD in Physics and Astronomy from the University of Milano Bicocca in 2010.

faculty.name: Benjamin Safdi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Benjamin Safdi received his undergraduate degree from the University of Colorado at Boulder, a Master of Advanced Study from Cambridge University, as a Churchill Scholar, and his PhD from Princeton University in 2014.

faculty.name: Eric Betzig
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Eric Betzig is a Professor of Molecular and Cell biology, the Eugene D. Commins Presidential Chair in Experimental Physics, a Senior Fellow at the Janelia Research Campus, and an Investigator of the Howard Hughes Medical Institute at the University of California, Berkeley. His Ph.D. thesis at Cornell University and subsequent work at AT

faculty.name: Gabriel Orebi Gann
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Gabriel attended the University of Cambridge in the UK from 2000 to 2004, where she received her BA and MSci in Natural Sciences. She went on to the University of Oxford, and was awarded her DPhil in Particle and Nuclear Physics in 2008.

faculty.name: Ori Ganor
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Ori Ganor received his B.Sc. in 1988 and his Ph.D. in 1996, both from Tel-Aviv University. He was a Robert H. Dicke fellow from 1996 until 1998 and an assistant professor from 1998 until 2001 at Princeton University. He joined the UC Berkeley Physics faculty as an associate professor in 2002.

faculty.name: Hernan Garcia
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biophysics, Genetics, Genomics and Developmental Biology
faculty.summary: Hernan G. Garcia obtained a Bachelor’s Degree in Physics from the University of Buenos Aires, Argentina in 2003. He then moved to Caltech where he obtained a PhD in Physics in 2011 working in the laboratory of Rob Phillips.

faculty.name: Oskar Hallatschek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research: Biophysics Theory and Experiments Research Interests The complexity of the biological world demonstrates that chance can produce powerful results since evolution is ultimately driven by random mutational events.

faculty.name: Frances Hellman
faculty.title: Professor of the Graduate School
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Frances Hellman received her BA in Physics from Dartmouth College in 1978, graduating summa cum laude and phi beta kappa with high honors in physics. She received her PhD in Applied Physics from Stanford University in 1985, studying what were then considered the high Tc superconductors (the A15's).

faculty.name: Steven Kahn
faculty.title: Dean of Mathematical & Physical Sciences, Professor of Physics, Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Steven M. Kahn is a Professor of Physics and Astronomy. He previously served on the faculty in these departments from 1984-98, but returned to Berkeley in 2022 to take up the position of Dean of Mathematical and Physical Sciences. Kahn received his A.B. (summa cum laude) from Columbia in 1975 and his Ph.D. in physics at Berkeley in 1980.

faculty.name: Dan Kasen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Prof. Kasen received his B.S. from Stanford University and his M.S. and Ph.D. in physics from UC Berkeley. Prior to returning to Cal, he was the Alan C. Davis fellow at Johns Hopkins University and a Hubble fellow at UC Santa Cruz. He joined the Berkeley physics faculty in 2010, jointly appointed with the nuclear science division at LBNL.

faculty.name: Edgar Knobloch
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Edgar Knobloch, Ph.D., Harvard University (1978), Sc.D. University of Cambridge (1994). Faculty member since 1978. Research Interests My research interests center on nonlinear dynamics of dissipative systems.

faculty.name: Adrian Lee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Adrian Lee joined the faculty in July 2000. He received his B.A. in physics from Columbia University in 1986 and his Ph.D. from Stanford University in 1993. At Stanford, he worked with Blas Cabrera on the early development of an experiment to detect non-baryonic dark matter.

faculty.name: Dung-Hai Lee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Professor Dung-Hai Lee received his B.S. degree from the National Tsinghua University of Taiwan. He went to the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in 1977 for graduate studies, and received his Ph.D. in physics in 1982. After staying at M.I.T. for another two years, he joined the IBM T.J. Watson Research Center in 1984.

faculty.name: Stephen Leone
faculty.title: The John R. Thomas Professor in Physics
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Dr. Leone received his B.A. in Chemistry at Northwestern University in 1970 and his Ph.D. in Chemistry at the University of California at Berkeley with Professor C. Bradley Moore in 1974. He was an assistant professor at the University of Southern California from 1974-76.

faculty.name: Eric Y. Ma
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Eric Y. Ma received his B.S. in Physics from Peking University in 2010 and his Ph.D. in Applied Physics from Stanford University in 2016. He stayed at Stanford as a joint postdoctoral scholar in Applied Physics and Electrical Engineering. He was also briefly Senior Scientist at Apple. In July 2021 Dr.

faculty.name: Daniel McKinsey
faculty.title: Professor, The Georgia Lee Distinguished Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Daniel N. McKinsey joined the Physics Department faculty in July 2015. He received a B.S. in Physics with highest honors at the University of Michigan in 1995. His Ph.D. was awarded by Harvard University in 2002, with a thesis on the magnetic trapping, storage, and detection of ultracold neutrons in superfluid helium.

faculty.name: Joel Moore
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
