faculty.summary: Joel Moore joined the physics department faculty as an assistant professor in January 2002. He received his A.B. summa cum laude in physics from Princeton University in 1995 and spent a Fulbright year abroad before graduate studies at MIT and a year at Bell Labs.

faculty.name: Holger Mueller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Holger Müller successfully applied for his first patent when he was 14. Later, he did his undergraduate thesis with Jürgen Mlynek at the University of Konstanz, Germany. He graduated from Humboldt-University, Berlin, with Achim Peters as his advisor.

faculty.name: Hitoshi Murayama
faculty.title: Professor, Former Director, Kavli Institute for the Physics and Mathematics of the Universe (IPMU), University of Tokyo
faculty.summary: Hitoshi Murayama is a well-known theoretical particle physicist who works broadly, even on astrophysics, cosmology, and condensed matter physics.

faculty.name: Kater Murch
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Open Quantum Systems and Non-Hermitian Physics, Quantum Sensing and Metrology, Superconducting Quantum Circuits, Dark Ma
tags: Open Quantum Systems and Non-Hermitian Physics, Quantum Sens
faculty.summary: Kater Murch is Professor of Physics and of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. Kater received his B.A. in physics from Reed College in 2002. After that, he spent a long year slacking off, working as a bee keeper, honing his guitar skills, and studying the cello before finally starting his Ph.D.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Neaton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Jeffrey B. Neaton received his Ph.D. in physics from Cornell University in 2000. After a postdoc at Rutgers University, and after having worked at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory as a postdoc and staff scientist at the Molecular Foundry, he joined the UC Berkeley faculty in 2014.

faculty.name: Yasunori Nomura
faculty.title: Professor, Director, Leinweber Institute for Theoretical Physics
faculty.summary: Yasunori Nomura received his Ph.D. from University of Tokyo in 2000, where he held a fellowship of Japan Society for the Promotion of Science. He was a Miller Research Fellow at University of California, Berkeley from 2000 to 2002, and an Associate Scientist at Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory from 2002 to 2003.

faculty.name: Joseph Orenstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Joseph Orenstein received both B.S. and Ph.D degrees in Physics from MIT. He then joined Bell Labs as a Member of the Technical Staff in 1981 and later as Distinguished MTS.

faculty.name: Geoff Penington
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Geoff Penington received his BA+MMath in Natural Sciences from Cambridge University in 2015, and his PhD in Physics, with Patrick Hayden, from Stanford University in 2020. He joined UC Berkeley in July 2020.

faculty.name: Saul Perlmutter
faculty.title: Franklin W. and Karen Weber Dabby Professor
faculty.summary: Saul Perlmutter is a 2011 Nobel Laureate, sharing the prize in Physics for the discovery of the accelerating expansion of the Universe. He is a professor of physics at the University of California, Berkeley, where he holds the Franklin W. and Karen Weber Dabby Chair, and a senior scientist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory.

faculty.name: Matt C. Pyle
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Michael M. Garland Chair
faculty.summary: Matt Pyle received B.S. in Physics (2001) and B.E. in Aerospace Engineering (2002) from the University of Notre Dame, and a Ph.D. in Physics from Stanford University (2012). Subsequently, he crossed the bay and was a postdoctoral researcher at Berkeley. He joined the Berkeley Physics faculty as the Garland Assistant Professor in 2015.

faculty.name: Zi Qiang Qiu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Zi Qiang Qiu received his BS in 1984 from the physics department of Peking University. He went to the graduate school of the physics department of the Johns Hopkins University via the China-U.S. Physics Examination and Application (CUSPEA) program, and obtained his PhD in 1991.

faculty.name: R. Ramesh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: R. Ramesh received his Ph.D. from UC Berkeley in 1987. APS David Adler Lectureship, 2005; Distinguished University Professor, University of Maryland, College Park, 2003; Fellow, American Physical Society, 2001; A.

faculty.name: Daniel Rokhsar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: My interests center on collective phenomena and ordering in condensed matter and biological systems. In the past, I have worked on high-temperature superconductivity, quantum antiferromagnetism, the fullerenes, and liquid crystals.

faculty.name: Chiara Salemi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Chiara Salemi is an Assistant Professor of Physics at UC Berkeley and a Faculty Scientist in the Physics Division at LBNL. Her research focuses on the search for axions, one of the leading candidates to be the universe’s dark matter.

faculty.name: Uros Seljak
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uros Seljak joined Berkeley as a faculty in 2008, jointly appointed with LBNL. He received his B.S. in 1989 and M.S. in 1991 from Ljubljana University, Slovenia and his PhD in 1995 from MIT. He was a Smithsonian Fellow at Harvard-Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics from 1995 to 1998.

faculty.name: Marjorie Shapiro
faculty.title: Professor of the Graduate School
faculty.summary: Marjorie D. Shapiro received her Ph.D. from the University of California, Berkeley in December 1984. She joined the Physics Department in 1990. She was a Presidential Young Investigator 1989-94 and is a Fellow of the American Physical Society.

faculty.name: Irfan Siddiqi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Irfan Siddiqi received his AB (1997) in chemistry

faculty.name: Alp Sipahigil
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Alp Sipahigil is the Ping

faculty.name: Dan Stamper-Kurn
faculty.title: Professor
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Dan M. Stamper-Kurn came to Berkeley following his studies at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (Ph.D. 2000) and postdoctoral work at the California Institute of Technology (1999 – 2001). He is the recipient of the 2000 APS Division of Atomic, Optical and Molecular Physics Outstanding Thesis award, the Alfred P.

faculty.name: Aziza Suleymanzade
faculty.title: Robert J. Birgeneau Professorship in Physics
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Aziza Suleymanzade is an Assistant Professor, holding the endowed Robert J. Birgeneau Professorship in Physics. Her group explores hybrid quantum systems that combine Rydberg atoms, superconducting circuits, and nanophotonics.

faculty.name: Feng Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Feng Wang received a B.A. from Fudan University, Shanghai, in 1999 and a Ph.D. from Columbia University in 2004. From 2005-2007, he has been a Miller Fellow with Miller Institute for Basic Science at Berkeley. He joined the physics faculty in fall, 2007.

faculty.name: Haichen Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Haichen Wang received a B.S. in physics from Peking University in 2007, and a Ph.D. in physics from the University of Wisconsin-Madison in 2013. His Ph.D. thesis was about the discovery of the Higgs boson using data collected by the ATLAS experiment at CERN's Large Hadron Collider.

faculty.name: Martin White
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Martin White received his B.S. in 1988 from the University of Adelaide and his Ph.D. in 1992 from Yale. After postdoctoral positions at the CfPA in Berkeley and an Enrico Fermi Fellowship in Chicago he became Assistant Professor of Physics and of Astronomy at UIUC.

faculty.name: Michael S. Witherell
faculty.title: Professor and Director Lawrence Berkeley National Lab
faculty.summary: Michael Witherell is Director of Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory and Professor of Physics. Previously he was Vice Chancellor for Research and held the Presidential Chair in Physics at the University of California, Santa Barbara, and before that he was Director of Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory (Fermilab).

faculty.name: Jonathan Wurtele
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Jonathan S. Wurtele earned his undergraduate degrees in Physics and Mathematics, as well as a Ph.D. in Physics, from UC Berkeley. After spending a decade at MIT, he returned to UC Berkeley in 1995, where he is currently a Professor of Physics. His research spans plasma and accelerator theory, and antihydrogen physics. Prof.

faculty.name: Victoria Xu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Victoria Xu received her B.S. in Physics from UC Santa Barbara, and her Ph.D. in Physics from UC Berkeley. For her thesis, she worked with Professor Holger Müller on trapped cavity atom interferometers for precision measurements and fundamental physics.

faculty.name: Ahmet Yildiz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Ahmet Yildiz received his Ph.D. in Biophysics with Paul Selvin at University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign in 2004. After completing his postdoctoral work with Ron Vale at the University of California San Francisco, he joined the Physics Department at the University of California, Berkeley in 2008.

faculty.name: Mike Zaletel
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
tags: Condensed Matter Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Michael Zaletel joined the Department of Physics faculty in July 2018 and is the Thomas and Alison Schneider Chair. An Oakland native, he received his AA from Deep Springs College, his AB with highest honors in physics from Harvard University in 2009, and his Ph.D. at Berkeley working with Professor Joel Moore.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Vladimir Yarov-Yarovoy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Computational Structural Biology / Ion Channel Biophysics
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods (Rosetta-Membrane) for membrane protein structure prediction and design, focusing on voltage-gated ion channels (Nav, Cav, Kv). Designs subtype-specific ion channel modulators for chronic pain and epilepsy treatment.

faculty.name: Eleonora Grandi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops biophysically detailed computational models of cardiac electrophysiology, ion transport, and signaling to understand mechanisms of heart failure, atrial fibrillation, and hypertension. Chair of the Biophysics Graduate Group.

faculty.name: Jon Sack
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates voltage-gated potassium (Kv) and sodium (Nav) channels, developing novel fluorescent probes and toxin-based tools to visualize and manipulate ion channel activity. Studies Kv2/KvS heteromers and their roles in neuronal signaling.

faculty.name: John Voss
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses electron paramagnetic resonance (EPR) spectroscopy to study protein misfolding and aggregation in neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's. Develops spin-labeled compounds that disrupt amyloid-beta aggregation and novel metal-free MRI contrast agents.

faculty.name: Sanjeevi Sivasankar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies cell-cell adhesion at the single-molecule level using atomic force microscopy (AFM), focusing on cadherin proteins critical for tissue integrity. Investigates how mechanical forces regulate cadherin adhesion, with implications for cancer metastasis.

faculty.name: Tonya Kuhl
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Colloidal and Interface Science / Membrane Biophysics
faculty.summary: Studies biological membrane interactions, colloidal self-assembly, and complex fluids using high-resolution surface force measurements. Investigates lipid raft stability, membrane phase behavior, and targeted drug delivery systems. Chair of Chemical Engineering.

faculty.name: Atul N. Parikh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates membrane biophysics and bioengineering, studying lipid phase separation, membrane remodeling under osmotic stress, and synthetic cell construction using giant vesicles. Develops phospholipid-based materials for biosensing and drug delivery.

faculty.name: Justin Siegel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Combines computational tools (Rosetta Molecular Modeling Suite) with experimental validation to design novel enzymes, including those catalyzing reactions not found in nature. Develops therapeutic enzymes for celiac disease and applies AI-driven protein engineering for industrial applications.

faculty.name: Cheemeng Tan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Engineers artificial cellular systems and synthetic cells for biosensors, drug delivery, and understanding biological principles. Develops platforms like VESSEL for engineering extracellular vesicle surface proteins and cyborg cells that are non-replicating but metabolically active.

faculty.name: R. Holland Cheng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses cryo-electron microscopy and tomography to study macromolecular structure and function, with focus on virus assembly and infection mechanisms. Develops protein-based capsids for precision nanomedicine and mucosal delivery of CRISPR gene-editing tools.

faculty.name: Alexei A. Stuchebrukhov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theoretical and computational methods for biological electron and proton transfer, including the foundational Tunneling Currents method. Studies the electron transport chain, redox-driven proton pumps in Complex I and cytochrome c oxidase, and proton migration along membranes.

faculty.name: Patrice Koehl
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods at the interface of computer science, biophysics, and structural biology, including algorithms for protein shape analysis, solvation thermodynamics, and conformational transition path computation (MinActionPath). Models biomolecular electrostatics and solvation for drug design.

faculty.name: Enoch P. Baldwin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies protein-nucleic acid interactions using X-ray crystallography and biochemistry. Investigates CTP synthetase filament formation as a novel regulatory mechanism relevant to anti-cancer drug development, and engineers TALE DNA-binding proteins for programmable DNA recognition.

faculty.name: Jawdat Al-Bassam
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies microtubule cytoskeleton structure and dynamics using cryo-electron microscopy. Mapped the structure and mechanism of tubulin cofactor complexes linked to rare childhood brain disorders (chaperone tubulinopathies), and investigates augmin-mediated microtubule branching and kinesin motor regulation.

faculty.name: Richard J. McKenney
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies molecular motor proteins (kinesins and dyneins) that transport cargo along microtubules, using single-molecule TIRF microscopy and biochemistry. Investigates how motor dysfunction leads to neurodegeneration and cancer, with focus on KIF1A-linked pediatric disorders.

faculty.name: Igor Vorobyov
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses molecular dynamics simulations and enhanced sampling methods to study drug interactions with ion channels and membrane proteins. Leads multi-scale modeling efforts linking atomistic simulations to cardiac cell/tissue models for predicting drug-induced arrhythmia risk.

faculty.name: Stefano Morotti
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops biophysically detailed mathematical models of cardiac and vascular myocyte electrophysiology and calcium signaling. Investigates mechanisms of heart failure and atrial fibrillation, with focus on sex-specific arrhythmia mechanisms and cross-species translational models.

faculty.name: Daisuke Sato
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies mechanisms of cardiac arrhythmias and sudden cardiac death using multiscale modeling and nonlinear dynamics. Investigates calcium cycling in cardiac myocytes, ryanodine receptor dynamics, and mechano-chemo-transduction coupling leading to arrhythmia.

faculty.name: Alla F. Fomina
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates calcium signaling and membrane trafficking in immune cells and neuroendocrine cells. Studies CRAC channels and store-operated calcium entry in T lymphocyte activation, and mechanisms of exocytosis and endocytosis in chromaffin cells.

faculty.name: Jill L. Silverman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses rodent model systems for translational research on neurodevelopmental disorders including Angelman syndrome, autism, and pediatric epilepsy. Pioneered stem cell gene therapy approaches to reverse Angelman syndrome phenotypes, integrating behavioral phenotyping with in vivo electrophysiology.

faculty.name: Leonor Saiz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates computational biophysics, systems biology, and machine learning to study gene regulation and signal transduction networks. Models protein-DNA interactions, DNA looping, and stochastic dynamics of macromolecular assemblies using multiscale approaches from atomistic MD to cellular behavior.

faculty.name: Surl-Hee (Shirley) Ahn
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops and applies enhanced sampling molecular dynamics methods (weighted ensemble) as a computational microscope to study protein dynamics at atomic resolution. Investigates drug-target interactions for pain therapy and proteins implicated in Parkinson's disease.

faculty.name: Jennifer Cash
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structural biology and regulation of Rho guanine nucleotide exchange factors (RhoGEFs) such as P-Rex1/2, which are implicated in cancer metastasis. Uses cryo-EM, X-ray crystallography, and HDX-MS to understand RhoGEF activation and identify small-molecule inhibitors.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Vassilis Angelopoulos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Principal Investigator of NASA's THEMIS and ARTEMIS missions studying particle acceleration in Earth's magnetosphere, magnetic reconnection triggering substorms, and the lunar plasma environment. Leads the Experimental Space Physics group and the ELFIN CubeSat program training undergraduates in space hardware.

faculty.name: Marco Velli
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Observational Scientist for NASA's Parker Solar Probe mission, studying solar wind acceleration, coronal heating, magnetic switchbacks, and heliospheric current sheet dynamics. Fellow of the American Geophysical Union with research spanning MHD turbulence, wave-particle interactions, and space weather.

faculty.name: Jonathan Aurnou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the SPINLab (Simulated Planetary Interiors Laboratory), using rotating tank experiments with liquid gallium and water to study planetary dynamo action, rotating turbulence, and convective transport in planetary cores and atmospheres. Also leads the DIYnamics educational outreach project.

faculty.name: Caroline Beghein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses earthquake and marsquake recordings to map Earth and Mars mantle structure and seismic anisotropy. NASA InSight Participating Scientist who co-detected surface waves from a 4.7 magnitude marsquake and identified a mid-mantle discontinuity on Mars using transdimensional Bayesian inversion methods.

faculty.name: Lars Stixrude
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses first-principles quantum mechanical theory (DFT), thermodynamic modeling, and machine learning potentials to understand planetary interiors from atoms to planets. Co-developed the HeFESTo code for mantle phase equilibria and studies silicate melts, magma oceans, and core formation in Earth and exoplanets. AGU Macelwane Medalist.

faculty.name: Carolina Lithgow-Bertelloni
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the connection between Earth's deep interior dynamics and surface processes including mantle convection, slab subduction, and dynamic topography. Co-developed the HeFESTo thermodynamic model for mantle minerals and demonstrated that many mantle plumes are barely hotter than surrounding mantle. AGU Union Fellow.

faculty.name: Lingsen Meng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on earthquake source imaging, rupture dynamics, and building next-generation earthquake and tsunami early-warning systems by integrating seismic array back-projection, adjoint full-waveform inversion, numerical simulations, and AI techniques. Studies supershear ruptures, earthquake swarms, and slow slip phenomena.

faculty.name: Hao Cao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the origin, dynamics, and impacts of planetary magnetic fields through dynamo theory, spacecraft data analysis, and numerical simulations. Involved in NASA Juno and Europa Clipper missions, with key discoveries on Jupiter's deep interior dynamics and Saturn's complex magnetic field. 2025 Sloan Research Fellow.

faculty.name: David Paige
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Principal Investigator of the Diviner Lunar Radiometer on NASA's Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter, mapping lunar surface temperatures and water ice in permanently shadowed regions since 2009. Also Deputy PI of RIMFAX ground-penetrating radar on Mars Perseverance rover and Co-I on Artemis lunar instruments. NASA Exceptional Scientific Achievement Medal recipient.

faculty.name: Jean-Luc Margot
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses radar and radio astronomy techniques to study planetary interiors, spin states, and surface properties of Venus, asteroids, and icy moons. EnVision Interdisciplinary Scientist for ESA's Venus mission and Europa Clipper gravity science team member. Also conducts radio SETI searches for technosignatures around nearby stars.

faculty.name: Jonathan Mitchell
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric dynamics and climate of terrestrial planets, particularly Titan and Earth, using a hierarchy of climate models combined with satellite observations. Leads the TPACE group exploring transitions between Earth-like and Titan-like climates by varying planetary rotation rate, water vapor, and global water reservoir size.

faculty.name: David Jewitt
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Co-discoverer of the Kuiper Belt (Kavli Prize in Astrophysics 2012, Shaw Prize). Studies comets, Kuiper Belt objects, and active asteroids using ground-based and space telescopes, focusing on the compositional and physical properties of icy bodies and the color systematics of small solar system bodies.

faculty.name: Hilke Schlichting
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theoretical models of planet formation and evolution at the intersection of astrophysics and planetary science. Studies super-Earths and sub-Neptunes, the core-powered mass-loss mechanism sculpting the exoplanet radius valley, magma ocean-atmosphere interactions, and planetary collisions. Asteroid 9522 named in her honor.

faculty.name: Mackenzie Day
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies wind-driven (aeolian) sediment transport and landscape evolution on Earth, Mars, and other planetary bodies through field work, wind tunnel experiments, and numerical modeling. Leads the GALE Lab investigating how dunes record climate history and reconstructing paleo-wind patterns from lithified Martian dunes. AGU Ronald Greeley Early Career Award recipient.

faculty.name: Seulgi Moon
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how tectonics, climate, and bedrock properties control landscape evolution, weathering, and erosion. Published a 2025 Science paper demonstrating that active faults weaken rocks up to ~100 km away, dramatically increasing erosional efficiency. AGU Luna B. Leopold Early Career Award and Sloan Research Fellow. Note: Moving to ETH Zurich as of February 2026 but still listed on UCLA EPSS faculty page.

faculty.name: John He
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Interdisciplinary field geologist studying lithospheric deformation and surface-crust-mantle connections through structural geology, sedimentology, geodynamic modeling, and thermochronology. Investigates lithospheric dripping beneath high-elevation plateaus and recently published a Science paper solving the mystery of the Colorado River's 5-million-year absence from the geological record.

faculty.name: Peng Ni
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental planetary scientist studying planet formation and differentiation through high-pressure/high-temperature experiments and non-traditional stable isotope geochemistry. Investigates planetesimal core formation, lunar evolution, and deep Earth processes using piston cylinder apparatus and synchrotron techniques applied to meteorites and lunar samples.

faculty.name: William Newman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies chaos and complexity in astrophysical and geophysical systems, including relativistic magnetohydrodynamics in astrophysical jets, star and solar system formation, earthquake modeling, and climate change statistics. Holds joint appointments in Physics & Astronomy and Mathematics. Former John Simon Guggenheim Fellow and member of the Institute for Advanced Study.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Michael McNitt-Gray
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops quantitative CT imaging techniques for lung cancer screening and radiation dose estimation, including Monte Carlo simulation methods for CT dosimetry and AI-driven computer-aided diagnosis systems. His lab also contributes to open-source CT reconstruction tools (FreeCT) and imaging biomarker validation for clinical trials.

faculty.name: Magnus Dahlbom
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on nuclear medicine physics with emphasis on PET/CT imaging, theranostics, and radiopharmaceutical therapy dosimetry. His research spans PSMA-targeted molecular radiotherapy for prostate cancer, preclinical small-animal PET systems, and myocardial perfusion quantitation using PET tracers.

faculty.name: Matthew S. Brown
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops quantitative CT imaging biomarkers and AI algorithms for lung diseases including COPD, interstitial lung disease, and idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. His work bridges computer vision, machine learning, and thoracic radiology to translate quantitative imaging tools into clinical practice.

faculty.name: Aichi Chien
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies intracranial aneurysm growth and rupture risk using computational fluid dynamics, MRA, and patient-specific hemodynamic analysis. Her lab also applies AI and vision-language models to cerebrovascular image analysis, including automated aneurysm detection and radiologic report summarization.

faculty.name: Anthony G. Christodoulou
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel MRI techniques through innovations in MR physics, machine learning, and image reconstruction. His signature MR Multitasking framework enables simultaneous multiparametric mapping in a single free-breathing scan for cardiovascular disease and cancer applications.

faculty.name: Benjamin Ellingson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the UCLA Brain Tumor Imaging Laboratory, developing and validating advanced MRI techniques to characterize brain tumors, assess treatment response, and predict patient outcomes. He co-authors consensus RANO criteria and investigates physiologic MRI biomarkers for anti-VEGF and immunotherapy response in gliomas and brain metastases.

faculty.name: Hyun Grace Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on quantitative chest CT imaging and computational modeling of fibrotic lung diseases, particularly idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. Combines medical imaging, statistical learning, and artificial intelligence to develop CT biomarkers that predict disease progression and treatment response.

faculty.name: Kyung Sung
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: MRI Physics / AI in Medical Imaging
faculty.summary: Develops artificial intelligence and quantitative MRI technologies for early cancer diagnosis, treatment guidance, and therapeutic response assessment. His lab integrates deep learning with advanced image reconstruction for multiparametric prostate MRI, placental imaging, and liver perfusion MRI.

faculty.name: M. Albert Thomas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Advances magnetic resonance spectroscopic imaging (MRSI) techniques for noninvasive metabolic assessment of tissues in health and disease. His lab develops novel MRSI methodologies and evaluates their clinical utility across brain, prostate, and other pathologies.

faculty.name: Holden Wu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Creates and translates quantitative MRI technologies for image-guided therapy spanning detection, treatment planning, interventional guidance, and treatment monitoring of cancer and metabolic diseases. His lab develops rapid quantitative MRI for prostate, liver, and placental applications, plus real-time MRI-guided ablation and biopsy techniques.

faculty.name: Daniel Tward
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational tools for neuroimaging and brain anatomy analysis, focusing on image registration and mapping algorithms to process and interpret large-scale brain MRI datasets. His work bridges computational mathematics and clinical neuroscience.

faculty.name: Arion Chatziioannou
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneers the development of high-resolution in vivo molecular imaging technology, particularly microPET and microSPECT scanners for preclinical small-animal research. His work spans detector physics, dual-modality PET/optical imaging, and automated radiochemistry systems for positron-emitting radioisotopes.

faculty.name: Johannes Czernin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads translational research in nuclear medicine and theranostics, with a focus on PSMA-targeted PET/CT imaging and 177Lu-PSMA radioligand therapy for prostate cancer. His work spans preclinical radiopharmaceutical development, multi-institutional clinical trials, and health policy advocacy for molecular imaging and radiotherapeutics.

faculty.name: Oluwatayo Ikotun
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops molecular imaging tools for in vivo characterization of immune cells, with expertise in immunoPET and radiotracer development using radionuclides such as zirconium-89. Her research spans cancer biomarker imaging, cardiovascular PET/CT, and advancing AAV gene therapy monitoring for Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

faculty.name: Jennifer Murphy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel 18F-radiolabeling methodologies to create targeted PET imaging tracers for cancer, including brain-penetrant EGFR kinase inhibitors for glioblastoma imaging. Her lab designs synthetic strategies using bioorthogonal chemistry for modular, dual-modality PET/fluorescence molecular imaging agents.

faculty.name: Caius Radu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops PET imaging approaches to monitor T lymphocyte activation in cancer and autoimmunity, bridging molecular imaging with immunology. His research focuses on creating noninvasive tools to track immune cell dynamics for improving cancer immunotherapy.

faculty.name: Michael van Dam
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops microfluidic technologies for cancer research and molecular imaging, focusing on miniaturized and automated platforms for PET radiopharmaceutical production. His lab integrates microfluidics, radiochemistry, and engineering to enable rapid, low-cost synthesis of molecular imaging probes.

faculty.name: Nzhde Agazaryan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Serves as Chief of Clinical Medical Physics in Radiation Oncology, specializing in stereotactic radiosurgery, SBRT, small field dosimetry, IGRT, and gated radiotherapy. His research focuses on technical innovation in treatment delivery accuracy, safety, and quality assurance across brain, spine, prostate, and lung treatments.

faculty.name: Daniel Low
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in radiation oncology medical physics with emphasis on MRI-guided radiation therapy, respiratory motion modeling via 4DCT/5DCT, and stereotactic body radiotherapy. He leads multi-institutional clinical trials including MIRAGE comparing MRI-guided versus CT-guided SBRT for prostate cancer.

faculty.name: James Lamb
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Advances radiation oncology through MRI-guided radiotherapy, automated quality assurance using deep learning, and AI-assisted treatment workflow. A key contributor to the MIRAGE randomized trial, his research spans online adaptive radiotherapy, motion management, and brachytherapy for uveal melanoma.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Daniel P. Arovas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on strongly correlated electron systems, quantum magnetism, and quantum fluids and solids.

faculty.name: Richard D. Averitt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist using ultrafast and terahertz spectroscopy to probe and control quantum materials.

faculty.name: Massimiliano Di Ventra
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on quantum transport, nanoscale electronics, and memory/neuromorphic computing.

faculty.name: Ivan K. Schuller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist and director of Q-MEEN-C studying quantum materials, superconductivity, magnetism, and correlated oxides.

faculty.name: Oleg Shpyrko
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist using coherent x-ray scattering and imaging to study nanoscale structure and dynamics in materials.

faculty.name: Tarun Grover
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist studying quantum entanglement, topological phases, and strongly correlated many-body systems.

faculty.name: Yi-Zhuang You
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on quantum many-body physics, topological phases, and machine-learning approaches to correlated systems.

faculty.name: Monica Allen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist building quantum electronic devices from two-dimensional and topological materials.

faculty.name: James G. Branson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist on the CMS experiment at the LHC, including data acquisition and Higgs boson studies.

faculty.name: Benjamin Grinstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist studying weak interactions, rare hadron decays, effective field theory, and dark matter.

faculty.name: Kenneth A. Intriligator
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist specializing in quantum field theory and its formal structure.

faculty.name: John A. McGreevy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on quantum field theory and its applications to quantum many-body and condensed matter systems.

faculty.name: Kaixuan Ni
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads dark matter direct-detection searches with the XENON1T/nT experiments and DARWIN development.

faculty.name: Liang Yang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist searching for neutrinoless double beta decay (EXO-200, nEXO) and dark matter (COSINE-100).

faculty.name: Frank Wurthwein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist on CMS and director of the San Diego Supercomputer Center, working on distributed high-throughput computing.

faculty.name: Javier Duarte
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist on CMS focusing on high-momentum Higgs measurements, exotic searches, and machine learning for triggers and reconstruction.

faculty.name: Terence T-L. Hwa
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies bacterial physiology, genomics, and proteomic resource allocation using quantitative and theoretical approaches.

faculty.name: Suckjoon Jun
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist studying quantitative cell physiology and the physics of bacterial growth and size control.

faculty.name: David Kleinfeld
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Seeks physical principles that explain the design and function of brain-wide systems using experimental neurophysics.

faculty.name: Douglas E. Smith
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist studying viral DNA packaging, chromatin assembly, and single-polymer dynamics at the single-molecule level.

faculty.name: Olga Dudko
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist studying synaptic transmission, viral infection dynamics, and chromatin organization.

faculty.name: Elena Koslover
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist modeling intracellular active transport and organelle dynamics.

faculty.name: Nigel Goldenfeld
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on pattern formation, condensed matter theory, and the physics of living systems.

faculty.name: Brian Keating
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Observational cosmologist studying the cosmic microwave background and its polarization.

faculty.name: Shelley Wright
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on optical and infrared instrumentation, galaxy formation and evolution, and technosignature searches.

faculty.name: George M. Fuller
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on nuclear and particle astrophysics, gravitation, dark matter, and cosmology.

faculty.name: Dusan Keres
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical astrophysicist running cosmological simulations of galaxy formation.

faculty.name: Patrick H. Diamond
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist studying plasma physics, fluids, turbulence, transport, and self-organization.

faculty.name: Tenio Popmintchev
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental AMO physicist working on ultrafast and attosecond laser science and high-harmonic generation.

faculty.name: Tongyan Lin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist focusing on dark matter and early-universe physics.

faculty.name: Mattia Serra
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops data-driven mathematical methods and physical models for nonlinear biological and physical systems, focusing on self-organization in embryonic development, morphogenesis control, active matter, and fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Leonid V. Butov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist studying excitons in semiconductor nanostructures, including exciton coherence, condensation, and transport in coupled quantum wells.

faculty.name: R. Sekhar Chivukula
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical particle physicist working on electroweak symmetry breaking, strong dynamics, and models of physics beyond the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Raphael Flauger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical cosmologist working on inflation, the cosmic microwave background, and connections between string theory and early-universe physics.

faculty.name: Michael M. Fogler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed matter theorist studying electronic properties of low-dimensional systems, graphene and other two-dimensional materials, excitons, and polaritons.

faculty.name: Alexander Groisman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist developing microfluidic techniques to study soft matter, complex fluids, and biological physics problems.

faculty.name: Jorge E. Hirsch
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed matter theorist known for work on superconductivity, including the theory of hole superconductivity, magnetism, and numerical methods for correlated electrons.

faculty.name: Michael Holst
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Mathematical and computational physicist (joint with Mathematics) working on numerical relativity, geometric analysis, and partial differential equations arising in gravitation.

faculty.name: Vivek A. Sharma
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist on the CMS experiment at the LHC, studying the Higgs boson and searching for physics beyond the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Elizabeth H. Simmons
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical particle physicist studying electroweak symmetry breaking, strong dynamics, and models of physics beyond the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Massimo Vergassola
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist applying statistical physics and fluid dynamics to biological problems, including turbulence, sensing, and search strategies in living systems.

faculty.name: Avraham Yagil
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist on the CMS experiment at the LHC, working on Higgs physics and searches for new phenomena.

faculty.name: Kam Arnold
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Observational cosmologist measuring the cosmic microwave background with experiments such as the Simons Observatory and POLARBEAR to probe inflation and neutrino physics.

faculty.name: Julio T. Barreiro
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental AMO physicist using ultracold atoms in optical lattices to realize and probe topological and strongly correlated quantum states.

faculty.name: Alex Frano
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist using x-ray scattering and spectroscopy to study quantum materials, oxide heterostructures, and correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Daniel Green
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical cosmologist studying inflation, the physics of the early universe, and cosmological signatures of particle physics.

faculty.name: Aobo Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies machine learning and AI to rare-event searches in neutrino and dark matter experiments, including neutrinoless double-beta decay (KamLAND-Zen, LEGEND); jointly appointed with the Halicioglu Data Science Institute.

faculty.name: Seungeun Oh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops label-free optical microscopy tools, including quantitative phase and nonlinear microscopy, to study how single cells regulate their physical properties; jointly appointed with Cellular and Molecular Medicine.

faculty.name: Daniel Parker
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed matter theorist studying quantum many-body dynamics, quantum chaos and operator growth, and the physics of moire and correlated materials.

faculty.name: Tzer Han Tan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental biophysicist studying active matter, self-organization, and morphogenesis in living and reconstituted systems.

faculty.name: Hongbo Zhao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the physics of living and soft matter using theory and computation, including biomolecular condensates, phase separation, and active matter; jointly appointed with Chemistry and Biochemistry.

funding.note: 12-month stipend plus fees and tuition; TA/RA/fellowships

faculty.name: Neville Bethel
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on the de novo design of protein motion, engineering proteins with defined conformational dynamics.

faculty.name: Itay Budin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the biochemistry and synthetic biology of cell membranes.

faculty.name: Michael Burkart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans natural product synthesis and biosynthesis, biological chemistry and enzymology, and metabolic engineering.

faculty.name: Galia Debelouchina
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses NMR spectroscopy and chemical biology to study structural biology problems in aging and gene regulation.

faculty.name: Lalit Deshmukh
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Applies solution NMR spectroscopy to study amyloids, neurodegenerative diseases, post-translational modifications, and viruses.

faculty.name: Neal Devaraj
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Presidential Chair in Biochemistry and Molecular Biophysics; research in biomimetic chemistry, artificial cells, and chemical biology.

faculty.name: Gourisankar Ghosh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Biochemistry and biophysics of transcription, signaling, pre-mRNA splicing, and mRNA transport.

faculty.name: Partho Ghosh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies mechanisms of bacterial and protozoan pathogenesis and the host response against infectious microbes.

faculty.name: Kamil Godula
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Co-Director of the UCSD Glycobiology Research and Training Center; develops nanotechnologies for analyzing glycan function and glycomaterials for stem cell-based tissue regeneration.

faculty.name: Mark Herzik
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Elucidates the mechanisms of macromolecular transport into and within mitochondria using cryo-EM.

faculty.name: Patricia Jennings
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Biophysical chemistry of protein structure, dynamics, and folding using NMR spectroscopy.

faculty.name: Lisa Jones
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses oxidative labeling and mass spectrometry for the structural characterization of proteins in their native cellular environment.

faculty.name: Simpson Joseph
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the molecular mechanism of protein synthesis.

faculty.name: Judy Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Biophysical chemistry using spectroscopic studies of membrane protein folding and dynamics.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Komives
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure, function, dynamics, and thermodynamics of protein-protein interactions using NMR, mass spectrometry, and kinetics.

faculty.name: Alexis Komor
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Deputy Director of the Sanford Stem Cell Innovation Center; research in genome editing, DNA damage and repair, and functional genomics.

faculty.name: Colleen McHugh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies RNA-protein interactions, assembly of RNPs, and the structural biology of multimeric complexes.

faculty.name: Tatiana Mishanina
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies transcription and its regulation, including RNA polymerase structure and function in bacteria and human mitochondria.

faculty.name: Ulrich Muller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the evolution of catalytic RNAs and the origin of life.

faculty.name: Christopher Obara
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in cellular biochemistry, biophysics, and macromolecular structure; joint appointment in Pharmacology.

faculty.name: Johannes Schoeneberg
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Roger Tsien Chancellor's Faculty Fellow; develops 4D adaptive optics lattice light-sheet imaging, petabyte-scale image processing, and machine learning to study mitochondrial dynamics and organoids.

faculty.name: Susan Taylor
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure, function, dynamics, and localization of PKA as a prototype for the protein kinase superfamily; also Distinguished Professor of Pharmacology.

faculty.name: F. Akif Tezcan
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry and Biochemistry
faculty.summary: Works on bioinorganic and biophysical chemistry, protein structure/function/evolution, and biomaterials.

faculty.name: Navtej Toor
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure and function of introns and retroelements.

faculty.name: Brian Zid
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies gene expression control during stress and mRNA localization to membrane-less compartments.

faculty.name: Daniel Donoghue
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Studies receptor tyrosine kinases in human cancer.

faculty.name: Fleur Ferguson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor jointly appointed in Chemistry and Biochemistry and the Skaggs School of Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences; specific research details not stated on the program faculty page.

faculty.name: Alex Guseman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of Chemistry and Biochemistry; specific research details not stated on the program faculty page.

faculty.name: Christine Hrycyna
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Chemistry and Biochemistry and Dean of the School of Physical Sciences (joined UCSD in 2023); a biochemist whose work has focused on membrane transport proteins and multidrug resistance.

faculty.name: Adrian Jinich
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Runs a mixed computational and experimental group developing machine-learning methods to predict enzyme function from sequence and uncover uncharacterized enzymes.

faculty.name: Julia Stauber
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in inorganic synthesis, organometallic synthesis, and bioinorganic chemistry.

faculty.name: Akif Tezcan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in bioinorganic and biophysical chemistry, materials and biomaterials chemistry, and protein structure, function, and evolution.

faculty.name: Wei Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in epigenomics, cellular reprogramming, protein recognition, computational biology, and systems biology; also Professor of Cellular and Molecular Medicine.

faculty.name: Jerry Yang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in bioorganic chemistry, molecular self-assembly, molecular synthesis, materials chemistry, and bionanotechnology; joint appointment in Materials Science and Engineering.

faculty.name: Hongbo Zhao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in computational soft and living matter and biomolecular condensates; joint appointment in the Department of Physics.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Leon Balents
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates quantum magnetism, correlated electron systems, and complex materials. Known for predicting topological insulators and Weyl semimetals, and for advancing the understanding of quantum spin liquids — exotic states of matter with macroscopic quantum entanglement of electronic spin.

faculty.name: Matthew Fisher
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of many-body theory and quantum information theory, with current focus on the non-equilibrium quantum dynamics of open and monitored quantum systems. Previously held positions at IBM, the Kavli Institute, Microsoft Station Q, and Caltech.

faculty.name: Andrea Young
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on quantum electronics in two-dimensional systems, exploring emergent electronic phenomena in van der Waals heterostructures and moiré materials.

faculty.name: Cenke Xu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies quantum many-body systems where the interplay between quantum fluctuation and correlation effects produces diverse phenomena such as quantum criticality, non-Fermi liquids, quantum spin liquids, and topological matter, with applications to transition metal oxides, moiré heterostructures, and cold atom systems.

faculty.name: Sagar Vijay
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of quantum condensed matter physics and quantum information science, focusing on far-from-equilibrium quantum dynamics, universal traits of highly entangled quantum matter, and quantum error correction. Recipient of the Sloan Research Fellowship and the 2023 William L. McMillan Award.

faculty.name: Chenhao Jin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Builds optical spectroscopy and imaging tools to watch quantum phenomena across space and time, exploring 2D materials, moiré superlattices, spin systems, strongly correlated materials, and topological systems, with a focus on the interplay between dimensionality, many-body interaction, fluctuations, and competing orders.

faculty.name: Ania Bleszynski Jayich
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Builds quantum technologies centered on solid-state spins, photons, and phonons to investigate foundational questions in quantum science and condensed matter physics.

faculty.name: Michel Devoret
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates quantum non-equilibrium phenomena in superconducting circuits with applications to quantum information processing, including quantum computation and quantum sensing. Pioneered the field of quantronics, where collective degrees of freedom like currents and voltages display quantum behavior.

faculty.name: John Martinis
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates superconducting devices, focusing on minimizing noise and enhancing sensitivity. His main effort involves building a quantum computer based on Josephson junction quantum bits, and he led the Google quantum supremacy experiment.

faculty.name: David Weld
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Employs ultracold quantum degenerate gases to investigate a broad spectrum of quantum dynamical phenomena, with current interests spanning quantum simulation, quantum thermodynamics, kicked quantum matter, Floquet engineering, quasicrystals, atom interferometry, and anyon synthesis.

faculty.name: David Patterson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Extends the tools of atomic, molecular, and optical physics to prepare polyatomic molecules in single quantum states for the first time. Develops novel spectroscopy methods for ultraprecise chemical analysis, with interests spanning quantum information systems, parity violation in molecular spectra, and single-molecule chiral analysis.

faculty.name: David Berenstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works primarily on quantum field theories and their equivalence to quantum gravity in higher dimensions via the AdS/CFT correspondence, exploring deep connections between gauge theories and gravitational physics.

faculty.name: Nathaniel Craig
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the interface of high energy physics, particle phenomenology, and cosmology, exploring physics beyond the Standard Model through effective field theory methods and collider signatures.

faculty.name: Xi Dong
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Explores the connection between quantum entanglement and emergent spacetime through the holographic duality between string theory and quantum field theories. Also investigates foundational questions in quantum gravity and phenomenological aspects of particle physics and cosmology.

faculty.name: Steven Giddings
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on quantum gravity, quantum field theory, black holes, quantum cosmology, and elementary particles, with a focus on fundamental questions at the intersection of general relativity and quantum mechanics.

faculty.name: David Gross
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Nobel laureate (2004) recognized for the discovery of asymptotic freedom in quantum chromodynamics. His research spans high energy physics, quantum field theory, and string theory. Permanent member of the Kavli Institute for Theoretical Physics.

faculty.name: Gary Horowitz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on classical and quantum gravity, with particular emphasis on black holes and spacetime singularities. Member of the National Academy of Sciences and former President of the International Society on General Relativity and Gravitation.

faculty.name: Donald Marolf
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Explores black holes, gravitational aspects of string theory, and how gravity, entropy, and entanglement intersect. Investigates how bulk physics is encoded in gauge theory quantum states and studies thermal transport, quantum observables in gravity, and the firewall paradox.

faculty.name: Claudio Campagnari
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Conducts direct searches for new physics processes with the CMS experiment at the LHC, with a shift toward searches for exotic signatures such as long-lived new particles and milli-charged particles. Also performs precision Standard Model tests including measurements of rare top quark decays and Higgs boson properties.

faculty.name: Joe Incandela
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Searches for new fundamental particles at high-energy colliders. Involved in the discovery of the top quark in 1995 and the first observation of the Higgs boson in 2012. His group currently works on high-granularity silicon-based calorimeters for the CMS experiment and the LDMX light dark matter experiment.

faculty.name: David Caratelli
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist studying neutrinos to understand the fundamental laws of nature and the evolution of the universe, with work centered on the MicroBooNE and DUNE experiments investigating sterile neutrino oscillations and developing liquid argon time projection chamber technology.

faculty.name: Brendan Bowler
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates planet formation, growth, migration, and atmospheric evolution using observatories like Keck, HST, and JWST. His ultimate objective is to directly characterize terrestrial worlds in the habitable zones of their host stars in search of signs of life.

faculty.name: Benjamin Mazin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops optical/UV/X-ray Microwave Kinetic Inductance Detectors (MKIDs) and astronomical instrumentation, with a current focus on building and using MKID-based instruments for detecting and characterizing nearby exoplanets.

faculty.name: S. Peng Oh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies galaxy formation and evolution along with the physics of diffuse plasmas, cosmic rays, turbulence, and the gaseous environments around galaxies and within galaxy clusters.

faculty.name: Crystal Martin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how galaxies assemble and how the universe transitioned from a neutral to an ionized intergalactic medium, using spectroscopy and imaging to measure properties of stars and gas in and around galaxies, applying concepts from hydrodynamics, radiative transfer, cosmology, and stellar evolution.

faculty.name: Maxwell Millar-Blanchaer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on the direct imaging of exoplanets, brown dwarfs, and debris disks using polarimetry to detect faint objects near bright stars, combining instrument-building, large-telescope observations, and novel data analysis techniques.

faculty.name: Prateek Agrawal
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of particle physics and cosmology, investigating dark matter, early universe physics, and astrophysical probes of new fundamental physics.

faculty.name: Philip Lubin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the early universe through millimeter-wavelength observations and develops applications of directed energy for planetary defense and relativistic propulsion. Co-investigator on the Planck cosmology mission and co-recipient of the 2006 and 2018 Gruber Prizes in Cosmology.

faculty.name: Lars Bildsten
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and Director of the Kavli Institute for Theoretical Physics (KITP), conducting research in theoretical astrophysics and cosmology.

faculty.name: Dirk Bouwmeester
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates quantum information science and quantum decoherence using optomechanical systems such as phononic crystal membranes made of silicon nitride and diamond, and on-chip optical waveguides coupled to superfluid helium.

faculty.name: Mark Bowick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Deputy Director of the KITP who investigates symmetry, symmetry-breaking, geometry, and topology across fields ranging from quantum field theory to soft and living matter, with recent focus on the statistical mechanics of thermalized 2D metamaterials such as graphene and elastic membranes.

faculty.name: Frank Brown
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Soft and Living Matter / Theoretical Biophysics
faculty.summary: Works in the physics of soft and living matter, developing theoretical and computational approaches to biophysical and biochemical systems.

faculty.name: Jean Carlson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies complex systems spanning condensed matter theory and the physics of soft and living matter, applying physics-based modeling to complex and biological systems.

faculty.name: Caitlin Casey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the assembly of the most massive galaxies in the universe using observations from telescopes such as JWST and ALMA to understand how galaxies formed and evolved in the earliest cosmic times, and leads the COSMOS survey that identifies rare, extreme galaxies and examines their large-scale environments.

faculty.name: Zvonimir Dogic
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Experimental / Soft and Living Matter
faculty.summary: Experimental soft matter physicist who reproduces diverse dynamical phenomena of living systems in simplified systems built from a minimal set of building blocks, studying out-of-equilibrium active matter with applications spanning robotics and microfluidics.

faculty.name: Clifford Johnson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Concentrates on quantum gravity, superstring theory, quantum field theory, and particle physics, with particular focus on strongly coupled phenomena.

faculty.name: Joseph Hennawi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in astrophysics and cosmology, studying quasars, the intergalactic medium, and the large-scale structure of the universe through observation and data analysis.

faculty.name: Andreas Ludwig
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on condensed matter theory, studying quantum many-body systems including topological phases of matter, disordered systems, and quantum entanglement.

faculty.name: M. Cristina Marchetti
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Condensed Matter Theory / Soft and Living Matter
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist studying active matter and living systems, investigating how assemblies of energy-consuming entities develop organized behaviors at scales much larger than their individual components, with applications ranging from crowd dynamics to epithelial cell migration in wound healing.

faculty.name: David Morrison
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in mathematical physics and string theory, studying the geometry of string compactifications and connections between algebraic geometry and quantum field theory.

faculty.name: Chetan Nayak
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed matter theorist and Director of Microsoft Station Q, working on topological phases of matter and their application to topological quantum computing.

faculty.name: Harry Nelson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: High Energy Experimental Physics / Dark Matter
faculty.summary: Designs and builds novel particle detectors to search for new physics, focusing for the past two decades on the direct detection of dark matter through underground experiments including CDMS, LUX, and LZ.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Richman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist working in high energy physics on collider-based searches and precision measurements that test the Standard Model and probe for new phenomena.

faculty.name: Cyrus Safinya
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Soft and Living Matter / Biomolecular Materials
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor studying the physics and materials science of biological systems, using soft matter physics and molecular engineering approaches to investigate biomolecular materials.

faculty.name: Joan-Emma Shea
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Soft and Living Matter / Theoretical Biophysics
faculty.summary: Works in the physics of soft and living matter, using theoretical and computational methods to study protein folding, biomolecular self-assembly, and related biophysical systems.

faculty.name: Mark Sherwin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist and Director of the Institute for Terahertz Science and Technology, using the UCSB Free-Electron Lasers to study quantum condensed matter driven by strong periodic fields, including photo-excited quasiparticles and quantum coherence in spin systems.

faculty.name: Boris Shraiman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Soft and Living Matter / Biophysics Theory
faculty.summary: Permanent Member of the KITP working in theoretical biophysics, applying physics-based approaches to problems in the physics of soft and living matter, including quantitative biology and developmental dynamics.

faculty.name: David Stuart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist and Department Chair, working in high energy physics with the CMS experiment at the LHC to search for new particles and test the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Anthony Zee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies field-theoretical methods to strongly correlated quantum systems across particle physics and condensed matter, using concepts such as gauge theory and topological actions to study superconductivity, quantum Hall fluids, and disordered systems, seeking insights that bridge particle and condensed matter physics.

faculty.name: Deborah Fygenson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Soft and Living Matter / Biophysics
faculty.summary: Experimental biophysicist and Director of the Graduate Program working in the physics of soft and living matter, studying biomolecular self-assembly and DNA nanotechnology.

faculty.name: Omar A. Saleh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Soft and Living Matter / Single-Molecule Biophysics
faculty.summary: Conducts experimental research on biopolymer elasticity using single-molecule stretching techniques and studies biomimetic organelles built from reconstituted assemblies of nucleic acids and proteins to understand fundamental principles of biomolecular behavior.

faculty.name: Andrew Jayich
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental AMO physicist working with trapped ions to perform precision measurements and searches for new physics through quantum control of atomic and molecular ions.

faculty.name: Xiao Luo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: High Energy Experimental Physics / Neutrino Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist working in high energy physics with a focus on neutrino physics and accelerator-based neutrino experiments.

faculty.name: Everett Lipman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Soft and Living Matter / Single-Molecule Biophysics
faculty.summary: Experimental biophysicist working in the physics of soft and living matter, developing single-molecule techniques and instrumentation to study biomolecular dynamics.

faculty.name: Matthieu Louis
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Soft and Living Matter / Biophysics and Neuroscience
faculty.summary: Investigates how odor tracking (chemotaxis) emerges through neural-circuit computations in Drosophila larvae, combining computational and experimental methods to map and functionally characterize the neural circuits that convert olfactory information into navigational decisions.

faculty.name: Sebastian Streichan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Physics of Soft and Living Matter / Developmental Biophysics
faculty.summary: Biophysicist working in the physics of soft and living matter, studying the physics of morphogenesis and tissue dynamics during animal development using quantitative imaging.

faculty.name: Hugh Lippincott
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: High Energy Experimental Physics / Dark Matter
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist and Vice Chair for Research working on the direct detection of dark matter, developing and operating underground noble-liquid detector experiments to search for dark matter particles.

faculty.name: Tejaswi Venumadhav Nerella
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works in astrophysics and cosmology with a focus on gravitational-wave astrophysics, developing methods to detect and analyze gravitational-wave signals from compact binary mergers.

funding.note: Full financial support to all admitted PhD students

faculty.name: David Awschalom
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor Awschalom received his bSc in physics from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, and his PhD in experimental physics from Cornell University. He was a Research Staff member and Manager of the Nonequilibrium Physics Department at the IBM Watson Research Center in Yorktown Heights, New York.

faculty.name: Edward Blucher
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental Physics research in particle physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Marcela Carena
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: John E. Carlstrom
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental Physics research in cosmology (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Cheng Chin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Cheng Chin earned his B.S. degree in Physics from National Taiwan University in 1993 and his Ph.D. degree in Physics from Stanford University in 2001 under the advisory of Dr. Steven Chu. He conducted postdoctoral research at Stanford (2001~2003) and at Innsbruck University (2003~2005).

faculty.name: Juan I. Collar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Detector development for Dark Matter and neutrino physics.

faculty.name: Clay Córdova
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical Physics research in condensed matter physics and general relativity and particle physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Luca Delacrétaz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in condensed matter physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Margaret Gardel

faculty.summary: Professors Gardel, Witten, and collaborators recreate cell division.

faculty.name: Luca Grandi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental Physics research in astrophysics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Philippe Guyot-Sionnest
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Physics and chemistry share tremendous potential at the nanoscale. This is where chemistry excels and where physics predicts that many properties can be tuned.

faculty.name: Keisuke Harigaya
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Keisuke Harigaya studies theoretical particle physics and cosmology. He studies mysteries/problems confronting particle physics, constructs models to explain them, and predicts signals to test the models.

faculty.name: Daniel Holz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: DanielHolzworks in the field ofgravitational waveastrophysics and cosmology. He studies how gravitational-wave detections of colliding black holes and neutron stars can helpus better understand the physics and history ofour Universe. He is amember of the LIGO collaboration, and contributed tothe discovery of GW1501914 and GW170817.

faculty.name: William T.M. Irvine
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental Physics research in condensed matter physics and soft matter and soft matter physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Heinrich M. Jaeger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Heinrich Jaeger received his Ph.D. in physics in 1987, working with Allen Goldman at the University of Minnesota on ultrathin superconducting films.

faculty.name: Young-Kee Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Prof. Young-Kee Kim is the Albert A. Michelson Distinguished Service Professor of Physics at UChicago and the Director Emeritus of Fermilab, America’s Particle Physics and Accelerator Laboratory. Previously she served as the President of the American Physical Society (APS).

faculty.name: Michael Levin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Recently, my research has focused on two areas of quantum condensed matter physics. The first area is the study of "topological phases" of matter, such as quantum Hall liquids and topological insulators.

faculty.name: Nadya Mason
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Before becoming dean of PME, Mason was the Rosalyn S. Yalow Professor of Physics at the University of Illinois at Urbana- Champaign, where she directed the Illinois Beckman Institute for Advanced Science and Technology and also served as founding director of the Illinois Materials Research Science and Engineering Center (I-MRSEC).

faculty.name: David W. Miller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: David Miller's research focuses on answering open questions about the fundamental structure of matter.

faculty.name: Arvind Murugan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in biophysics and soft matter and soft matter physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Stephanie Palmer
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in biophysics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Dam Thanh Son
faculty.title: University Professor
faculty.summary: Applies gauge-gravity duality from string theory to strongly interacting systems including the quark-gluon plasma, quantum Hall systems, graphene, and the BCS-BEC crossover in cold atoms. His work bridges high energy theory with condensed matter and nuclear physics.

faculty.name: Abigail Vieregg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Prof. Vieregg builds radio and millimeter wave experiments and use them for neutrino astrophysics and cosmology with the cosmic microwave background.  Vieregg is the PI of PUEO and RNO-G, which are balloon-borne and ground-based radio detectors for ultra-high energy neutrinos.

faculty.name: Scott Wakely
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: My research spans a number of topics in the categories of experimental astroparticle physics and high-energy astrophysics. This includes several investigations into the nature and origin of very high energy (VHE) cosmic radiation, including gamma rays and cosmic rays above 1 GeV.

faculty.name: Zoe Yan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Before joining UChicago, Zoe was a Dicke Postdoctoral Fellow at Princeton University.  She earned her Ph.D. from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and her B.S. with honors in physics from Stanford University.

faculty.name: David Schmitz
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Department of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental Physics research in particle physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Aaron S. Chou
faculty.title: CASE Senior Scientist
faculty.summary: Chou’s research focuses on developing quantum computing and sensing techniques to study fundamental physics.

faculty.name: Jacob Covey
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in atomic physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: David DeMille
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Research in atomic physics and particle physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.summary: Theoretical Physics research in particle physics (per department research-area classification).
