faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental Physics research in condensed matter physics and hard condensed matter (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Kwang-Je Kim
faculty.title: CASE Senior Scientist
faculty.summary: Kwang-Je Kim was trained as an elementaryparticle theorist, but switched to accelerator physics in 1978, when he joined LBNL, serving as the Deputy Director of Center for Beam Physics. He moved to ANL in 1998, where he founded the Argonne Accelerator Institute.

faculty.name: Dahlia Klein
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in condensed matter physics and hard condensed matter (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: ​David Kutasov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: My main research focus in recent years was on a number of questions in field and string theory. One is the dynamics of strongly coupled field theories such as Quantum Chromodynamics, and in particular qualitative phenomena such as confinement and chiral symmetry breaking.

faculty.name: Kathryn Levin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: I am a condensed matter theorist who has worked largely in the fields of superconductivity and superfluidity, with close interactions to experiment. Early work in this subject began with studies of superfluid helium-3, then moved to heavy fermion superconductors and then to the high Tc cuprates.

faculty.name: Ivar Martin
faculty.title: CASE Senior Scientist
faculty.summary: His other interests include microscopic theory of quantum decoherence and quantum measurement, ways to implement unusual correlated states in quantum hardware, and classical nonlinear phenomena of phase and mode locking.

faculty.name: Emil J. Martinec
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Superstring/M-theories; in particular, their geometrical structure and their role in grand unification and the quantization of gravity. Also theories of random surfaces, quantum gravity, black hole physics, and two-dimensional quantum field theory.

faculty.name: Jeffrey McMahon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in astrophysics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Sidney R. Nagel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Many complex phenomena are so familiar that we hardly realize that they defy our normal intuition.Examples include the anomalous flow of granular material, the longmessy tendrils left by honey spooned from one dish to another, the pesky rings deposited by spilled coffee on a table after the liquid evaporates or the common splash of a...

faculty.name: Paolo Privitera
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental Physics research in astrophysics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Robert Rosner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical Physics research in astrophysics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Michael Rust
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental Physics research in biophysics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Guy Savard
faculty.title: Part-time Professor
faculty.summary: My current research is centered around low-energy tests of the Standard Model of electroweak interaction. These studies are performed on samples of radioactive ions captured in ion traps where they are available for high-precision experiments.

faculty.name: Savdeep S. Sethi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: My research orients around questions with the following flavor: what is dark energy? What is the microscopic structure of space and time? Did space and time emerge in some kind of Big Bang? Will the universe end in a Big Crunch? What can we learn about quantum information from quantum gravity and black hole physics?

faculty.name: Dam T. Son
faculty.title: University Professor
faculty.summary: I have a broad research program encompassing several areas of theoretical physics.​

faculty.name: Vincenzo Vitelli
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical Physics research in condensed matter physics and soft matter and soft matter physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Carlos E. M. Wagner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical Physics research in particle physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental Physics research in particle physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Robert Wald
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: My research has been concerned with a broad range of topics in classical general relativity, cosmology, and quantum phenomena related to gravity.

faculty.name: LianTao Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical Physics research in particle physics (per department research-area classification).

faculty.name: Pavel Wiegmann
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed Matter Physics : Electronic Physics in Low Dimensions, Quantum Magnetism, Correlated Electronic Systems, Quantum Hall Effects, Topological aspects of Condensed Matter Theories, Electronic systems far from equilibrium.

faculty.name: Linda Young
faculty.title: Part-time Professor
faculty.summary: Linda Young is a Distinguished Fellow at Argonne NationalLaboratory and a professor of physics at The University ofChicago. She obtained her S.B. from MIT and her Ph.D. from theUniversity of California, Berkeley.

faculty.name: Wendy W. Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical Physics research in condensed matter physics and soft matter and soft matter physics (per department research-area classification).

funding.note: Fully funded PhD

faculty.name: Tobin R. Sosnick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Sosnick lab integrates experimental (HX, NMR, SAXS, mass spectrometry) and computational (molecular dynamics, coarse-grain simulations) approaches to study protein and RNA folding, function, and design. Recent work explores transcriptome-wide mRNP condensation in stress granule formation, temperature-dependent fold-switching in the circadian clock protein KaiB, and the design of light-sensitive allosteric proteins for optogenetics.

faculty.name: Erin J. Adams
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Adams lab investigates the molecular signals the immune system uses to distinguish healthy from unhealthy tissue, focusing on unconventional T cell recognition involving γδ T cells, NKT cells, and MAIT cells alongside antigen presentation by nonclassical MHC-like proteins. The group integrates biochemistry, structural biology, protein engineering, and cellular assays to uncover how immune effector cells regulate human disease.

faculty.name: Eduardo Perozo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Perozo lab studies the structure, function, and gating mechanisms of ion channels, including potassium channels (Shaker, HERG, BK), mechanosensitive channels (MscS), and voltage-sensing domains. The group uses structural biology, electrophysiology, and biophysical approaches to understand how lipid bilayer deformations drive channel gating and how conformation-specific tools can discriminate functional states.

faculty.name: Robert J. Keenan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Keenan lab investigates the molecular mechanisms underlying membrane protein biogenesis and quality control using biochemical reconstitution, structural biology (cryo-EM), and cell biology approaches. Recent work focuses on the ribosome-translocon complex, regulated N-glycosylation at the secretory translocon, and unifying models for how multi-pass membrane proteins are built and folded at the ER.

faculty.name: Benoit Roux
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Roux lab bridges theoretical/computational chemistry and experimental biochemistry to understand molecular mechanisms of membrane proteins, especially ion channels. The group develops and applies molecular simulation tools (notably the CHARMM software package) to study channel gating, ion permeation, and inactivation processes at atomic resolution, while also working on antibody polyreactivity characterization and biosensor design.

faculty.name: Joseph A. Piccirilli
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Piccirilli lab investigates RNA structure, function, and enzymology, with a focus on ribozyme mechanisms, tRNA modifications and their roles in mitochondrial pathology, and RNA-small molecule interactions. The group employs chemical biology, biochemistry, and structural approaches to understand RNA conformational dynamics in disease contexts and the fundamental chemistry of RNA catalysis.

faculty.name: Phoebe A. Rice
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Rice lab investigates how mobile genetic elements move using biochemical, structural, and microbiological approaches. Current work focuses on the SCCmec element of MRSA, the paradigmatic Mu transposase, large serine integrases and their directionality control, and transpososome structures, with implications for understanding horizontal gene transfer and microbial pathogenesis.

faculty.name: Anthony A. Kossiakoff
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Kossiakoff Group investigates how molecular recognition drives biological function, combining structural methods (crystallography, cryo-EM) with protein engineering tools. A major focus is developing synthetic antibodies and chaperone-assisted crystallography to tackle protein systems recalcitrant to conventional approaches, with applications spanning mycobacterial cell wall biosynthesis, mTOR signaling, chemokine receptor-arrestin interactions, and apoptosis.

faculty.name: Rama Ranganathan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Ranganathan lab studies the design principles underlying structure, function, and adaptation in biological systems from atomic to cellular scales. The group uses coevolutionary analysis, deep learning-guided protein design, time-resolved crystallography, and systems biology approaches to understand how complex biological functions — from protein folding to allosteric regulation to signaling networks — arise through evolutionary processes.

faculty.name: Tao Pan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Pan lab studies tRNA biology and epitranscriptomics, focusing on how tRNA dynamics and mRNA modifications like m6A regulate gene expression. The group developed high-throughput sequencing methods to measure tRNA abundance, charging, and modifications, discovered the m6A switch mechanism, and investigates how the gut microbiome reprograms host m6A modifications in a tissue-dependent manner.

faculty.name: Marcos Sotomayor
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Sotomayor lab integrates computational structural biology, simulations, and biochemistry to study how mechanical forces are detected and transduced at the molecular level. A central focus is the auditory hair-cell mechanotransduction complex, including TMC1/2 channels and cadherin-based tip-link proteins, with additional work on TMEM63 mechanosensitive channels and the biophysics of lipid scrambling.

faculty.name: Marvin W. Makinen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Makinen lab focuses on vanadium-based compounds for cancer imaging and therapy, developing vanadium-48-labeled vanadyl acetylacetonate as a novel PET imaging agent. Research also investigates vanadyl chelates as inhibitors of protein tyrosine phosphatase-1B, an enzyme regulating insulin and leptin signaling linked to breast, colorectal, and ovarian cancer.

faculty.name: Chuan He
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The He lab investigates how reversible RNA and DNA methylation regulate gene expression and cellular processes. Discovered the first RNA demethylase (FTO), launching the field of epitranscriptomics, and characterized the m6A methyltransferase complex and reader proteins. Recent work explores RNA modification systems as therapeutic targets in leukemia, microbiome-derived cell-free RNA modifications for colorectal cancer detection, and m6A readers in antitumor immunity.

faculty.name: Joseph W. Thornton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Thornton lab investigates how protein functions evolve over deep time using phylogenetic reconstruction of ancestral proteins followed by synthesis and experimental characterization. The group tackles questions such as how complex molecular systems evolve, the role of epistasis, whether evolution is reversible, and how protein architecture itself evolves, combining evolutionary biology, biochemistry, biophysics, and computational biology.

faculty.name: D. Allan Drummond
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: The Drummond lab investigates how cells cope with stress at the molecular level, focusing on the formation and dissolution of large assemblies of proteins and RNA during events like heat shock. The group integrates in vivo imaging, in vitro reconstitution, mechanistic biochemistry, quantitative proteomics, and molecular evolutionary analyses, primarily using budding yeast as a model organism to study stress-triggered biomolecular condensation as an adaptive, evolutionarily tuned response.

faculty.name: Jingyi Fei
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: The Fei lab investigates how RNAs govern gene expression across bacterial and eukaryotic systems using advanced single-molecule and super-resolution imaging. Recent work has explored transcriptome-wide mRNA condensation preceding stress granule formation, RNA localization dynamics at nuclear speckles linked to splicing efficiency, translational T-box riboswitch mechanisms, and KARR-seq for mapping cellular higher-order RNA structures.

faculty.name: Engin Ozkan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: The Ozkan lab investigates the biochemistry and interaction networks of proteins essential for nervous system development, focusing on deciphering protein-protein interaction networks of the extracellular space required for axon guidance, pruning, and synapse formation. The group employs a multidisciplinary approach combining crystallography, cellular signaling assays, and protein engineering with genetic modifications in model organisms.

faculty.name: Minglei Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: The Zhao lab studies the mechanisms of molecular machines and their roles in human disease, focusing on two primary systems: the p97/ubiquitination system (a central hub in cellular protein homeostasis linked to neurodegenerative diseases and cancer) and the vault RNP particle (the largest ribonucleoprotein in eukaryotes whose function remains elusive). The group uses structural, proteomic, and imaging approaches to elucidate molecular mechanisms.

faculty.name: Demet Arac-Ozkan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: The Arac lab investigates how cell-surface receptors mediate intercellular adhesion and communication, focusing on their roles in brain development, synapse formation, and neural wiring. Using structural biology and biochemical approaches, the group studies adhesion GPCRs (ADGRG6/GPR126, ADGRL3/Latrophilin3, CELSR1-3) with direct relevance to neurological disorders, developmental impairments, glioblastoma, and congenital anosmia.

faculty.name: Ronald S. Rock
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: The Rock lab investigates how myosin motor proteins navigate the cytoskeleton and dynamically organize cells, studying biological motion from single-molecule to cellular scales. The group uses advanced fluorescence microscopy, optical tweezers, and structural biology to observe myosin movement in vitro and in living cells, and has developed optogenetic tools like LILAC for enhanced actin imaging.

faculty.name: Cassandra Hayne
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: The Hayne lab investigates how tRNAs are processed and regulated, focusing on the making and breaking of tRNAs by ribonucleases, with implications for neurodegenerative and neurodevelopmental disorders. Using cryo-electron microscopy, the group has elucidated the structural basis of pre-tRNA recognition and cleavage by the human TSEN (tRNA splicing endonuclease) complex.

faculty.name: Axel Concepcion
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: The Concepcion lab identifies and characterizes novel membrane proteins and ion channels in immune cells, combining functional genomics, systems immunology, electrophysiology, and cell biology to understand how ion channels regulate immune responses. Research has implications for viral infection, autoimmunity, and cancer, with recent work funded by the NIH and Cancer Research Foundation.

funding.note: Fully funded PhD

faculty.name: Samuel G. Armato III
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chair of the Committee on Medical Physics; develops computer-aided diagnosis and quantitative image analysis methods, particularly for lung cancer and mesothelioma.

faculty.name: Maryellen L. Giger
faculty.title: Distinguished Service Professor
faculty.summary: A.N. Pritzker Distinguished Service Professor of Radiology working on computer-aided diagnosis and machine learning for cancer risk assessment, diagnosis, and treatment response.

faculty.name: Gregory S. Karczmar
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of MRI research developing functional and anatomic magnetic resonance imaging methods to improve cancer detection, diagnosis, and treatment monitoring.

faculty.name: Xiaochuan Pan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops advanced reconstruction algorithms enabling high-quality images from sparse, low-dose CT data and novel imaging system designs.

faculty.name: Patrick J. La Riviere
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops algorithms and algorithm-enabled imaging systems across scales from x-ray microscopy to human CT, with themes of dose reduction and material identification.

faculty.name: Chien-Min Kao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel detector technologies and organ-specific PET systems, including flat-panel time-of-flight PET detectors and PET/MRI inserts.

faculty.name: Ingrid Reiser
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Clinical medical physicist focusing on x-ray imaging physics, image quality assessment, model observers, and anthropomorphic phantoms for breast tomosynthesis and radiography.

faculty.name: Zheng Feng Lu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Clinical diagnostic physicist and section chief of clinical physics, directing the clinical imaging medical physics residency and working on imaging performance and QA.

faculty.name: Yulei Jiang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on imaging evaluation and assessment methodology, including computer-aided diagnosis and reader studies in breast imaging.

faculty.name: Chin-Tu Chen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on integrative multi-modality molecular imaging spanning imaging physics and instrumentation, image reconstruction, imaging probes, and quantitative analysis.

faculty.name: Kenneth B. Bader
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the BADER Lab translating therapeutic ultrasound, including histotripsy and cavitation-based ablation, for cardiovascular and cancer treatment.

faculty.name: Howard J. Halpern
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops very-low-frequency electron paramagnetic resonance imaging to measure tumor tissue oxygenation and guide radiation therapy response.

faculty.name: Kamil M. Yenice
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Chief of clinical physics working on image-guided stereotactic treatment delivery and quality assurance for radiation therapy.

faculty.name: Bulent Aydogan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of medical physics working on advanced radiation therapy including compensator-based small-animal IMRT, oxygen-guided radiotherapy, and total marrow irradiation.

funding.note: TA: $29,000 12-month stipend + subsidized health (Fall 2027); fully funded

faculty.name: Amlan Biswas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist studying complex oxides and phase separation in correlated electron materials.

faculty.name: Stephen Hagen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Department Chair; studies gene regulation and bacterial dynamics using biophysical approaches.

faculty.name: Peter Hirschfeld
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying unconventional superconductivity and correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Sergey Klimenko
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental gravitational-wave physicist developing data-analysis methods for the LIGO detectors.

faculty.name: Andrey Korytov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist studying Higgs boson properties and searches for beyond-standard-model physics at CMS.

faculty.name: James Hamlin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist studying superconductivity and quantum materials under high pressure.

faculty.name: Dmitrii Maslov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying electron transport and correlated metals.

faculty.name: Guido Mueller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental gravitational-wave physicist developing laser interferometry for space-based and ground-based detectors such as LISA and LIGO.

faculty.name: Heather Ray
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist working on neutrino and astroparticle physics experiments.

faculty.name: Tarek Saab
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental astroparticle physicist working on direct-detection dark matter searches with cryogenic detectors.

faculty.name: Christopher Stanton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying semiconductors, nanostructures, and ultrafast optical phenomena.

faculty.name: David Tanner
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist using infrared/optical spectroscopy to study correlated materials; also involved in axion dark matter searches.

faculty.name: Clifford M. Will
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Works on the theory of gravitational radiation from astrophysical sources, tests of general relativity in the solar system and binary pulsars, and studies relativistic effects of massive black holes including tests of black hole no-hair theorems.

faculty.name: Richard Woodard
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist working on quantum gravity and quantum field theory in cosmological settings.

faculty.name: Kevin Ingersent
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying quantum impurity problems and correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Xiao-Xiao Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist using ultrafast and optical probes to study quantum and 2D materials.

faculty.name: Imre Bartos
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental astrophysicist working on multi-messenger astrophysics and extreme cosmic events, including gravitational-wave observations.

faculty.name: Laura Blecha
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical astrophysicist studying black holes and galaxy evolution.

faculty.name: Paul Fulda
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Graduate Recruiter; experimental gravitational-wave physicist developing interferometry and optics for detectors such as LIGO.

faculty.name: BingKan Xue
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical biological physicist building models of species interaction, adaptation, and evolution.

faculty.name: Wei Xue
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist working on dark matter, particle astrophysics, and physics beyond the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Jeff Andrews
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical astrophysicist studying compact-object binaries and stellar populations relevant to gravitational-wave sources.

faculty.name: Jeff Dror
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist in the Institute for Fundamental Theory working on dark matter and physics beyond the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Dominique Laroche
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist studying quantum transport in nanostructures and low-dimensional systems.

faculty.name: Edwin Pedrozo-Peñafiel
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops cavity quantum electrodynamics platforms for non-destructive measurements and collective light-matter interactions, creating entangled states for sensing beyond the standard quantum limit and using ultracold atoms in optical cavities to benchmark quantum measurement techniques.

faculty.name: Kathleen Amm
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Research Professor working on superconducting magnet technology and applied superconductivity. Verify PhD advising in the physics program.

faculty.name: Laura Greene
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Research Professor and MagLab Chief Scientist; experimental condensed matter physicist studying unconventional superconductivity and strongly correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Jacobo Konigsberg
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Research Professor in experimental high energy physics working on Higgs boson physics and trigger systems at the CMS experiment.

faculty.name: Thomas Angelini
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads research in cell mechanics and biophysics, studying the mechanical behavior of living soft matter.

faculty.name: Gabriel Birzu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops quantitative models of microbial ecosystems and population dynamics.

faculty.name: Philip Chang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist working on electroweak multi-boson production and Higgs physics at the CMS experiment.

faculty.name: Sudi Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist studying quantum materials. Specific research focus not confirmed from department pages.

faculty.name: Yohei Ema
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist working on particle cosmology and physics of the early universe.

faculty.name: Ivan Furic
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist working on the CMS experiment at the LHC.

faculty.name: Selman Hershfield
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Undergraduate Coordinator; theoretical condensed matter physicist working on nonequilibrium quantum transport.

faculty.name: Rachel Houtz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist in the Institute for Fundamental Theory working on physics beyond the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Chunjing Jia
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Computational condensed matter theorist studying quantum materials and correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Yoonseok Lee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Graduate Coordinator; low-temperature physicist specializing in superfluid helium and quantum turbulence.

faculty.name: Mark Meisel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist studying magnetism and quantum magnets at low temperatures.

faculty.name: Khandker Muttalib
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying disordered systems and mesoscopic transport.

faculty.name: Haining Pan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying topological and moire quantum materials.

faculty.name: Edwin Pedrozo-Penafiel
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental AMO physicist working on quantum metrology and entanglement-enhanced atomic clocks.

faculty.name: Emrah Simsek
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies quantitative systems microbiology of environmental stress and antibiotic resistance.

faculty.name: Gregory Stewart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist studying heavy-fermion systems and superconductivity at low temperatures.

faculty.name: Neil Sullivan
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Low-temperature physicist studying quantum fluids and solids using NMR; long associated with the National High Magnetic Field Laboratory.

faculty.name: Yuta Takahashi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist specializing in tau-lepton physics, B-physics, and searches for new physics at CMS.

faculty.name: Yasumasa Takano
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Low-temperature experimentalist studying quantum and frustrated magnetism.

faculty.name: Yuxuan Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying superconductivity and quantum criticality in correlated systems.

faculty.name: Clifford Will
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on general relativity, experimental tests of gravity, and gravitational-wave theory.

faculty.name: Siyao Xu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical astrophysicist studying turbulence, magnetic fields, and the interstellar medium.

faculty.name: John Yelton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist studying heavy-flavor physics and hadron spectroscopy.

faculty.name: Jiabin Yu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying topological materials and quantum-geometric effects.

requirements.admission_model: GRE General and Physics Subject optional

funding.note: Grainger PhD guaranteed funded appointment (tuition waiver + stipend) first 5 years

faculty.name: Peter Abbamonte
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates electronic properties of quantum materials using spectroscopic techniques and probes emergent phenomena in strongly correlated systems.

faculty.name: Peter Adshead
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches early-universe cosmology, including inflation and the generation of primordial perturbations.

faculty.name: Aleksei Aksimentiev
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses molecular dynamics simulations to study biomolecular systems and nanoscale transport.

faculty.name: Douglas H. Beck
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on fundamental symmetries and precision measurements in nuclear physics.

faculty.name: Alexey Bezryadin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies quantum transport and superconductivity in nanowires and low-dimensional systems.

faculty.name: Barry Bradlyn
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on topological phases of matter and the classification of electronic band structures.

faculty.name: Yann Chemla
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops single-molecule techniques to study the mechanics and dynamics of biomolecular machines.

faculty.name: Lu Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Explores quantum materials using advanced spectroscopic and scanning probe methods.

faculty.name: Jacob P. Covey
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on ultracold atoms and molecules for applications in quantum computing and simulation.

faculty.name: Karin A. Dahmen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical mechanics models for avalanches, earthquakes, and deformation in materials.

faculty.name: Brian Leeds DeMarco
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses ultracold atoms to explore quantum many-body physics and develop quantum technologies.

faculty.name: Patrick I. Draper
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Addresses beyond-Standard-Model physics, naturalness, and connections between quantum information science and fundamental theory.

faculty.name: Aida X. El-Khadra
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Performs lattice QCD calculations to understand strong-interaction physics and precision phenomenology.

faculty.name: Charles Forbes Gammie
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies black-hole accretion, plasma astrophysics, and theoretical models of astrophysical flows.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Goldschmidt
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies light-matter interactions and quantum memories for applications in quantum networks.

faculty.name: Gilbert Holder
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on cosmic microwave background observations and their implications for cosmology and structure formation.

faculty.name: Taylor L. Hughes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates topological phases, classification of quantum matter, and topological quantum computation.

faculty.name: Paul G. Kwiat
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches quantum optics, quantum information processing, and fundamental tests of quantum mechanics.

faculty.name: Vidya Madhavan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses scanning tunneling microscopy to study topological and correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Mark Neubauer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on ATLAS at the LHC, searching for new particles and measuring Higgs boson properties.

faculty.name: Jorge Noronha
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies relativistic hydrodynamics and the quark-gluon plasma in heavy-ion collisions and neutron-star mergers.

faculty.name: Jaki Noronha-Hostler
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses hydrodynamics and transport theory to model nuclear matter under extreme conditions.

faculty.name: Wolfgang Pfaff
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops superconducting qubits and hybrid quantum systems for quantum information processing.

faculty.name: Philip W. Phillips
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on strongly correlated electron systems, holographic duality, and the theory of high-temperature superconductivity.

faculty.name: Jessie Shelton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Explores dark matter phenomenology and connections between particle physics and cosmology.

faculty.name: Anne M. Sickles
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the quark-gluon plasma using heavy-ion collisions at RHIC and the LHC.

faculty.name: Joaquin Vieira
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Observes the early universe using millimeter and submillimeter telescopes to study galaxy formation and the CMB.

faculty.name: Smitha Vishveshwara
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical work spans topological matter, ultracold atoms, and quantum information in many-body systems.

faculty.name: Lucas K. Wagner
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops and applies quantum Monte Carlo methods to understand strongly correlated materials.

faculty.name: Nicolas Yunes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on gravitational-wave astrophysics, tests of general relativity, and neutron-star structure.

faculty.name: Bryan K. Clark
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational and quantum-information methods, including tensor networks and quantum Monte Carlo, to study strongly correlated quantum systems.

faculty.name: S. Lance Cooper
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses light-scattering and optical spectroscopy to study correlated-electron and quantum materials, including their behavior under extreme conditions.

faculty.name: James N. Eckstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Grows and studies oxide thin films and heterostructures by molecular-beam epitaxy to explore superconductivity and interfacial quantum phenomena.

faculty.name: Thomas Faulkner
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on quantum field theory, holography, and the role of quantum entanglement and information in gravity and strongly coupled systems.

faculty.name: Rafael Fernandes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying superconductivity, magnetism, electronic nematicity, and correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Jeffrey P. Filippini
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Observational cosmologist building balloon-borne and ground-based instruments to measure the cosmic microwave background and probe inflation.

faculty.name: Eduardo H. Fradkin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed matter theorist working on topological phases, field-theoretic approaches to strongly correlated systems, and the fractional quantum Hall effect; directs the Institute for Condensed Matter Theory.

faculty.name: Russ Giannetta
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist performing low-temperature measurements of superconductors and correlated electron materials.

faculty.name: Ido Golding
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Biological physicist studying gene expression and the physics of bacteria and bacteriophages at the single-molecule and single-cell level.

faculty.name: Matthias Grosse Perdekamp
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental nuclear physicist studying the spin structure of the nucleon and quantum chromodynamics using high-energy collisions.

faculty.name: Benjamin Hooberman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental high energy physicist on ATLAS at the LHC, searching for dark matter and supersymmetry and developing machine-learning methods.

faculty.name: Sangjin Kim
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Biological physicist using single-molecule and quantitative approaches to study gene regulation and molecular machines.

faculty.name: Angela Kou
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist studying superconducting quantum circuits and hybrid quantum systems for quantum information.

faculty.name: Eric Kuo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Physics education researcher studying student reasoning, problem solving, and the learning of physics concepts.

faculty.name: Jong Yeon Lee
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed matter theorist studying topological phases, quantum information in many-body systems, and measurement-induced phenomena.

faculty.name: Chen-Yu Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist using ultracold neutrons to make precision measurements of fundamental symmetries, including the neutron lifetime.

faculty.name: Josh Long
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist performing precision tests of gravity at short distances to search for new forces and physics beyond the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Virginia Lorenz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental AMO physicist working on quantum optics, single-photon sources, and quantum information in photonic systems.

faculty.name: Fahad Mahmood
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses ultrafast and time-resolved spectroscopy to study and control quantum materials and correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Telemachos Mouschovias
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical astrophysicist studying star formation and the role of magnetic fields in the interstellar medium.

faculty.name: Munir H. Nayfeh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist studying silicon nanoparticles and their optical, electronic, and applied properties.

faculty.name: Paul Selvin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops single-molecule fluorescence techniques to study molecular motors and neuroscience-related biophysics.

faculty.name: Stuart L. Shapiro
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical astrophysicist using numerical relativity to study compact objects, black holes, neutron stars, and gravitational waves.

faculty.name: Hector Silva
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on gravitational physics, neutron stars, and tests of general relativity with gravitational waves.

faculty.name: Jun Song
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies physics and computational modeling to genomics, gene regulation, and problems in quantitative biology.

faculty.name: Tim Stelzer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: High energy theorist working on collider phenomenology and automated tools for particle-physics calculations, as well as physics education.

faculty.name: Michael Stone
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist working on mathematical physics, condensed matter theory, and topological aspects of quantum systems.

faculty.name: Tanner Trickle
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working at the interface of particle physics and condensed matter, developing methods to detect light dark matter using quantum materials.

faculty.name: Pengjie Wang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimentalist studying quantum transport in two-dimensional and topological materials.

faculty.name: Helvi Witek
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist working on numerical relativity, black holes, and gravitational-wave signatures of fundamental fields.

faculty.name: Hyun Youk
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Biological physicist studying how cells and cellular collectives process information and self-organize, using synthetic and quantitative biology.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Aleksei Aksimentiev
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Aksimentiev's group uses atomic-scale computational modeling to understand how biological nanomachines achieve their function and applies this knowledge to design synthetic molecular systems, bridging biomolecular structure and dynamics with engineering.

faculty.name: Mikael P Backlund
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Backlund's lab develops imaging and sensing techniques that integrate quantum information science with biophysics, including nanoscale magnetic resonance using quantum defects in diamond and new single-molecule optical microscopy methods applied to amyloid proteins.

faculty.name: Andrew S Belmont
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Belmont investigates how chromatin fibers fold into chromosomes and how interphase chromosome positioning within the nucleus regulates gene expression and replication, using CRISPR-based tagging, advanced microscopy, and TSA-seq to relate genome positioning to transcription near nuclear condensates.

faculty.name: Yann Robert Chemla
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chemla studies the structural dynamics and mechanics of biological systems at the single-molecule level using high-resolution optical tweezers, focusing on molecular motors and nucleic-acid and protein translocases.

faculty.name: Ido Golding
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Quantitative / systems biology of gene expression
faculty.summary: Golding studies how living cells process environmental information and make decisions, combining genetic manipulation, high-sensitivity fluorescence imaging, and mathematical modeling in model systems such as E. coli and bacteriophage lambda.

faculty.name: Claudio F Grosman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Grosman's lab investigates the structure-function relationship in neurotransmitter-gated ion channels, particularly nicotinic receptors, using electrophysiology and protein engineering to dissect ligand-binding kinetics and postsynaptic current decay.

faculty.name: Hee Sun Han
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Han develops bioanalytical technologies using microfluidics, bioimaging, and nanotechnology, including multiscale/multimodal imaging platforms and droplet-microfluidics assays to study brains, tumor tissues, and microbial communities.

faculty.name: Hong Jin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Jin studies the regulation of protein synthesis, focusing on how ribosomes initiate and terminate translation, using structural biology and genomic approaches to understand cap-independent translation and bacterial ribosome dynamics relevant to disease and antibiotics.

faculty.name: Sangjin Kim
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Kim investigates the physical principles of molecular interactions in cells, using single-molecule microscopy to study how gene-expression machines work together, including DNA supercoiling and the spatial organization of genetic processes in bacteria.

faculty.name: Zaida Ann Luthey-Schulten
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Luthey-Schulten develops computational approaches spanning molecular dynamics to complete-cell simulations, focusing on whole-cell simulations of bacterial and eukaryal organisms and the energy landscapes of biological molecules.

faculty.name: Sergei Maslov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Maslov carries out physics-based computational modeling of complex biological systems, spanning genome evolution, biomolecular networks, and microbial ecosystem dynamics.

faculty.name: Satish K Nair
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Nair (Director of the Center for Biophysics and Quantitative Biology) studies how bacteria produce natural products for inter-species communication and competition, combining biochemistry, microbiology, and X-ray crystallography to inform new antibiotics and therapeutics.

faculty.name: Lisa Olshansky
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Olshansky's lab designs biomimetic metal complexes that undergo stimulus-responsive conformational changes to control chemical reactivity, with applications in biomedical imaging, targeted drug delivery, and renewable energy.

faculty.name: Supriya Gangadharan Prasanth
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Prasanth studies eukaryotic DNA replication, chromosome structure and maintenance, heterochromatin organization, and cell-cycle control, with focus on how Origin Recognition Complex proteins coordinate replication with chromosome segregation relevant to cancer.

faculty.name: Paul R Selvin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Selvin develops novel fluorescence imaging techniques such as FIONA (Fluorescence Imaging with One-Nanometer Accuracy) to study molecular motors and biomolecules at the single-molecule level, revealing hand-over-hand motion of motors like kinesin and myosin.

faculty.name: Diwakar Shukla
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Shukla develops computational and experimental methods, including AI/ML approaches, to understand the functional role of dynamics in molecular processes such as chemical perception and molecular transport at atomic resolution.

faculty.name: Beth Marie Stadtmueller
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Stadtmueller's lab uses structural biology and biophysics to study protein structures and mechanisms in immune, bacterial, and retroviral systems, including secretory IgA antibodies for therapeutic engineering and endogenous retroviral proteins in health and disease.

faculty.name: Emad Tajkhorshid
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Tajkhorshid applies computational methods to understand membranes and membrane proteins, including molecular transport mechanisms, energy transduction in bioenergetic proteins, and how lipids regulate protein signaling.

faculty.name: Nicholas Ching Hai Wu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Wu investigates constraints on viral evolution and antibody specificity, primarily in influenza and SARS-CoV-2, using sequence-structure-function analysis and machine learning to inform vaccine design.

faculty.name: Kai Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Zhang studies how signal transduction governs cell-fate decisions and how signaling is compromised in disease, using live-cell imaging and optogenetic control of signaling pathways with applications to neurological disorders.

faculty.name: Huimin Zhao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Zhao's group applies synthetic biology, machine learning, and laboratory automation to engineer improved proteins, metabolic pathways, and genomes for applications spanning drug discovery, industrial biotechnology, and mammalian synthetic biology.

faculty.name: Ting Lu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lu studies how gene regulatory networks can be analyzed, built, and reprogrammed, emphasizing bacterial regulatory networks and engineered microbial consortia for biomedical applications by integrating mathematical modeling with experiments.

funding.note: Virtually all Ph.D. students fully funded for duration via TA/RA positions

faculty.name: Sankar Das Sarma
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed matter theory and topological quantum computation. Director of the Condensed Matter Theory Center.

faculty.name: Jordan Goodman
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Particle astrophysics and cosmic ray physics. Associated with the HAWC observatory.

faculty.name: Christopher Jarzynski
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Non-equilibrium statistical mechanics and thermodynamics, known for the Jarzynski equality.

faculty.name: Theodore Jacobson
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Quantum gravity and black hole thermodynamics.

faculty.name: James Drake
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Plasma physics and magnetic reconnection in space and astrophysical plasmas.

faculty.name: Abolhassan Jawahery
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental high energy physics with the LHCb experiment studying CP violation and rare decays.

faculty.name: Thomas Antonsen
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Works in plasma physics and electromagnetic theory.

faculty.name: Sylvester Gates
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Supersymmetry, string theory, and mathematical physics.

faculty.name: Bei Lok Hu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gravitational theory and quantum cosmology.

faculty.name: Kara Hoffman
faculty.title: Professor (Chair)
faculty.summary: Experimental gravitational wave physics with LIGO.

faculty.name: Kaustubh Agashe
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical high energy physics at the Maryland Center for Fundamental Physics.

faculty.name: Steven Anlage
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research involves complex materials and quantum systems.

faculty.name: Maissam Barkeshli
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter and quantum information.

faculty.name: Paulo Bedaque
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical nuclear and particle physics including lattice QCD.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Beise
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental nuclear physics and fundamental symmetries.

faculty.name: Alberto Belloni
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: High energy experimental physics.

faculty.name: Zackaria Chacko
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical high energy physics and beyond-Standard-Model physics at the Maryland Center for Fundamental Physics.

faculty.name: Thomas Cohen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical nuclear and particle physics.

faculty.name: Zohreh Davoudi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Lattice gauge theory and quantum simulation for nuclear physics.

faculty.name: Victor Galitski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed matter theory and quantum many-body systems. Chesapeake Chair Professor.

faculty.name: Michelle Girvan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Nonlinear dynamics and complex networks.

faculty.name: Mohammad Hafezi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Quantum photonics and topological optics. Minta Martin Professor.

faculty.name: Carter Hall
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental gravitation, dark matter detection, and plasma physics.

faculty.name: Anson Hook
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical high energy physics and dark matter.

faculty.name: Drew Baden
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental high energy physics at collider experiments.

faculty.name: Sarah Eno
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental high energy physics with the CMS experiment at the LHC.

faculty.name: Manuel Franco Sevilla
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental high energy flavor physics at collider experiments.

faculty.name: Will Fox
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental plasma physics in the Energy Research Facility.

faculty.name: Brian Clark
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Experimental Astroparticle Physics / High-Energy Neutrino Astronomy
faculty.summary: Detects and studies ultra-high-energy neutrinos using radio-detection techniques across the Askaryan Radio Array, the IceCube Neutrino Observatory, and the Radio Neutrino Observatory-Greenland, spanning data analysis, simulation, and detector development.

faculty.name: Phoebe Hamilton
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist on the LHCb experiment at the LHC, studying semileptonic b-hadron decays, lepton-flavor-universality tests, and CP violation, and developing detector electronics for the Upstream Tracker sub-detector.

faculty.name: Xiangdong Ji
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Theorist studying the quark and gluon structure of the nucleon within Quantum Chromodynamics, known for a gauge-invariant decomposition of nucleon spin into quark and gluon spin and orbital angular momentum contributions; also participates in liquid-xenon dark-matter searches.

faculty.name: Ki-Yong Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Ultrafast Optical Science group, using ultrashort laser pulses spanning terahertz to X-ray wavelengths to probe laser interactions with atoms, molecules, solids, and plasmas, with a focus on intense-laser generation of strong THz radiation via two-color gas plasmas.

faculty.name: Alicia Kollár
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Chesapeake Associate Professor whose group builds superconducting circuit-QED systems as artificial photonic materials, engineering coplanar-waveguide resonator lattices (including hyperbolic-space lattices and flat bands) to realize quantum simulators of lattice and spin models.

faculty.name: Daniel Lathrop
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Nonlinear Dynamics / Experimental Geophysical & Fluid Physics
faculty.summary: The Nonlinear Dynamics Lab studies turbulent fluid flows, geomagnetism, and superfluid helium, using rotating liquid-sodium experiments (including a 3-meter geodynamo) as laboratory models of Earth's core to understand dynamo generation of planetary magnetic fields.

faculty.name: Norbert Linke
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental group working with trapped atomic ions on quantum information processing, including implementation of quantum algorithms, machine learning for quantum computers, hybrid quantum simulation, and ion-photon quantum networking; directs UMD's National Quantum Laboratory (QLab).

faculty.name: Wolfgang Losert
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the nonlinear dynamics of complex living systems at the convergence of physics, biology, and AI, examining how cell motion and collective behavior respond to physical cues such as surface topography, adhesivity, and cell-cell adhesion, with a focus on cancer biology.

faculty.name: Ryo Mori
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Alford L. Ward Assistant Professor whose group investigates emergent phenomena in quantum materials using advanced multidimensional photoemission spectroscopy, exploring intrinsic states as well as states created through external driving or artificial design.

faculty.name: Maria Mukhina
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops intracellular force nanoprobes with quantitative optical readout and super-resolution imaging to study how cells manage mechanical energy across subcellular structures and orchestrate transitions between functional architectures of the genome.

faculty.name: Min Ouyang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans nanoscale condensed matter physics, materials chemistry, and instrumentation development, focusing on light-matter-spin interactions at the nanoscale using nanomaterials advancement, ultrafast spectroscopy, and new local-probe techniques.

faculty.name: Johnpierre Paglione
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Maryland Quantum Materials Center, blending materials exploration with the study of quantum phenomena in unconventional superconductors, topological materials, and strongly correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Christopher Palmer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on the CMS experiment at the LHC, investigating the Higgs boson through di-Higgs searches and contributing to detector development including the CMS MIP Timing Detector, following earlier work on the Higgs discovery and HL-LHC tracker upgrades.

faculty.name: Sasha Philippov
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: First-principles theory and modeling of plasmas around black holes and neutron stars, studying coherent radio emission mechanisms (including Fast Radio Bursts), flares from accreting black holes and merging neutron stars, and particle acceleration in black-hole-powered jets.

faculty.name: Douglas Roberts
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental high-energy physicist whose research centers on the BaBar experiment; has also served as the department's Associate Chair for Graduate Studies.

faculty.name: Steven Rolston
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: AMO physicist and JQI Fellow leading the Rolston ultracold matter group, creating controlled interactions between photons using atoms as intermediaries and developing techniques to manipulate individual and paired photons for light-based quantum technologies.

faculty.name: Jay Sau
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist applying topological principles to protected solid-state and cold-atomic systems for quantum information processing, working on topological superconductivity, Majorana fermions, and non-abelian phases for quantum computation.

faculty.name: Nathan Schine
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: AMO physicist whose lab studies the intersection of controlled coherent dynamics and engineered dissipation by interfacing a neutral-atom (ytterbium) array with an optical cavity to simulate hard-to-probe quantum phenomena.

faculty.name: Eun-Suk Seo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads UMD's Cosmic Ray Physics Group, studying the origin, acceleration, and propagation of highly relativistic galactic cosmic rays using balloon- and space-borne detectors through projects such as CREAM/ISS-CREAM, ATIC, AMS, and BESS.

faculty.name: Peter Shawhan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gravitational-wave physicist working on LIGO who searches for signals from merging black holes and neutron stars, and who helped establish rapid sharing of gravitational-wave event candidates to enable multi-messenger electromagnetic follow-up.

faculty.name: Aaron Sternbach
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist whose lab investigates and manipulates quantum materials using nano-optical methods including ultrafast, near-field, and broadband spectroscopy, achieving roughly 10-nanometer spatial resolution across the far- to near-infrared.

faculty.name: Gregory Sullivan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on neutrino detection and cosmic-ray physics through the IceCube Neutrino Observatory at the South Pole, where he served as spokesperson (2011-2013); his group contributed to the first observations of cosmic neutrinos.

faculty.name: Raman Sundrum
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical research on extra spacetime dimensions, supersymmetry, and strongly coupled dynamics in particle physics and cosmology; best known for the Randall-Sundrum warped extra-dimension models. Director of the Maryland Center for Fundamental Physics.

faculty.name: Arpita Upadhyaya
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: The Upadhyaya lab uses quantitative imaging, biophysical measurements, and computational analysis to study cellular mechanics and the physical forces that let cells sense and respond to their environment, focusing on immune cells and cancer cells.

faculty.name: Edo Waks
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Herbert Rabin Distinguished Professor leading the Quantum Photonics Laboratory, developing quantum technology based on nanoscale photonic and semiconductor devices for quantum computation, communication, and sensing, spanning photonic quantum walks, semiconductor quantum emitters, and solid-state quantum nonlinear optics.

faculty.name: Ronald Walsworth
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Minta Martin Professor leading an interdisciplinary group developing precision-measurement and quantum-sensing tools centered on nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centers in diamond, including quantum diamond microscopy, NV-NMR spectroscopy, and dark-matter searches; founding director of the UMD Quantum Technology Center.

faculty.name: Victor Yakovenko
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Condensed matter theorist studying the electronic properties of materials and a pioneer of econophysics, applying statistical-physics methods to economic and financial problems such as income and wealth inequality.

faculty.name: William Phillips
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Works on laser cooling and trapping of neutral atoms, quantum information with single-atom qubits, Bose-Einstein condensates, atoms in optical lattices, and coherent matter-wave optics. Shared the 1997 Nobel Prize in Physics for developing methods to cool and trap atoms with laser light.

funding.note: Admitted with TA or RA; university and Chemical Physics fellowships available

faculty.name: John T. Fourkas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the dynamics of liquids and interfaces with ultrafast nonlinear optical spectroscopy and develops multiphoton nanolithography for nanofabrication.

faculty.name: Amy S. Mullin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses high-resolution laser spectroscopy to study molecular collisional energy transfer and employs an optical centrifuge to spin molecules into extreme rotational states.

faculty.name: Christopher Jarzynski
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Develops the theory of nonequilibrium statistical mechanics and thermodynamics, including the Jarzynski equality and thermodynamics of small systems.

faculty.name: Pratyush Tiwary
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Combines machine learning with statistical mechanics to accelerate molecular dynamics simulations and understand rare events such as conformational changes and crystallization.

faculty.name: Wolfgang Losert
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies collective and dynamic behavior in living cells and in nonlinear physical systems such as granular materials.

faculty.name: Daniel P. Lathrop
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Runs experiments on turbulence, nonlinear dynamics, and magnetohydrodynamics, including a rotating liquid-sodium model of the Earth's geodynamo.

faculty.name: Howard M. Milchberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates intense laser-matter interactions, nonlinear optics, and laser-plasma phenomena such as optical filamentation.

faculty.name: William D. Phillips
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Nobel laureate whose research develops laser cooling and trapping of atoms and studies ultracold quantum gases.

faculty.name: Steve Rolston
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies ultracold atoms, ultracold plasmas, and quantum information science using atomic physics techniques.

faculty.name: Edward Ott
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: A leading figure in chaos and nonlinear dynamics, studying complex systems and machine-learning approaches to predicting dynamics.

faculty.name: Ronald L. Walsworth
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops quantum sensing tools based on nitrogen-vacancy centers in diamond for precision measurement and imaging across the sciences.

faculty.name: Arpita Upadhyaya
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the physics of cell mechanics and force generation, including how immune cells sense and respond to mechanical cues.

faculty.name: Janice E. Reutt-Robey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses scanning tunneling microscopy and surface science methods to study molecular self-assembly and reactions at interfaces.

faculty.name: Yu-Huang Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the chemistry and spectroscopy of carbon nanotubes and DNA-nanotube hybrid materials.

faculty.name: Efrain Rodriguez
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Synthesizes and characterizes solid-state materials such as superconductors and magnetic compounds, using neutron and X-ray scattering.

faculty.name: Johnpierre Paglione
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist studying unconventional superconductivity and quantum materials.

faculty.name: Min Ouyang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies nanoscale materials and their optical and quantum properties for nanophotonics applications.

faculty.name: Sang Bok Lee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops nanostructured materials for electrochemical energy storage and related applications.

faculty.name: Srinivasa R. Raghavan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the self-assembly of soft materials such as gels, vesicles, and complex fluids.

faculty.name: Leah Dodson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses spectroscopy of gas-phase radicals and clusters to study chemical species relevant to atmospheric and astrochemical environments.

faculty.name: Avik Dutt
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops photonic systems including synthetic dimensions in optical resonators for quantum information and simulation.

faculty.name: Silvina Matysiak
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops multiscale molecular simulations to study the structure and dynamics of proteins, peptides, and membranes.

faculty.name: Thomas E. Murphy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on photonics, nonlinear optics, and optoelectronic devices, including photonic approaches to computing.

faculty.name: Yanne Chembo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies nonlinear optical phenomena in microresonators, optical frequency combs, and microwave photonics.

faculty.name: Hong-Zhou Ye
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops electronic structure and quantum chemistry methods to model the properties of molecules and materials.

faculty.name: Daniel Falvey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies photochemical reactions and reactive intermediates using physical organic chemistry approaches.

faculty.name: Lawrence Sita
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops organometallic catalysts for precision polymerization and materials synthesis.

faculty.name: Robert M. Briber
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure of polymers and soft/biological materials using neutron and X-ray scattering.

faculty.name: Wendell T. Hill III
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Chemical Physics Program; studies strong-field and ultrafast laser-matter interactions, atomic and molecular dynamics, and quantum optics.

faculty.name: David Fushman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses NMR spectroscopy and computation to study protein structure and dynamics, focusing on ubiquitin signaling and the recognition of polyubiquitin chains.

faculty.name: Andrei Vedernikov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops organometallic complexes and catalysts for selective C-H functionalization and oxidation reactions.

faculty.name: Alice C. Mignerey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies nuclear reaction dynamics and heavy-ion collisions to probe the properties and transport of nuclear matter.

faculty.name: Eun-Suk Seo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads balloon- and space-borne experiments such as CREAM/ISS-CREAM to measure high-energy cosmic rays and probe their origin and propagation.

faculty.name: Zhiwei Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads a group using structural chemistry to synthesize and programmably assemble nanoparticles, studying photon-electron and photon-phonon dynamics for photonics, quantum engineering, and energy applications.

faculty.name: Yanxin Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Combines cryo-electron microscopy, cryo-electron tomography, and molecular dynamics simulation to study molecular chaperones such as HSP90 and disease-related protein complexes.

faculty.name: Yu Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses laser cooling and optical/ion trapping to prepare ultracold molecules in defined quantum states for studies of quantum chemistry, coherent control, and quantum computation.

faculty.name: Maria Mukhina
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Combines nanobiotechnology, materials science, and new microscopy techniques to study the mechanics of the genome and how cells generate, transduce, and respond to mechanical forces.

faculty.name: Timothy Koeth
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on nuclear engineering, particle accelerators, and reactor physics, including radiation detection and nuclear materials.

faculty.name: Peter Kofinas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chair of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering who develops functional polymer materials for energy storage, batteries, membranes, and biomedical applications.

faculty.name: Mohamad Al-Sheikhly
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies radiation-induced chemistry and its use in synthesizing and modifying polymers and biomaterials, including free-radical mechanisms and radiation dosimetry.

faculty.name: John Cumings
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses in situ electron microscopy to study nanoscale transport of charge and heat and the mechanical and magnetic behavior of nanomaterials.

faculty.name: Luz J. Martinez-Miranda
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses X-ray scattering to study the structure and ordering of liquid crystals and their interfaces with nanoparticles and biological systems.

faculty.name: Yifei Mo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses first-principles computation and machine learning to design materials for energy storage, including solid-state battery electrolytes.

faculty.name: Oded Rabin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops nanostructured materials for plasmonic sensing, thermoelectrics, and nanoscale energy conversion.

faculty.name: Lourdes Salamanca-Riba
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses transmission electron microscopy to characterize the structure of thin films, nanostructures, and covetic metal-carbon materials.

faculty.name: Mario Dagenais
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops integrated photonic and optoelectronic devices, including semiconductor lasers, waveguides, and solar cells.

faculty.name: Ashwani Gupta
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Studies combustion, high-temperature reacting flows, and energy conversion, including distributed/colorless combustion and waste-to-energy processes.

faculty.name: Panagiotis Dimitrakopoulos
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computational modeling to study the dynamics of cells, capsules, and droplets in flow and other soft-matter and interfacial phenomena.

faculty.name: Ian White
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops optical and paper-based biosensors, including SERS and optofluidic devices, for rapid chemical and biological diagnostics.

faculty.name: James A. Carton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies ocean circulation, ocean reanalysis, and the role of the oceans in climate variability.

faculty.name: Russell Dickerson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Measures trace gases and aerosols to study air pollution, atmospheric chemistry, and their effects on climate and air quality.

faculty.name: Sumant Nigam
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies large-scale climate dynamics, including ocean-atmosphere interactions, droughts, and decadal climate variability.

faculty.name: Ramalingam Chellappa
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Works on computer vision, pattern recognition, and machine learning methods for image and video analysis.

funding.note: TAs and Dean's Fellowships to all entering students with tuition and health insurance

faculty.name: Arpita Upadhyaya
faculty.title: Professor of Physics (Biophysics Program Co-Director)
faculty.summary: Upadhyaya's lab studies how cellular mechanics and cytoskeletal forces let cells sense and respond to their physical environment, focusing on the mechanical regulation of biochemical signaling during immune response, cancer, and development. The group uses advanced and single-molecule microscopy, computational image analysis, and biophysical force measurements.

faculty.name: Jeffery Klauda
faculty.title: Professor of Chemical & Biomolecular Engineering (Biophysics Program Co-Director)
faculty.summary: Klauda's group uses molecular simulations and thermodynamic modeling to describe the function, dynamics, and physical properties of cellular membranes, developing models of lipid-lipid interactions and membranes ranging from yeast and bacteria to human ocular-lens and neuronal membranes. The lab also studies membrane-associated proteins involved in transport, cell death, and signaling.

faculty.name: Wolfgang Losert
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Losert's lab investigates the dynamical properties of complex living systems at the convergence of physics and biology, with a focus on cancer, studying how physical cues such as electric fields and nanotopography guide the motion of individual and collective cells. The group discovered that cell migration can be guided by nanotopography through control of actin-wave dynamics.

faculty.name: Christopher Jarzynski
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor, Chemistry & Biochemistry / Physics / IPST
faculty.summary: Jarzynski develops theoretical and computational tools for understanding nonequilibrium behavior and estimating thermodynamic properties at the molecular scale, including applications to biophysical problems, artificial molecular machines, and the relationship between thermodynamics and information. He is known for the 1997 Jarzynski equality relating nonequilibrium work to equilibrium free-energy differences.

faculty.name: Pratyush Tiwary
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry & Biochemistry / IPST
faculty.summary: Tiwary's group combines equilibrium and nonequilibrium statistical mechanics with deep learning to model the thermodynamics and dynamics of complex molecular systems, including the statistical dynamics of rare events and the dynamics of drug unbinding and protein-kinase plasticity for computational drug design. His RAVE method was among the first to use AI to enhance molecular-dynamics sampling.

faculty.name: Silvina Matysiak
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Matysiak's lab uses statistical thermodynamics and coarse-grained molecular modeling to understand how molecular behavior dictates macroscopic properties of biological systems, studying the self-assembly of soft materials such as surfactants, proteins, lipids, and polysaccharides. A particular focus is on molecular mechanisms relevant to neurodegenerative diseases and certain cancers.

faculty.name: Sergei Sukharev
faculty.title: Professor of Biology / IPST
faculty.summary: Sukharev studies the molecules and principles cells use to detect mechanical force, focusing on mechanosensitive ion channels that convert membrane tension into cellular signals. His lab cloned the bacterial large-conductance mechanosensitive channel MscL and continues to dissect the gating mechanisms of MscL and the smaller MscS using electrophysiology, biochemistry, and biophysics.

faculty.name: David Fushman
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry & Biochemistry
faculty.summary: Fushman studies proteins as dynamic molecular machines at atomic resolution, focusing on structure, dynamics, and recognition in multi-domain proteins and complexes and on ubiquitin-mediated intracellular signaling. His lab combines NMR and relaxation, small-angle X-ray/neutron scattering, and molecular dynamics, and has developed methods to build and determine 3D structures of defined-length ubiquitin chains.

faculty.name: Theodore Kwaku Dayie
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry & Biochemistry
faculty.summary: Dayie studies RNAs as dynamic signaling molecules and drug targets, probing the structural and dynamic basis of molecular recognition in RNA complexes using isotope (13C/15N/2H) labeling, high-resolution multidimensional solution NMR, and SAXS. His group developed chemoenzymatic labeling technologies that yield sharp NMR signals for large viral RNAs of 20-78 kDa.

faculty.name: Jason Kahn
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemistry & Biochemistry
faculty.summary: Kahn's lab studies the analysis, structure, function, and design of protein-DNA complexes on the 50-1000 bp length scale, best known for work on DNA looping, bending, twisting, and cyclization. Projects use bulk and single-molecule FRET to characterize Lac-repressor-anchored DNA loops and the role of DNA structure in transcription and gene regulation.

faculty.name: Yanxin Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Chemistry & Biochemistry / IBBR
faculty.summary: Liu's lab focuses on the molecular and structural understanding of protein complexes related to human disease, with an emphasis on chaperone-mediated protein folding and translocation into membrane-bound organelles. The group integrates high-resolution single-particle cryo-EM, cryo-electron tomography, and large-scale atomistic molecular dynamics simulation.

faculty.name: Giuliano Scarcelli
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Scarcelli directs the Optics Biotech Lab, where he developed confocal Brillouin scattering microscopy, a label-free, non-contact technique that maps the mechanical properties (stiffness, viscosity) of cells and tissues at the nanoscale without disturbing function.
