He applies it to how mechanical properties change during aging, wound healing, disease, and tumor metastasis.

faculty.name: Kimberly Stroka
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Stroka's lab explores the role of mechanical forces in physiological and pathological processes, focusing on mechanobiology of the vascular endothelium, cell-cell interactions at the blood-brain barrier during tumor metastasis, and engineered cellular microenvironments for cell adhesion, migration, and division. The group builds in vitro models using microfabricated devices, cell-mechanics tools, and quantitative image analysis.

faculty.name: Gregg Duncan
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Duncan leads the Respiratory Nano Bioengineering Lab, using nanotechnology and bioengineering to understand the lung airway microenvironment and mucus barrier in health and disease and to develop therapies for asthma, COPD, and cystic fibrosis. His group studies nanoparticle mobility in mucus (including machine-learning-informed prediction) and engineers synthetic mucus biomaterials.

faculty.name: Helim Aranda-Espinoza
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Aranda-Espinoza leads the Cell Biophysics Laboratory, studying how cells respond to external mechanical stresses in healthy and pathological conditions, along with soft matter and the biophysics of membranes and the cytoskeleton. His long-term aim is a fundamental, quantitative understanding of how motile cells adhere, spread, and crawl, focusing on neurons and cells involved in cardiovascular disease.

faculty.name: Peter Nemes
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemistry & Biochemistry
faculty.summary: Nemes develops ultrahigh-resolution, ultrasensitive quantitative mass spectrometry at single-cell and micro-region scales to study the biomolecular mechanisms of development, brain function, aging, and addiction. His lab built single-cell MS instrumentation capable of identifying and quantifying roughly 1,000-2,000 proteins between single embryonic cells and performs subcellular proteomics and metabolomics in vivo.

faculty.name: Brian Pierce
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Cell Biology & Molecular Genetics / IBBR
faculty.summary: Pierce's lab performs computational structural biology focused on modeling and design of immune recognition, including antibodies, T cell receptors, and structure-based vaccine design, using docking and design algorithms such as Rosetta, ZDOCK, and ZAFFI. The group has produced tools including the TCR3D and CoV3D structure databases and the TCRmodel/TCRmodel2 servers for modeling TCR complexes.

faculty.name: Michelle Girvan
faculty.title: Professor of Physics / IPST
faculty.summary: Girvan's research on complex networks sits at the intersection of statistical physics, nonlinear dynamics, and computer science, with biological applications including epidemiology and genetic regulatory networks and recent work at the interface of network science and machine learning. She is a co-namesake of the Girvan-Newman community-detection algorithm.

faculty.name: Joshua Weitz
faculty.title: Professor of Biology (Clark Leadership Chair in Data Analytics)
faculty.summary: Weitz leads the Quantitative Viral Dynamics group, exploring how viruses transform human and environmental health by synthesizing theory, computational simulation, and model-data integration. Research spans quantitative viral ecology, marine viral impacts on the carbon cycle, bacteriophage therapy, microbial ecology and evolution, and infectious disease dynamics.

faculty.name: Catherine K. Kuo
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Kuo directs the Developmental and Regenerative Engineering Laboratory, developing tissue-engineering and therapeutic solutions for tendon injuries, disease, and tendon-related birth defects, informed by mechanisms of embryonic tendon development and scarless healing. Her team studies the mechanobiology of embryonic tendon development to identify regulators of tendon formation for regenerative therapies.

faculty.name: Katharina Maisel
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Maisel leads the MAISEL Lab (Mucosal Associated Immune System Engineering and Lymphatics), integrating materials science, immunology, and mucosal-barrier physiology to design nanoparticles that probe and cross biological barriers such as the lymphatics, interstitial tissue, and mucosal surfaces. The group studies how tissue material properties govern nanoparticle transport to improve vaccines and immunotherapies.

faculty.name: Robert M. Briber
faculty.title: Professor of Materials Science & Engineering
faculty.summary: Briber is an internationally recognized researcher in polymer physics and directs the High Resolution Neutron Scattering Research Program at Maryland. His interests include polymer phase transitions, morphology and structure, the chain conformation of synthetic and biological polymers, and neutron and X-ray scattering characterization of materials.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Vanessa Sih
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applied Physics program director whose group uses optical methods to study spin dynamics and transport in semiconductors.

faculty.name: Scott Baalrud
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Nuclear Engineering and Radiological Sciences who develops kinetic theory of plasmas, including strongly coupled and low-temperature plasmas.

faculty.name: Alexander Burgers
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor of EECS whose research uses arrays of Rydberg atoms for quantum simulation and quantum information.

faculty.name: Roy Clarke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Marcellus L. Wiedenbeck Collegiate Professor of Physics who uses X-ray scattering to study thin-film growth and nanostructured materials.

faculty.name: Parag B. Deotare
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of EECS studying nanophotonics, optomechanics, and exciton transport in two-dimensional materials.

faculty.name: John E. Foster
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Nuclear Engineering and Radiological Sciences working on low-temperature plasmas, plasma-liquid interactions, and plasma-based water treatment.

faculty.name: Rachel S. Goldman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Materials Science, EECS, and Physics whose group studies epitaxial growth and properties of semiconductor thin films and nanostructures.

faculty.name: Carolyn Kuranz
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Nuclear Engineering and Radiological Sciences conducting high-energy-density physics and laboratory astrophysics experiments with high-power lasers.

faculty.name: Cagliyan Kurdak
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Physics studying electronic transport in low-dimensional and nanoscale condensed matter systems.

faculty.name: Angela Violi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Mechanical Engineering using molecular simulation to study nanoparticle formation and interactions of nanomaterials with biological systems.

faculty.name: Steven Cundiff
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Harrison M. Randall Collegiate Professor of Physics who develops ultrafast lasers and multidimensional coherent spectroscopy to study materials and atoms.

faculty.name: Herbert Winful
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Joseph E. and Anne P. Rowe Professor of EECS whose research spans nonlinear fiber optics, laser array dynamics, and the physics of optical tunneling.

faculty.name: Mackillo Kira
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of EECS and Physics who develops many-body and quantum-optics theory to explain experiments in semiconductor and quantum-material physics.

faculty.name: Kai Sun
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Physics working on theoretical condensed matter, including topological phases and strongly correlated electronic systems.

faculty.name: Liuyan Zhao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Physics using nonlinear optical spectroscopy to probe magnetism and symmetry breaking in two-dimensional quantum materials.

faculty.name: Emanuel Gull
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Physics developing quantum Monte Carlo and Green's-function methods to study strongly correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: L. Jay Guo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of EECS whose research covers nanophotonics, nanoimprint fabrication, optical metamaterials, and flexible/transparent electronics.

faculty.name: Stephen R. Forrest
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Peter A. Franken Distinguished University Professor of EECS whose research spans organic optoelectronics, OLEDs, organic solar cells, and photonic integrated circuits.

faculty.name: Jennifer Ogilvie
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Physics and Biophysics who develops multidimensional ultrafast spectroscopy and imaging to study energy transfer in photosynthetic light-harvesting systems.

faculty.name: Louise Willingale
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of EECS and NERS studying intense laser-plasma interactions, including laser-driven ion acceleration and relativistic laser propagation.

faculty.name: Karl Krushelnick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of NERS, EECS, and Physics who directs ultrafast-optics research on high-intensity laser-plasma interactions and laser-driven particle acceleration.

faculty.name: Theodore Norris
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gerard A. Mourou Professor of EECS whose research covers ultrafast optics, terahertz science, and photonic and optoelectronic devices.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Henriette Elvang
faculty.title: Professor (Chair)
faculty.summary: Works on scattering amplitudes, supersymmetry, and quantum field theory approaches to fundamental interactions.

faculty.name: Fred Adams
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the formation and evolution of stars, planets, and galaxies, with focus on early universe cosmology.

faculty.name: Christine Aidala
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the internal spin structure of protons and neutrons through high-energy scattering experiments.

faculty.name: Ratindranath Akhoury
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on quantum field theory, scattering amplitudes, and the theoretical foundations of the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Dante Amidei
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Participates in large collider experiments searching for new particles and fundamental interactions.

faculty.name: Camille Avestruz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses simulations and data analysis to study galaxy clusters, dark matter, and the large-scale structure of the universe.

faculty.name: Myron Campbell
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on high-energy particle collider experiments, including searches for physics beyond the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Timothy Chupp
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Explores fundamental symmetries using precision atomic and nuclear measurements, including searches for permanent electric dipole moments.

faculty.name: Roy Clarke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies novel electronic and structural properties of complex oxide materials and nanostructured systems.

faculty.name: Steven Cundiff
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies ultrafast laser spectroscopy to investigate coherent dynamics in semiconductors and atomic systems.

faculty.name: Robert Deegan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies fluid dynamics, pattern formation, and instabilities in soft matter and granular materials.

faculty.name: Hui Deng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates quantum optical phenomena in semiconductor microcavities, including polariton condensates and quantum fluids of light.

faculty.name: Gus Evrard
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Models the formation of cosmic structure, galaxy clusters, and the interplay between dark matter and baryonic processes.

faculty.name: Emanuel Gull
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods, including quantum Monte Carlo, for strongly correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Scott Haselschwardt
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on dark matter direct detection and novel particle detectors for rare-event searches.

faculty.name: Christian Herwig
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on collider-based searches for new phenomena using advanced data analysis techniques.

faculty.name: Jordan Horowitz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies stochastic thermodynamics, information processing in biological systems, and nonequilibrium statistical mechanics.

faculty.name: Dragan Huterer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on dark energy, weak gravitational lensing, and cosmological parameter inference from large surveys.

faculty.name: Na Hyun Jo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates topological materials, quantum criticality, and correlated electron systems using transport and spectroscopic techniques.

faculty.name: Cagliyan Kurdak
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies mesoscopic physics, quantum transport in semiconductor nanostructures, and two-dimensional electron systems.

faculty.name: Alex Kuzmich
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on quantum information science using cold atoms and photons, including quantum memory and entanglement.

faculty.name: Finn Larsen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches string theory, black hole microstates, and holographic duality.

faculty.name: Lu Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Explores quantum materials including superconductors, topological insulators, and magnetic systems via high-field transport and thermodynamic measurements.

faculty.name: James Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies quantum field theory, supergravity, and the AdS/CFT correspondence.

faculty.name: Wolfgang Lorenzon
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Roberto Merlin

faculty.name: Mark Newman

faculty.name: Leopoldo Pando Zayas

faculty.name: Aaron Pierce
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Sara Pozzi

faculty.name: Jianming Qian

faculty.name: Georg Raithel

faculty.name: Keith Riles

faculty.name: Tom Schwarz

faculty.name: Vanessa Sih
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Joshua Spitz
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Ctirad Uher
faculty.title: C. Wilbur Peters Collegiate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist studying thermoelectric materials, thermal transport, and magnetic properties of solids.

faculty.name: James Wells
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical particle physicist working on physics beyond the Standard Model, Higgs physics, and collider phenomenology.

faculty.name: Dominika Zgid
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Chemistry
faculty.summary: Develops quantum many-body computational methods bridging quantum chemistry and condensed matter for strongly correlated electron systems.

faculty.name: Liuyan Zhao
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Junjie Zhu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist on the ATLAS experiment at the LHC, studying electroweak physics and searching for new phenomena.

faculty.name: Michal Zochowski
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Studies the dynamics of neural networks and collective behavior in biological systems using theoretical and computational approaches.

faculty.name: Timothy A. McKay
faculty.title: Arthur F. Thurnau Professor of Physics, Astronomy, and Education
faculty.summary: Works on observational cosmology and astrophysics using large surveys, and on physics education research and learning analytics.

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Sarah Veatch
faculty.title: Director of Biophysics; Daniel Axelrod Collegiate Professor of Biophysics and Physics
faculty.summary: Studies the organization of cell membranes, using fluorescence and super-resolution imaging to understand how lipid-mediated phase separation and membrane heterogeneity (lipid rafts) contribute to cellular function.

faculty.name: Charles L. Brooks III
faculty.title: Cyrus Levinthal Distinguished University Professor of Chemistry and Biophysics; Warner-Lambert/Parke-Davis Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Develops and applies computational and theoretical methods, including molecular dynamics and the CHARMM software, to study protein folding, conformational dynamics, and biomolecular recognition relevant to drug discovery.

faculty.name: Jens-Christian Meiners
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Investigates the dynamics of single DNA molecules and DNA-protein complexes using optical single-molecule techniques such as laser trapping, focusing on how DNA topology and mechanics affect molecular function.

faculty.name: Qiong Yang
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biophysics and Physics
faculty.summary: Combines microfluidics, single-cell imaging, and nonlinear dynamics theory to study self-organizing biological oscillators such as the cell cycle and developmental segmentation clock in early embryos.

faculty.name: Jordan Horowitz
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biophysics and Complex Systems
faculty.summary: Develops theory of nonequilibrium and stochastic thermodynamics, including thermodynamic uncertainty relations, to understand the energetic costs and tradeoffs of information processing in biological and physical systems.

faculty.name: Michal Zochowski
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Uses theoretical and computational physics to study the dynamics of neural networks and how collective patterns of activity emerge in the brain.

faculty.name: James E. Penner-Hahn
faculty.title: George A. Lindsay Collegiate Professor of Chemistry and Biophysics; Associate Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Uses X-ray absorption spectroscopy and related methods to determine the structure of metal sites in metalloproteins and to understand how metal centers govern biological function.

faculty.name: Sarah Keane
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biophysics and Chemistry; Associate Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Studies the structure and dynamics of non-coding RNAs and protein-RNA complexes, particularly in microRNA biogenesis, using solution NMR spectroscopy and biophysical methods.

faculty.name: Markos Koutmos
faculty.title: Seyhan N. Ege Assistant Professor of Chemistry; Assistant Professor of Biophysics
faculty.summary: Examines how biomolecular structure governs mitochondrial RNA processing and vitamin B12 metabolism, including methionine synthase, aiming to develop therapeutics for diseases of dysfunctional biomolecular maturation.

faculty.name: Anna Maurer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biophysics
faculty.summary: Investigates adeno-associated viral (AAV) vector biology, studying how host cells regulate AAV-delivered DNA and viral capsid assembly to advance gene therapy applications.

faculty.name: Lauren McCormick
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biophysics
faculty.summary: Studies how the microtubule cytoskeleton adapts to the structural and mechanical needs of specialized cells through the tubulin code, combining simulations of microtubule assembly with cell-based work and biophysical assays using recombinant human tubulin.

faculty.name: Jung-Shen Benny Tai
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biophysics
faculty.summary: Studies bacterial biofilms from the perspectives of microbiology, soft matter physics, and ecology, deciphering how single-cell heterogeneities in shape, organization, and gene expression give rise to the collective function of microbial communities.

programs.university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

faculty.name: Justin Jenson

faculty.name: Brian D. Strahl

faculty.name: Joshua D. Strauss

faculty.name: Qi Zhang

faculty.summary: Uses NMR to study protein dynamics and biomolecular interactions.

programs.program_name: Biochemistry, Biophysics and Chemical Biology, PhD

requirements.admission_model: Umbrella graduate group (BGS), independent of departments

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Kim A. Sharp
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics (Chair, BBCB Graduate Group)
faculty.summary: Uses computational approaches including electrostatics modeling, molecular dynamics, and Monte Carlo methods to study how proteins recognize and bind other proteins, drugs, ligands, and nucleic acids, with current work on protein-ligand binding calculations and viral genome packaging.

faculty.name: Nikolaos Sgourakis
faculty.title: Associate Professor (Vice-Chair, BBCB Graduate Group)
faculty.summary: Investigates mechanistic molecular immunology at atomic resolution using NMR spectroscopy and X-ray crystallography combined with computation, addressing peptide antigen presentation, CAR-T cell targeting, and T cell receptor activation.

faculty.name: Ronen Marmorstein
faculty.title: George W. Raiziss Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics
faculty.sub_field: Structural biology of chromatin and metabolism
faculty.summary: Studies protein post- and co-translational acetylation, acetyl-CoA metabolism, epigenetic regulation, and MAPK signaling using X-ray crystallography and cryo-EM, and develops small-molecule inhibitors targeting these mechanisms in cancer and neurodegeneration.

faculty.name: Ben E. Black
faculty.title: Eldridge Reeves Johnson Foundation Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Uses biophysical, biochemical, genetic, and cell biological approaches to understand how protein complexes epigenetically mark centromeres and direct accurate chromosome segregation, with recent work on PARP-1 enzyme mechanisms for targeted therapies.

faculty.name: Gregory D. Van Duyne
faculty.title: Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Investigates how DNA and RNA are maintained and processed by cells, including replication, transcription, DNA repair, and recombination, using X-ray diffraction to build three-dimensional models and test mechanistic hypotheses.

faculty.name: Roberto Dominguez
faculty.title: William Maul Measey Presidential Professor of Physiology
faculty.summary: A structural biologist studying how actin filaments are assembled and disassembled using cryo-EM, X-ray crystallography, biochemical reconstitution, and biophysics, with focus on filament dynamics, branched networks formed by Arp2/3, and thin-filament regulation in muscle.

faculty.name: E. Michael Ostap
faculty.title: Professor of Physiology (Director, Pennsylvania Muscle Institute)
faculty.summary: Studies cytoskeletal motor proteins such as myosin, dynein, and kinesin using biochemistry, single-molecule biophysics, and microscopy to understand how these machines convert chemical energy into mechanical force during muscle contraction and cell migration.

faculty.name: Kenji Murakami
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Uses cryo-electron microscopy and cross-linking mass spectrometry with biochemistry to study the mechanisms of RNA polymerase II transitions between initiation, elongation, and re-initiation, and how transcription is regulated within chromatin.

faculty.name: Vera Moiseenkova-Bell
faculty.title: Professor of Systems Pharmacology and Translational Therapeutics
faculty.summary: Specializes in structural biology and pharmacology of ion channels, particularly TRP channels, using cryo-electron microscopy combined with functional studies and structure-based discovery to understand channel mechanisms and develop therapeutic inhibitors.

faculty.name: Kushol Gupta
faculty.title: Research Associate Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Applies X-ray crystallography and solution-based biophysics including small-angle X-ray and neutron scattering to study enzymes underlying metabolic disorders, retroviral integrases and HIV drug development, and biophysical characterization of mRNA lipid nanoparticles.

faculty.name: Kathy Fange Liu
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Investigates how enzymes regulate gene expression differently in normal physiology versus cancer, studying cancer fusion proteins, ribosome function, and enzyme-mediated RNA modifications to expose cancer vulnerabilities.

faculty.name: James Shorter
faculty.title: Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Studies how the proteostasis network prevents, reverses, or promotes formation of misfolded conformers including prions, amyloids, and toxic oligomers, focusing on protein disaggregases such as Hsp104 and molecular chaperones relevant to Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, and ALS.

faculty.name: Rahul M. Kohli
faculty.title: Professor of Medicine (Infectious Diseases) and Biochemistry & Biophysics
faculty.summary: Studies DNA-modifying enzymes and their role in genomic complexity, focusing on enzymatic deamination, oxidation, and methylation of cytosine and bacterial pathways to antibiotic resistance, using biochemistry, chemical synthesis, and biological assays.

faculty.name: Jeffery G. Saven
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Uses theory and computation to study biomolecules, polymers, and condensed-phase systems, with emphasis on developing computational approaches for understanding and designing proteins and biomolecular materials in collaboration with experimental teams.

faculty.name: Greg Bowman
faculty.title: Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Combines biophysical experiments, machine learning, and large-scale physics-based simulations to map protein structural ensembles and dynamics, aiming to develop therapeutics and interpret genetic variation in diseases such as Alzheimer's and COVID-19.

faculty.name: Yi-Wei Chang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Uses cryo-electron tomography to visualize macromolecular machines directly within intact cells and tissues, studying how molecular structure and organization drive biological function across viral assembly, bacterial machines, and host-pathogen interactions.

faculty.name: Blanton Tolbert
faculty.title: Professor of Biochemistry and Biophysics
faculty.summary: Uses biophysical approaches to understand virus-host interactions, examining structural mechanisms of viral entry, replication, and immune evasion to develop antiviral strategies and therapeutic interventions.

faculty.name: Tobias Baumgart
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Studies the biophysics of biological membranes, focusing on curvature sensing and curvature-generating peripheral membrane proteins such as amphiphysin and endophilin and how protein partitioning between coexisting membrane phases governs signal transduction.

faculty.name: E. James Petersson
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Studies protein post-translational modifications and the enzymes that install them, and develops synthetic protein modification tools including backbone thioamide probes and fluorescent unnatural amino acids for protein folding studies and imaging.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Rhoades
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Uses single-molecule fluorescence and other biophysical techniques to study intrinsically disordered and amyloidogenic proteins such as tau and alpha-synuclein, elucidating how conformational plasticity links to structure-function relationships in neurodegenerative disease.

faculty.name: David Chenoweth
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Grounds research in organic chemistry and molecular recognition to build small-molecule and peptide chemical tools for probing biological systems with spatial and temporal control, including collagen mimetic peptides to interrogate the collagen interactome.

faculty.name: George Burslem
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Cancer Biology and Biochemistry & Biophysics
faculty.summary: Focuses on the chemical biology of lysine post-translational modifications, developing chemical approaches to manipulate protein function and pursuing intracellular protein-editing technologies.

funding.note: Five years of funding as TA, GRA, or Fellow (typical)

faculty.name: Vernita Gordon

faculty.name: Diana Vaman

faculty.name: Kent Yagi

faculty.name: David Nichols

faculty.name: Israel Klich

faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses lipidomics and mass spectrometry to study lipid oxidation and metabolism and their roles in disease, including Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome.

faculty.name: Jesse Zalatan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Engineers signaling pathways and CRISPR-based tools and studies how scaffold proteins and bacterial systems organize and control cellular signaling.

programs.program_name: Nuclear Engineering and Engineering Physics, PhD

funding.note: Unknown/Verify

faculty.name: Paul Wilson
faculty.title: Grainger Professor of Nuclear Engineering (Department Chair)
faculty.summary: Wilson develops computational modeling tools for complex nuclear energy systems, with applications in radiation shielding, nuclear waste management, nonproliferation and energy policy, leading the Computational Nuclear Engineering Research Group (CNERG).

faculty.name: Adrien Couet
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Couet directs the Materials Degradation under Corrosion and Radiation (MaDCoR) lab, studying fuel cladding corrosion, materials for molten salt reactors, and radiation-resistant alloys for fission and fusion using advanced characterization and modeling.

faculty.name: Yongfeng Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Zhang uses microstructure-based modeling to study materials aging and degradation under radiation, high temperature, stress, and corrosion, aiming to uncover degradation mechanisms and design novel materials for extreme environments.

faculty.name: JungHyun Bae
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Bae develops advanced muon tomography systems for monitoring spent nuclear fuel casks, supporting deep geological repository storage, nuclear security, and safeguards through radiation detection technology.

faculty.name: Stephanie Diem
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Diem conducts experimental plasma physics for fusion energy, using radio-frequency waves to heat and drive current in magnetically confined plasmas, with emphasis on electron Bernstein wave experiments on the Pegasus-III device.

faculty.name: Benedikt Geiger
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Geiger investigates heat and particle confinement in high-temperature fusion plasmas on stellarators and tokamaks, leading the Turbulence and Spectroscopy Group and operating advanced diagnostics at major fusion facilities.

faculty.name: Chris Hegna
faculty.title: Harvey D. Spangler Professor
faculty.summary: Hegna works in theoretical plasma physics with emphasis on magnetic confinement, combining analytical and computational methods to study magnetohydrodynamic stability, transport, and instabilities for magnetic fusion energy.

faculty.name: Charles Hirst
faculty.title: Steven J. and Teresa M. Zinkle Nuclear Materials Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Hirst studies nuclear materials for fission and fusion power systems, investigating the interplay between radiation damage, stored energy, temperature and stress to predict material behavior in irradiation environments.

faculty.name: Ben Lindley
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Lindley works on nuclear reactor physics, advanced reactor design, and integrated energy systems, including flexible reactor operation, economics of advanced nuclear power, and computational methods for SMRs and molten salt reactors.

faculty.name: Juliana Pacheco Duarte
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Pacheco Duarte investigates safety analysis, thermal-hydraulics, and risk assessment of advanced nuclear systems, with expertise in experimental two-phase heat transfer, post-critical-heat-flux behavior, and computational thermal-hydraulic analysis.

faculty.name: Sébastien Philippe
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Philippe combines technical and policy analysis on nuclear weapon effects modeling, nuclear security and nonproliferation, arms control verification, and remote sensing to inform nuclear risk understanding and evidence-based policy.

faculty.name: Oliver Schmitz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Schmitz studies plasma physics at the edge of high-temperature fusion plasmas and plasma-wall interaction across tokamaks and stellarators, including 3-D plasma edge transport, helicon wave plasmas, and spectroscopic diagnostics.

faculty.name: Kumar Sridharan
faculty.title: Grainger Professor; Vilas Distinguished Achievement Professor
faculty.summary: Sridharan works on nuclear reactor materials, corrosion, physical metallurgy, and surface modification and coatings, studying irradiation effects, high-temperature performance, and cold-spray deposition for advanced reactor systems.

faculty.name: Adelle Wright
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Wright focuses on fusion energy and stellarators, studying plasma stability and behavior in advanced fusion reactor designs through magnetohydrodynamics, high-performance scientific computing, and nonlinear dynamical systems.

faculty.name: Cary Forest
faculty.title: Prager Professor of Experimental Physics
faculty.summary: Forest is an experimental plasma physicist (cross-listed with NEEP) whose work investigates plasma dynamics, magnetic dynamo, and laboratory astrophysics using devices such as the Big Red Ball at the Wisconsin Plasma Physics Laboratory.

funding.note: All PhD students funded (50% assistantships) for degree duration

faculty.name: Kevin Black
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Particle physics at the energy frontier; works on the CMS experiment at the LHC.

faculty.name: Maxim G. Vavilov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies out-of-equilibrium many-body systems and uses neural networks for characterization and optimization of quantum devices.

faculty.name: Yang Bai
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Particle phenomenology focusing on dark matter, black holes, neutrino physics, collider physics, and the early universe.

faculty.name: A. B. Balantekin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Nuclear Theory / Neutrino Physics / Quantum Computing
faculty.summary: Studies neutrino properties, neutrino cosmology, Big Bang nucleosynthesis, and develops quantum computing algorithms for nuclear and particle physics.

faculty.name: Vernon Barger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Particle physics phenomenology with focus on collider physics and neutrino physics.

faculty.name: Uwe Bergmann
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates nonlinear X-ray phenomena, reveals hidden writings in ancient manuscripts, and images chemical ghosts of fossils.

faculty.name: Stanislav Boldyrev
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies plasma astrophysics including turbulence and magnetic reconnection in space and astrophysical plasmas.

faculty.name: Tulika Bose
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Particle physics at the energy frontier with the CMS experiment; extracts particle physics signatures using machine learning.

faculty.name: Duncan Carlsmith
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: High energy experiment on CMS, LZ dark matter search, and CDF; also works on computation in the physics curriculum.

faculty.name: Daniel Chung
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the interface of cosmology and high energy theory, studying the early universe and fundamental physics.

faculty.name: Kyle Cranmer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Particle physics at the energy frontier with the ATLAS experiment; leads machine learning in the physical sciences and simulation-based inference.

faculty.name: Sridhara Dasu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Particle physics at the energy frontier with the CMS experiment; uses machine learning for particle physics signatures.

faculty.name: Jan Egedal

faculty.name: Mark Eriksson
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Lisa L. Everett
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Cary Forest
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Experimental plasma physics focusing on plasma astrophysics and fusion-related plasma dynamics.

faculty.name: Pupa Gilbert
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Kael Hanson
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Akikazu Hashimoto

faculty.name: Alex Levchenko

requirements.admission_model: Admitted via IGP umbrella, then home program

funding.note: Fully funded (stipend, tuition, health)

faculty.name: Jennifer (Piper) Below
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Vanderbilt Genetics Institute whose lab develops and applies computational methods to understand the genetic and epigenetic basis of human disease, including diabetes, obesity, Alzheimer's disease, cardiovascular disease and metabolic traits.

faculty.name: Nancy Cox
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Quantitative human geneticist and former director of the Division of Genetic Medicine whose lab develops methods for integrating genome variation with genome function and applies them in BioVU, the Vanderbilt biobank.

faculty.name: Alexander Bick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Physician-scientist and Director of the Division of Genetic Medicine focused on identifying the causes and consequences of clonal hematopoiesis and developing new treatments targeting it.

faculty.name: Eric Gamazon
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops and applies genomic and computational methods to investigate the genetic architecture of complex traits, including disease risk and drug response; recipient of an inaugural NIH Genomic Innovator Award.

faculty.name: Melinda Aldrich
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the genetic and non-genetic mechanisms driving disparities in lung cancer risk and survival, and works to address inequities in lung cancer screening for African American and rural populations.

faculty.name: Ruben Martinez Barricarte
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on inborn errors of immunity, a group of monogenic diseases that impair development or function of the immune system; his work has helped discover new genetic disorders causing immunodeficiency.

faculty.name: Ellen Wright Clayton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Ethical, legal and social implications of genomics
faculty.summary: Leader in law and genomics whose research focuses on the ethical, legal, and social implications of conducting and translating genomics research into clinical care; holds appointments in Pediatrics, Health Policy and the Law School.

faculty.name: James Crowe
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs a lab that identifies human monoclonal antibodies against pathogenic viruses including COVID-19, Zika, Ebola, influenza, dengue and RSV; awarded the 2022 Harrington Prize for his antibody work.

faculty.name: Todd Edwards
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Genetic epidemiologist studying the heritable factors that modulate disease risk and developing statistical techniques for designing efficient next-generation sequencing experiments to follow up GWAS discoveries.

faculty.name: Andrew Glazer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Geneticist deciphering which mutations underlie rare Mendelian diseases with a focus on arrhythmia syndromes, using high-throughput in vitro methods and large population cohorts to determine which variants cause disease.

faculty.name: Emily Hodges
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how epigenetic features shape human genomes, focusing on DNA methylation and non-coding gene regulatory variation in evolution, development and disease using genomic and bioinformatic approaches.

faculty.name: Timothy Hohman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how some individuals accumulate Alzheimer's disease neuropathology without clinical symptoms, focusing on identifying molecular drivers of resilience and sex-specific genetic predictors of the disease.

faculty.name: Nikhil Khankari
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Examines the role of nutrition, genetics, and the interaction between these exposures in cancer, with interests spanning cancer, genetic and nutritional epidemiology.

faculty.name: Ela Knapik
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses in vivo zebrafish approaches to model the genetic and developmental processes underlying human disease, with a focus on mechanisms of protein transport and secretion.

faculty.name: Bingshan Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods and computational tools for genetics and genomics and applies them to map disease genes, with integrative approaches to identify genes and cell types implicated in neuropsychiatric and other complex diseases.

faculty.name: Jirong Long
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Molecular and genetic epidemiology of cancer
faculty.summary: Research program focuses on the molecular and genetic epidemiology of various cancers, identifying novel genetic and epigenetic markers and microbiota to understand mechanisms of cancer development.

faculty.name: Janet Markle
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates genetic determinants of immunity through patient-based studies, using whole exome and genome sequencing to discover monogenic inborn errors of immunity in children with rare infectious, autoimmune or auto-inflammatory diseases.

faculty.name: Maggie Chor Yin Ng
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Lab focuses on the genetic architecture of complex traits in African Americans, particularly type 2 diabetes, its complications such as diabetic kidney disease, and contributors such as obesity and insulin resistance.

faculty.name: Dan Roden
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads Vanderbilt's efforts in pharmacogenomics discovery and implementation and serves as PI for the Vanderbilt sites of the Pharmacogenomics Research Network and the eMERGE Network.

faculty.name: Douglas Ruderfer
faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: Ran Tao
faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: Nancy Carrasco
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Department chair whose lab studies the sodium/iodide symporter (NIS) and other ion-coupled membrane transporters, including their structure, transport mechanism, regulation, and roles in thyroid physiology, pathophysiology, and public health.

faculty.name: Roger J. Colbran

faculty.name: David H. Wasserman

faculty.name: Matthew J. Lang

faculty.name: Owen P. McGuinness

faculty.name: David A. Jacobson
